quotes.text: If you can see us, we are dangerous indeed. But that is nothing to the peril you face if you cannot see us, and all you can hear is our laughter.

quotes.speaker: Abrahim Kobal, Techmarine of the Salamanders
quotes.text: Nothing survives its touch, nothing escapes its grasp. It is a weapon of unspeakable power and unmatched devastation – a horror from a bygone age.

quotes.speaker: Acchus Skorrn, Iron Father of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Fury without logic is madness. Logic without fury is impotence. Thus we embody both, making perfect every stroke of the axe and firing of the bolter.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 31

quotes.speaker: Adar Geronan, Imperial Fists Sergeant 4th Company
quotes.text: Raise the flag high. Let those degenerates know who comes to claim their lives this day!

quotes.speaker: Aethon Shaan, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 1st Company
quotes.text: The undefeatable foe is a myth, conjured by weak minds to conceal flagging willpower. Vision. Purpose. Hope. Take these things from him, afraction at a time if you must, and victory will follow as surely as turbulent night yields to cold dawn.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Raven Guard (8th Edition), pg. 12

quotes.speaker: Aevar Qeld, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 5th Company
quotes.text: Give me a confident foe, one who believes in the strength of his warriors and the reach of his own arm. Give me an enemy who will commit swiftly and decisively to his course and forge ahead in the sure knowledge of victory. I will gladly prove his confidence ill-founded, as the ravens’ talons close about his throat and the shadows descend.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Raven Guard (8th Edition), pg. 63

quotes.speaker: Akjai Kor'Varis, Reiver Sergeant of the Raven Guard
quotes.text: So many of our brother Chapters take pride in weathering the fury of the foe. They proclaim their resilience and their endurance under fire. We of the Raven Guard make no such claims. Our enemies are dead long before they have the chance to subject us to such needless punishment.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Raven Guard (8th Edition), pg. 56

quotes.speaker: Alars Lydoro, Captain of the Imperial Fists 4th Company
quotes.text: Let the skies roar and the world tremble! I come to this place with the Emperor’s fury in my soul, and I shall not be denied!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 15

quotes.speaker: Aldrik Voldus, Grand Master of the Grey Knights
quotes.text: Emperor, grant me the humility to remember that I am but your servant, and the strength with which to serve.

quotes.speaker: Alessio Cortez, Captain of the Crimson Fists
quotes.text: I haven't lost an arm, brother. It's right over there.

quotes.speaker: Ancient Kryll, Space Wolves Dreadnought
quotes.text: Yes, my lord, you could order the Long Fangs to engage the Orks from that ridge. That's what we did at Karnax 17, and it cost the lives of three entire packs. I advise a direct, frontal assault by the Dreadnoughts, followed by the second wave of the Great Company's kin. If that doesn't smash these greenskins back to the stinking pits of Octavius then I'll answer to the Allfather in person.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two - Second Edition: War Machines of the Adeptus Astartes, pg. 164

quotes.speaker: Ancient Tahncred
quotes.text: At Lastrati we gathered every Whirlwind in the Crusade and massed them at the gates of Sogoth. At my order, the entire formation opened fire and the night sky blazed as bright as day. Moments later the city was aflame,its defences shattered and its defenders broken. Inly then did Iorder the Assault squads to attack, and then the battle was truly joined.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two - Second Edition: War Machines of the Adeptus Astartes, pg. 82

quotes.speaker: Androcles, Captain of the Star Phantoms
quotes.text: The lives of millions, and the honour of our Chapter, hang in the balance brothers. Failure is not an option.

quotes.speaker: Anonymous
quotes.text: Consider the Predator. Let your soul be armoured with Faith, driven on the tracks of obedience which overcomes all obstacles, and armed with the three great guns of Zeal, Duty and Purity.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 112 (UK), pg. 44(republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 85)

quotes.speaker: Antaro Chronus, Ultramarines Sergeant
quotes.text: Let them hide in their fortress. My crew can use the target practice.

quotes.speaker: Antaro Chronus, Ultramarines Sergeant
quotes.text: The roar of engines, the recoil of cannons. That is where the true joy of battle lies.

quotes.speaker: Apollo Diomedes, Captain of the Blood Ravens
quotes.text: The rabble has deployed heavy weapons. As the Codex demands, flank their positions and avoid their lanes of fire.
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II - Retribution Classics

quotes.speaker: Apothecary Pythol, Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: Death will find you soon enough. War is not about glory. War is about victory!

quotes.speaker: Apothecary Pythol, Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: May the Emperor bless and protect this sacred legacy, so that the strength of these brave warriors may live on in Ultramarines yet unborn, for the abiding honour of our fortress home, Macragge, in the name of the Chapter and the Primarch, to whom we owe all.-while applying his Reductor to fallen Marines

quotes.speaker: Apothecary Pythol, Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: Oh, Proteus. You novices are all alike. And I'm the one who has to put you back together. It isn't combat I resent, Brother. It's the thirst for glory that gets men cut to ribbons.

quotes.speaker: Arch- Scribe Urtos, Memorite of the Book of Days and Leagues
quotes.text: Shoulder-plate to shoulder-plate the Adeptus Astartes stand as mankind's wall against the dark.

quotes.speaker: Archmagos (Novel), Chapter 26
quotes.text: You are scaring the serfs. Calm, brother.
quotes.source: Gathein, Primaris Epistolary of the Avenging Sons Chapter

quotes.speaker: Arenos Karlaen, Captain of the Blood Angels 1st Company
quotes.text: It is a blessed thing, to bear the blood of the Great Angel within one's veins. And yet it is a burden, too. We fight not only against the Emperor's foes, but against the Flaw that seeks to claim our souls. The shadow of death looms over us, but we do not retreat from it. Instead we meet the darkness as did the Primarch, with all the righteous fury of avenging angels.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Blood Angels (10th Edition), pg. 76

quotes.speaker: Arenos Karlaen, Captain of the Blood Angels 1st Company
quotes.text: They fall upon our enemies like Sanguinius’ own fury, angels of vengeance with wings of fire and fists full of killing thunder.

quotes.speaker: Argentius, Commander of the Silver Skulls
quotes.text: Victory does not always rest with the big guns: but, if we rest in front of them we shall be lost.
quotes.source: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 79 Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader,pg. 183 Warhammer 40,000 5th Edition Rulebook,pg. 17

quotes.speaker: Arjac Rockfist of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: As the mountain is ArjacA snow-capped peakHis rage overshadows the wounded bear.The Rockfist endures when all seems lost.

quotes.speaker: Armand Titus, Chaplain- Dreadnought of the Howling Griffons
quotes.text: Here we stand and here shall we die, unbroken and unbowed, though the very hand of death itself come for us, we will spit our defiance to the end!
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Nine - The Badab War - Part One, pg. 124

quotes.speaker: Arn Ironjaw, Wolf Guard
quotes.text: Street by street and block by block we’ll sweep them out. There are snipers on the rooftops and the underpass is heavily mined. It’s going to be dangerous, it’s going to be fun.

quotes.speaker: Artellus Numeon, Captain of the Salamanders Firedrakes
quotes.text: We have all burned. Down in the fire pits, or from the brander’s iron in the solitorium, we have all touched the fire. It leaves scars, even for us. We carry them proudly, with honour. But the scars we took that day on that battlefield, we bear only with shame and regret. They are a memorial in flesh, a physical reminder of everything we have lost, a burn even we fire-born cannot endure without pain.

quotes.speaker: Astorath, High Chaplain of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: Treat them with honour, my Brothers. Not because they will bring us victory this day, but because their fate will one day be ours.

quotes.speaker: Avitus, Blood Ravens Devastator Sergeant
quotes.text: Come you aliens, show me what passes for fury amongst your misbegotten kind!

quotes.speaker: Avitus, Blood Ravens Devastator Sergeant
quotes.text: Don't fool yourselves. War is all we are! Killing is all we are good for! The rest is delusion.

quotes.speaker: Avitus, Blood Ravens Devastator Sergeant
quotes.text: We are the Emperor's angels of death, not his angels of mercy.

quotes.speaker: Avitus, Blood Ravens Devastator Sergeant
quotes.text: We are weapons. There is the Emperor, and there is war. Nothing more.

quotes.speaker: Azrael, Commander of the Dark Angels
quotes.text: Forget your past life. From this day on, you are simply a Dark Angel—nothing else is of consequence. The Chapter is all that matters.

quotes.speaker: Baldus Bael, High Lord of Terra
quotes.text: They are the sons of the Angel, the blooded host, the defenders of Humanity. They are strength. They are nobility. They are the Blood Angels, and I say to you there are no more loyal or determined servants of the Emperor alive today.

quotes.speaker: Balthazar von Heppel, Ministorum Adept
quotes.text: Of all the Primarchs, the end of Leman Russ is the most mysterious, for he is accounted to have vanished ten thousand years ago. One of the most fundamental of the Space Wolves' tenets is the belief that their Primarch will eventually return for the final battle at the end of the universe.

quotes.speaker: Balthazar von Heppel, Ministorum Adept
quotes.text: The Space Wolves have already proven their ability to stave off the most determined attacks on their realm, so overt action is out of the question. However, I believe that it is imperative that they are closely monitored for signs of abomination.

quotes.speaker: Barabel, Praetors of Orpheus Techmarine
quotes.text: Offer the incantation of activation, speak forth the litany of retribution and deliver unto the enemy the wrath of the Omnissiah.

quotes.speaker: Barrgus Ghorrean, of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Feirros is the best of us: disciplined; resolute; loyal. In his centuries of service he has learned every lesson of combat–the data from which we all know. Thus are our wars driven by uncompromising logicand total confidence in victory.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 52

quotes.speaker: Basistus, Lieutenant of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Our First Founding brothers follow the teachings of their Primarchs and in so doing learn to specialise in particular areas of battle. Be it the siegecraft of Dorn’s sons, the swift strikes of the White Scars or the shadow weaving of the Raven Guard, all are powerful if singular ways of war. We of the Ultramarines have no such luxury. Our father teaches of balance in all things, and so must we excel in all areas of strategy to the exclusion of none. This we do, brothers.

quotes.speaker: Battle-cry of the Salamanders
quotes.text: Into the fires of battle!Unto the anvil of war!

quotes.speaker: Belial, Grand Master of the Dark Angels
quotes.text: Fenris breeds heroes like a bar breeds drunks - loud, proud and spoiling for a fight.

quotes.speaker: Bev’tharn, Lexicanium of the Salamanders
quotes.text: Vulkan’s fire lies at the heart of every battle-brother of the Chapter, indeed at the heart of what it means to be a Salamander. I have learned to use my mind to draw upon this fire, to shape it as I shaped my sword on the anvil. And like my sword, the fire at my heart is a weapon in service to the Imperium, a burning brand to sear our enemies or a torch to guide my brothers.

quotes.speaker: Bjorn the Fell-Handed, Venerable Dreadnought of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: God-Emperor? Calling him a god was how all this mess started.

quotes.speaker: Bjorn the Fell-Handed, Venerable Dreadnought of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: I was on Prospero, brothers. I was there when we burned their heresy from the galaxy. I saw Leman Russ lay waste to their cherished places. I saw Traitors weep from corrupted eyes as we turned their pyramids of glass into barren wasteland. That will not happen here. They were made weak by the knowledge of their treachery. We are made strong by the knowledge of our fidelity. Where Tizca fell, the Aett will stand. Though it may cost the lives of us all, the Aett will stand.
quotes.source: Battle of the Fang (Novel), ch. Fourteen, pg. 304

quotes.speaker: Blaan Gihl, Iron Father of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: We must be one brotherhood. Unflagging. Unfaltering. United in purpose. Those who cannot abide by the will of this council must seek their own future, lest they bring ruin unto us all.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 29

quotes.speaker: Blood Ravens Librarian saying
quotes.text: An open mind is like a fortress with its gates unbarred and unguarded.

quotes.speaker: Boreas, Interrogator-Chaplain of the Dark Angels
quotes.text: I ask myself what it means to be one of the Dark Angels. Is it to hunt the Fallen, chasing shadows through the dark places of the galaxy? Is it to pursue our quest at any expense, forgoing all other oaths and duties? Is it to lie, to hide and to plot so that others will never know of our shame? Is it to keep our own brethren unacquainted with the truth of our past, the legacy we all share in? Or ist it to be a Space Marine? Is it to follow the path laid down by the Emperor and Lion El'Johnson at the founding of this great Imperium of man? To protect mankind, to purge the alien, cleanse the unclean?

quotes.speaker: Brother Proteus and Apothecary Pythol, Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: The Emperor protects.-And having a loaded bolter never hurt, either.

quotes.speaker: Brother Proteus, Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: Everything has a purpose. The Emperor ordains it so. You may corrupt the souls of men. But I am steel! I am doom! I march for Macragge, and I know no fear!

quotes.speaker: Cadmus Gracchus, Chaplain of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: Ability is not enough. Dedication is not enough. Even victory is not enough. To become one of our number, you must embody all of these, and yet be more still. What that more is I cannot tell you. Find the answer in your bones, in your heart and soul, and you will be a warrior.

quotes.speaker: Caelicus, Ultramarines Sergeant
quotes.text: The history of our chapter is in this bolter. Twelve times it has passed from one of our fallen Brothers to his successor. May the deeds it performs in your hands live up to theirs.

quotes.speaker: Calistus, Sergeant of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: It should convey some measure of the threat that the Tyranids represent to the Emperor’s realm that their continued onslaught compelled the most Codex-adherent of all Space Marine Chapters to voluntarily alter their sacred doctrines. The fact of that alteration, of course, also speaks volumes of the Ultramarines’ absolute determination to annihilate this rapacious xenos menace.

quotes.speaker: Captain Iacton Qruze of the Luna Wolves
quotes.text: I'll not be known as a Son of Horus again, mark that well, lad.
quotes.source: The Flight of the Eisenstein (Novel), ch. Nine, pg. 229

quotes.speaker: Captain Palatine of the White Consuls
quotes.text: Onwards they came and we were ready. A tide of filth and heresy swept towards us but it could not pierce the adamantine shield of our purity and devotion. Our righteousness flung back the traitors, back into their daemon-haunted fastness and from thence into the void where the Emperor’s justice was served upon their craven souls.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 235 (UK),pg. 45 – Battlefleet Gothic: To Cleanse the Stars

quotes.speaker: Carab Culln,of the Red Scorpions
quotes.text: As I bear the colours of my Chapter boldly, so too do these glorious machines. Their flankes caryy our Livery with pride and the scream of their jets proclaims the coming for all our foes to hear.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two - Second Edition: War Machines of the Adeptus Astartes, pg. 120

quotes.speaker: Carab Culln,of the Red Scorpions
quotes.text: Camouflage is the colour of fear... I have no need to hide from my foes... I have no fear of death. My colours I wear openly, they proclaim louder than any words, "I am proud to live - I am proud to die".
quotes.source: White Dwarf 100 (UK), pg. 5 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 84)

quotes.speaker: Carab Culln,of the Red Scorpions
quotes.text: Corvus Corax, Primarch of the Raven Guard
quotes.source: The Horus Heresy Book Three - Extermination, pg. 282

quotes.speaker: Carab Culln,of the Red Scorpions
quotes.text: If my Marines are ordered to serve alongside these tainted half-animals, they shall do so. But upon their return they shall be quarantined and purified by every means in our Apothecarion.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 110 (UK), pgs. 17 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 52)

quotes.speaker: Carab Culln,of the Red Scorpions
quotes.text: They are an effective force in their way, and I do not doubt the Emperor's wisdom in allowing tainted subhuman filth to serve Him. But my men shall not be exposed to the corruption of these beasts.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 109 (UK), pg. 49 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 150)

quotes.speaker: Carleon, Blood Angels Sergeant
quotes.text: Baryk Carya,Shipmaster of the Eisenstein, Imperial Armada

quotes.speaker: Carleon, Blood Angels Sergeant
quotes.text: Time flows in contradictions since the invasion as an unexpected slowness in the aftermath of an impossible victory. A languid sweetness of air tasted by the hapless victor, and yet I also feel the turning of change.
quotes.source: The Art of Warhammer Video Games,pg. 100, Battlesector

quotes.speaker: Carnak, Chaplain of the Imperial Fists 3rd Company
quotes.text: Everything has a purpose, Proteus. So the Emperor ordains.

quotes.speaker: Catechism of Hate (Novella),ch. One
quotes.text: Catechism of Hate (Novella), ch. Nine

quotes.speaker: Catechism of Hate (Novella),ch. One
quotes.text: We give hope. Ever has it been that the Space Marines are too few to conquer every threat. Yet the truth of our existence and the hope of our intervention steels the hearts of lesser warriors and lends strength to their conviction. They hold out against impossible odds and offer up prayers to the Emperor that the Angels of Death will come. They fight harder, knowing that if they do so we might intervene. Mankind believes our Emperor to be a god, and that is both foolish and blind. Yet they also believe the Adeptus Astartes to be the instrument of His will, and that is not so ignorant. I cannot say whether the Emperor answers prayers thrown up in desperation, but I do know that the Ultramarines reply to calls for aid if it is possible. To not answer that cry for help threatens to shatter something far more precious than ore worlds and hive factories: faith in the Space Marines.

quotes.speaker: Cato Sicarius, Captain of the Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: Brothers! War calls you. Will you answer?

quotes.speaker: Cato Sicarius, Captain of the Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: Slay the beast and the horde will die.

quotes.speaker: Cato Sicarius, Captain of the Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: We are the slayers of kings, the destroyers of worlds, bringers of ruination and death in all its forms. These things we do in the name of the Emperor and in the defense of Mankind. Let none stay our wrath.

quotes.speaker: Chaplain Fergas Nils
quotes.text: As our bodies are armoured with Adamantium, our souls are protected with our loyalty. As our bolters are charged with death for the Emperor's enemies, our thoughts are charged with his wisdom. As our ranks advance, so does our devotion, for are we not Marines? Are we not the chosen of the Emperor, his loyal servants unto death?
quotes.source: White Dwarf 108 (UK), pg. 45 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 21) Warhammer 40,000 5th Edition Rulebook, pg. 20

quotes.speaker: Chaplain Mikelus of the Blood Ravens
quotes.text: Faith is purest when it is unquestioning.

quotes.speaker: Chaplain Mikelus of the Blood Ravens
quotes.text: Pain now! Reward in the afterlife.

quotes.speaker: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 63
quotes.text: The Imperium is nothing less than the greatest fortress in Human history. Every world is a bastion, a redoubt made mighty by faith and toil. We are the defenders of this fortress, the praetorians who stand watch upon the walls, and who sally out to shatter each new threat as it masses before the Imperium’s gates. While we stand our watch, this realm shall never fall.
quotes.source: Vladimir Pugh, Chapter Master of the Imperial Fists(deceased)

quotes.speaker: Codex: Necrons (3rd Edition), pg. 2
quotes.text: The Machine is strong. We must purge the weak, hated flesh and replace it with the blessed purity of metal. Only through permanance can we truly triumph, only through the Machine can we find vistory. Punish the flesh. Iron in mind and body. Hail the Machine!
quotes.source: Paullian Blantar, Iron-Father of the Kaargul Clan Iron Hands Chapter

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 29
quotes.text: Let the Orks come by the thousand, or by the tens of thousands; we shall be ready for them. This world is the Emperor's, not theirs, and we shall wash it clean with their blood.

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 29
quotes.text: We have been wounded sorely. Yet still we stand, with fire in our eyes and valour in our hearts. Let them think us beaten. We shall teach them otherwise.
quotes.source: Pedro Kantor, Chapter Master of the Crimson Fists

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 52
quotes.text: My sword is at the Emperor's command. I answer to no other man, living or dead.
quotes.source: Tu'shan, Chapter Master of the Salamanders

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 52
quotes.text: What use is an untempered blade? A false and faithless companion. A weapon whose veneer of embellishment cannot hide the weakness within. Such arms are the mark of traitors and oath breakers– those for whom haste and inconstancy are virtues. The tempered blade does not shatter. It endures and it slays.

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 74
quotes.text: Victory often rests on the correct weaponry being in the right place and at the right time. Even the most destructive weapons of war are worthless if absent from the battle.
quotes.source: Artor Amhrad, Chapter Master of the Astral Knights(deceased)

quotes.speaker: Codex: Space Marines (5th Edition), pg. 77
quotes.text: I believe in three tenets of battle - firepower, firepower, and more firepower. Should the Omnissiah have wished otherwise, he would not have provided me with such a mighty tool as the Razorback. I long for the day in which my entire Chapter can ride to war in transports such as these, vehicles whose rage can be given voice through their sanctified armament.
quotes.source: Kardan Stronos, Chapter Master of the Iron Hands

quotes.speaker: Confessor Belithael
quotes.text: Mercy is a word for traitors and cowards...Let all who speak it be strung up by their duplicitous tongues.
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000 8th Edition Rulebook, pg. 201 Pariah Nexus (Animated Series), ep. 2

quotes.speaker: Corphal, Veteran Sergeant of the Blood Angels 1st Company
quotes.text: I have fought on worlds wreathed in perpetual darkness, where unclean monsters crawled from the slime in their millions. I have fought across blazing sky-cities as they fell from the heavens, and battled heretic mind-witches in the depths of their twisted sanctums. I have locked blades with Ork warriors so strong they could crush ceramite with their fists, duelled traitors whose blades moved faster than thought, and slain howling abominations the size of tanks. Through all of it, I have learned to hold one truth above all others– our strength flows from the Primarch, and he who venerates Sanguinius above all else will never be defeated.

quotes.speaker: Crastor, Ultramarines Sergeant
quotes.text: Ultramarines - A Warhammer 40,000 Movie

quotes.speaker: Credo of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: So it was in the beginning; so ever it shall be.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 105 (UK), pg. 19(republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 129)

quotes.speaker: D'Kestrel, Raven Guard Sergeant 1st Company
quotes.text: We go where we wilt. We slay who we wilt. Let the Emperor judge the righteousness of our deeds.

quotes.speaker: Dac'tyr, Captain of the Salamanders 4th Company
quotes.text: Strategy Cards for Planetary Empires expansion game, card Energy Field - Minor Stratagem", (saved archive page, dated February 2011, last accessed 6 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Dain Fellerus, Vanguard of Kill Team Ebony Gladius
quotes.text: The Great Rift promised us doom, and by the Emperor, it delivered. Some said the planet was lost as soon as the heretic fleet appeared in the sky. But they are weak-spirited fools, and deserve death.

quotes.speaker: Dante, Commander of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: Bountiful fate has presented us with the means by which we might repair those wounds upon our souls caused by the weakness of centuries past. It is not only our duty but our right to face the darkness again; to stare into the abyss of the unknown. This time we shall be the cleansing light in the shadow, the burning beacon of righteous war. Restore the honour of the shamed fallen through the death of your foes!
quotes.source: White Dwarf September 2016, Space Hulk - Ductways

quotes.speaker: Dante, Commander of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: For eleven hundred years, I have fought and I have seen the darkness in our galaxy. I have seen the vileness of the alien and the heresy of the mutant. I have witnessed the sin of possession. I have seen all the evil that the galaxy harbours, and I have slain all whose presence defiles the Emperor. I have seen what you will see. I have fought what you must fight, and I have slain what you must slay... so fear not and be proud, for we are the sons of Sanguinius, the protectors of Mankind. Aye, we are indeed the Angels of Death.

quotes.speaker: Darnath Lysander, Captain of the Imperial Fists 1st Company
quotes.text: Brothers! It falls to us to rebuild that which was broken. To remake the glorious Bastion Imperialis that was the Primarch’s dream and duty. The flesh and bone of Mankind itself will be the rampart; the blood of traitors and heretics the mortar that binds the fastness as one. We will bring light to the dark of the Imperium Nihilus, and glory to the Emperor!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 6

quotes.speaker: Darnath Lysander, Captain of the Imperial Fists 1st Company
quotes.text: Gaze upon the deeds of old, brothers, but remember always that the triumphs of history are naught but ash unless reconsecrated by the blood and valour of the present, in every battle of every war. Our ancestors stood witness at the birth of the Emperor’s dream. Our inheritors will see that dream complete!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 12

quotes.speaker: Darnath Lysander, Captain of the Imperial Fists 1st Company
quotes.text: I have spoken to many who proclaim this an age of darkness, saying that only pride sustains us in its grasp. Perhaps that is so, but I will see the Emperor’s Light shine again, even if I must topple every fortress, crush every traitorous warlord, and grind ten thousand worlds to dust. These words I speak not out of pride, but out of duty. Duty to Primarch, to Emperor, and to Mankind itself.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 34

quotes.speaker: Darnath Lysander, Captain of the Imperial Fists 1st Company
quotes.text: I have travelled far and seen much. Yet nothing warms my heart so much as the sight of a gun so massive that its fury makes the very world tremble.

quotes.speaker: Darnath Lysander, Captain of the Imperial Fists 1st Company
quotes.text: My kind were created to feel no fear, but we understand it. We were all once men who felt fear as does anyone else, and we must know it because it is a weapon we wield.

quotes.speaker: Deacon Euphrati Kyne
quotes.text: Angels, they call them, and magnificent they are indeed when they crush our profane foes... but what angel does not believe that our hallowed Emperor is God?

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 68
quotes.text: Yours is to serve, unto the last drop of your blood, that your brothers might live to fight on. Watch over them, heal them, offer up your very life should it extend theirs but a moment, and by your sacrifice the enemy be slain. And when the time comes that you must fulfil your most solemn oath, speak clearly, speak proudly, speed the bolt that brings his end and send him joyous to the Emperor’s side. Then do your duty, take that which is due, and know your mission is done.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse V, Chapter L (M40 Redaction)

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 72
quotes.text: Look not behind you, brother. Know in your heart that your kin are there, offering their fire and their prayers. Look instead to your foe as you scream from the skies upon jets of white fire. Take but one instant to savour the fear in his eyes as he looks skywards, and there, in the keen edge of your blade, he sees his doom, delivered by your hand.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse I, Chapter VI

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 76
quotes.text: Look to your fore—there you shall see your foe. Blast him! With bolt, flame, and plasma, lay waste to his body and shrive his very soul. Do this so that your brothers may close upon their target, do it because their very lives depend upon your deeds, do it because they are your kin, and you are theirs, and you can never fail.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse I, Chapter V

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 80
quotes.text: Your fate is to know that which may not be known, to look into those places of darkness and desolation and know that horrors look back upon you. Speak not to your brothers of such things, but use the gifts you have been given, by the Emperor himself, to blast and sear and rend and banish all those who would deny us our destiny.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse XXI, Chapter LXI (Appendix cii)

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 84
quotes.text: No place shall be denied to them, no enemy shall bar their way. They shall be proud of heart and stoic of spirit. They shall be measured and wise, yet their hatred shall burn with such heat that their enemies wither and die before them. They shall be masters of every weapon, every ploy, and every strategy, fierce in the attack and solid in the defence. They shall go where others fear to tread, and return victorious, that we all might live but one more day.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse I, Chapter III

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 88
quotes.text: Yours is to heed the machine as others heed their kin. Tend to the war spirits all about, but do so in the knowledge that you do the Emperor’s duty. Without your ministrations, no bolt may be fired, and no enemy slain.
quotes.source: Apocrypha of Eons, Verse III, Chapter CIV

quotes.speaker: Deathwatch Core Rulebook,pg. 180
quotes.text: As the chainsword rends flesh, so will you rend souls. As the boltgun tears bodies, so will you tear minds. The power of the Immaterium is your weapon, and you will use it well.
quotes.source: Cadras, Epistolary of the White Consuls Chapter

quotes.speaker: Dek’Naru, Reiver of the Salamanders
quotes.text: If our appearance sows fear, then let us reap a harvest, brothers. If the enemy quails before our ashen visages, then let it be a foreshadowing of their blackened corpses. And if they tremble at the embers of our eyes, let their terror overwhelm them as we strike, our combat knives slicing through their kin like drake teeth. Our distance to Humanity is no burden, but a tool, crafted for duty.

quotes.speaker: Demetrian Titus, Captain of the Ultramarines 2nd Company
quotes.text: The Codex Astartes is a set of rule. They guide us, shape us as Ultramarines, teach us to place duty and honour above all. But how we live with those rules is the true test of a Space Marine. And you have failed.
quotes.source: The Art of Warhammer Video Games, pg. 136, Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine

quotes.speaker: Denallus, Veteran Sergeant of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: The Imperium faces encroaching darkness upon every side. More than ever, the might of the Ultramarines is needed to drive the enemy back and safeguard the Emperor’s realm. We shall not shirk from that duty, no matter what it may cost us.

quotes.speaker: Develain, Reclusiarch of the Black Templars
quotes.text: Let the aspirant's first lesson be obedience, for foolishness is bound to his heart. But the Chapter's discipline and the will of the Chaplain shall drive it from him.

quotes.speaker: Diomedon of Luna, A Study of the Reconstruction
quotes.text: When we talk of those times, we use terms that we recognise: Space Marines, Custodians, High Lords. It is natural, therefore, to assume that the nature of them is common between the two ages. It is not. All institutions were changed by that war. The Space Marines we venerate now, and rarely see, resemble their earlier brothers in form and function, but do not be fooled – they are new creations. The weapons and armour are much the same, as are some formations and tactical divisions, but crucial elements have undergone lasting transformation. Their minds are not what they were. Their dispositions are not what they were. Their imaginations, which beforehand were much as free as ours, have been curtailed. Their judgement on matters outside war has been limited. They are now both less and greater than the baseline human, a weapon forged solely against corruption. It was not always so, and yet history demanded the change. Too much blood had been shed, and too many had turned against their creator, for the old freedoms to be permitted. It happened quickly, this change, making use of protocols already in development within several separate institutions on Terra. There was no gradual progression, no slow evolution of the new type. Even as the last echoes of the Traitor’s demise ebbed through the wounded galaxy, the troops destined to replace their forefathers were being schooled and shaped into their new nature. Those who reconquered the galaxy would not, despite superficial similarities, be the ones who had first won it for humanity.

quotes.speaker: Donatos Aphael, Captain of the Blood Angels 2nd Company
quotes.text: Barricades, is it? And reserves doubtless lurking in the ruins behind, poised to ambush. Send in the Repulsors, and leave nothing but rubble and dust.

quotes.speaker: Drusani, Sergeant of the Blood Angels 1st Company
quotes.text: No angel ever descended from the heavens with as much sound and fury as do we. Let the Drop Pods’ roar herald our coming–the brief warning our foe hears shall avail them naught.

quotes.speaker: Dvan Skor, Veteran Scout Sergeant of the Storm Wardens
quotes.text: Give me the Scout as a boy, and I'll give you a Battle-Brother as a man.

quotes.speaker: Eddan Bourne, Captain of the Silver Skulls No. 2 Assault Company
quotes.text: Show me a fortress and I'll show you a ruin.

quotes.speaker: Erasmus Tycho, Captain of the Blood Angels 3rd Company(deceased)
quotes.text: No matter how many Imperial lives our actions save, how many worlds we rescue from the clutches of monsters and how much selfless glory we cover ourselves in from one war zone to the next, none of it changes the bleak truth that we are cursed. And when the black tide rises, and drowns all that we are, does the legacy we leave behind excuse the monsters we become? Does it make our sacrifice worthwhile, as we are told? Or is it all swept away, rendered meaningless by the same bloody flood that takes our sanity? I just do not know...

quotes.speaker: Euphrati Keeler, Remembrancer
quotes.text: Why do you weep? Is it for us?-For him, Nathaniel, because he can't. Today you and I have broken a brother's heart, and nothing will ever mend it.
quotes.source: The Flight of the Eisenstein (Novel), ch. Fourteen, pg. 341

quotes.speaker: Extract from the Pax Terminatum
quotes.text: Within dark and forgotten places hide the enemies of the Emperor. You have been chosen to enter such places and, protected by the best armour the Adeptus Mechanicus can provide, cleanse it. Take with you weapons, a valiant heart and the Emperor's blessing, and engage the enemy where it makes its lair. Acknowledge death as it approaches, but do not succumb to its touch, for your purpose is great. You have proved yourselves to be worthy of the status you now hold. Everyone of you standing here, all of whom have declared allegiance to the Emperor and take his will as your guide, have shown your courage and have been rewarded for it. Those that stand before me, I charge you now, go forth and vanquish the foe.

quotes.speaker: Ferdina, Captain and Lord of the Marches of the Tigers Argent
quotes.text: We face greenskin invasion rimward, Eldar raiding coreward, rebellion trailing and the tendrils of a Hive Fleet incoming spinward. Situation excellent, attacking on all fronts...

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: A warrior’s heart should be as iron: Unforgiving, unyielding, and uncaring of all except duty. With such a heart, a warrior can stand against the terrors of the universe unbowed and unbroken.

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Make war and move on, and again, and again, until nothing breathes which stands against us. All else is sophistry and pretty lies.
quotes.source: The Horus Heresy Book Two - Massacre, pg. 68

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Say what you like about the Fourteenth Legion. They are mean, ugly bastards but tenacious. There’s no one else I’d rather have by my side in a war of attrition, and almost anyone else I’d rather have against me.

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: The molten heart of Medusa burns in my breast and sometimes it rises in my gullet before I can think.

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: The weak must be expunged in order for Humanity to survive. Only the strong can be trusted, my sons. Our will must be as steel, our resolve as adamantium; it cannot yield even for a moment. We few have been entrusted with a sacred duty to ensure the Emperor’s reign is eternal. So shall it be, whatever the cost.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 6

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: They are not my hands.This fact is forgotten by my brothers - inexplicably, it has always seemed to me. The hands are strong, to be sure, and have created great things for us all, but they are not mine. And that counts for something.They forget that the silver on my arms comes from a beast that I vanquished. It is the mark of a great evil that I ended, and yet it persists within me... I would struggle to remove it now... I will not remove the silver from my flesh because I have learned to depend on it. The fault is with my mind. I rely on the augmentation given to me by my metal gauntlets, so much so that the flesh beneath them is now little more than a distant memory... A day will come when I will strip it from me, lest I lose the power to master myself forever. Already my Legion's warriors replace their shield hands with metal in my honour, and so they too are learning to doubt the natural strength of their bodies. They must be weaned off this practice before it becomes a mania for them. Hatred of what is natural, of what is human, is the first and greatest of the corruptions. So I record it here: when the time comes, I will strip my hands of their unnatural silver. I will instruct my Legion to recant their distrust of the flesh. I will turn then away from the gifts of the machine and bid them relearn the mysteries of flesh, bone and blood. When my father's Crusade is over, this shall be my sacred task. When the fighting is done, I shall cure my Legion and myself. For if fighting is all there is, if we may never pause to reflect on what such devotion to strength is doing to us, then our compulsion will only grow. Already I see the madness that path leads to, and so I shall excise the silver from my hands. In doing so I shall weaken myself and my sons, but nonetheless it must be done. The hands are strong, and have created great things, but they are not mine.

quotes.speaker: Ferrus Manus, Primarch of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: We cannot do this alone. We have struggled for months to accomplish this task on our own when it should have been clear that we could not. In all things we strive to eradicate weakness, but it is not weakness to ask for help, my brothers. It is weakness to deny that help is needed. To fight on without hope when there are those who would gladly lend a hand is foolish, and I have been as blind as any to this, but no more.

quotes.speaker: Flesh Tearers Battle-Brother, Maion
quotes.text: I am His vengeance as He is my shield. I will deliver death to His enemies as He brings deliverance to my soul.

quotes.speaker: Franz Grenzstein, Librarian of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: Don’t confuse gratitude for a serendipitous event with any cloying compassion towards the author of that event...

quotes.speaker: Franz Grenzstein, Librarian of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: The Chaos Power known as Tzeentch conceives plans to alter history schemes which are altogether too devious and far-flung for any human being to hope to comprehend.

quotes.speaker: Franz Grenzstein, Librarian of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: True justice is quite simply the will of the Emperor.

quotes.speaker: Franz Grenzstein, Librarian of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: We of the Librarium must be aware of Chaos. Yet we do not ourselves aim to contend with Chaos, unless compelled to.
quotes.source: Space Marine (Novel), ch. Fifteen Heretic Tomes

quotes.speaker: Furio, Brother-Captain of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: Only when your hearts have beat to the same pulse of clashing weapons as your enemy’s, only when his blood anoints you, and you glimpse the blackness waiting for us all in his dying eyes—only then have you known battle.

quotes.speaker: G’narr, Apothecary of the Salamanders
quotes.text: The Imperium is a vast domain. There may yet be Chapters, having fought in darkness for millennia to protect pockets of Mankind, in whose veins runs Vulkan’s vital strength. Who knows, too, if amongst the lauded Ultima Founding we have kin? Though they are not Nocturne-bred, my hearts lift at the thought of distant foes brought low by resolute warriors of noble mien.

quotes.speaker: Gabrian, Veteran Sergeant Blood Angels 6th Company
quotes.text: Erikon Gaius, Captain of the Ultramarines 21st Company
quotes.source: Garro: Oath of Moment (Audio Book), Track 4: In The Tunnel

quotes.speaker: Gabrian, Veteran Sergeant Blood Angels 6th Company
quotes.text: Kinsmen, we are being tested. We cannot know what madness has overcome Lorgar, we do not know the fate of our brothers and our primarch. But what we do know is duty. Our duty is to hold this approach to the city, to deny it to the enemy. These were Guilliman's last words to us. Meles gave his life in service of that command, and if called, then so shall we.
quotes.source: Garro: Oath of Moment (Audio Book), Track 4: In The Tunnel

quotes.speaker: Gabrian, Veteran Sergeant Blood Angels 6th Company
quotes.text: Strive, my brothers, to cage the beast within, for its crimson thirst shall make monsters of us all. Yet know that, should ever the beast break free, the shame of that moment can only be salved through the unmitigated slaughter of the foe.

quotes.speaker: Galetus Tyvus, Codicier of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: The more potent a weapon is, the more dangerous and complex a process is its wielding. The more energy a blade or gun unleashes, the more damage it can inflict, the more perilous it would prove to an unwary wielder. How deadly, then, is the infinite power of the warp when focused through the mind of a trained Librarian, and how dolorous a duty is ours in ensuring that this source of untrammelled destruction does not run wild to the damnation of all.

quotes.speaker: Garnon Daar, Iron Father of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: There can only ever be one answer to weakness, and that is the mercy of death. This, and this alone, is the only such mercy in our gift.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 31

quotes.speaker: Garviel Loken, Luna Wolves Captain
quotes.text: Brother... kinsman... every time the death comes closer, but still I am rejected. Do you know why? Will you not tell me? Death took you! Why not me? WHY NOT ME?!
quotes.source: Garro: Legion of One (Audio Book), Track: Cerberus Alone

quotes.speaker: Gerhart, Black Templars
quotes.text: Yes, I have a damn question... what wars must there be in creation that require warriors like you?

quotes.speaker: Gerlach, Black Templars Dreadnought
quotes.text: The Emperor's realm must be cleansed of the taint of the xenos and the heretic. We destroy the former by the sword, we shall destroy the latter through faith and the sword.

quotes.speaker: Ghol’kwe, Veteran Sergeant of the Salamanders
quotes.text: The weapon you carry today is as much apart of your squad, your company, as the warriors who stand shoulder to shoulder with you in the crucible of battle. Honour its victories, maintain its warrior spirit, dedicate oaths of brotherhood to its name. The weapon you carry today has been forged in fire as you have. It has been crafted with consummate skill, has endured the anvil of war and has thrown down the Emperor’s enemies as you have. The weapon you carry today is feared by our foes, for it is a harbinger of death, as you are

quotes.speaker: Gideon, Black Templars Marshal, Mephistari Crusade
quotes.text: Those who are pure of heart and strong of faith have nothing to fear from the Black Templars, this is true. But who then, amongst the heaving, sinful masses of Humanity, can truly count themselves as safe from our wrath?

quotes.speaker: Golgaar, Sergeant of Iron Hands Clan Company Raukaan
quotes.text: Divest yourself of mercy, for mercy is a weakness. Divest yourself of compassion. for compassion is a weakness. Divest yourself of hope, for hope is a weakness. When nothing remains within but hate, and the purity of your purpose, then you will be strong.

quotes.speaker: Gonzago, Chaplain
quotes.text: Bravery is stronger than Adamantium. One is the stuff of the soul-forges, the other an illusion of safety created by fools. Adamantium walls cannot keep us out! We have the courage of the Emperor! And in the Emperor's name... ATTACK!! ATTACK!! NO PRISONERS!!
quotes.source: White Dwarf 108 (UK), pgs. 48(republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 22) Warhammer Siege, pg. 109

quotes.speaker: Grimaldus, Chaplain of the Black Templars, hero of Hive Helsreach
quotes.text: Let a wave of repugnance for the enemy wash over you. Let hatred fill you. Hate is good, for our goal is a Human galaxy. We are called by the Emperor with a sacred duty to conquer it in His name.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 311 (US), pg. 25,Assault on Cephian IV

quotes.speaker: Grimaldus, Chaplain of the Black Templars, hero of Hive Helsreach
quotes.text: To the darkness, I bring fire. To the ignorant, I bring faith. Those who welcome these gifts may live, but I will visit naught but death and eternal damnation on those who refuse them.

quotes.speaker: Gryvuus Krohl, Librarian of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: To commune with the spirit of the machine as we do is to see its soul unlike any other can. Every Predator, Vindicator and Impulsor is a battle-brother, as eager to vanquish the foe as we.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 62

quotes.speaker: Gyvan Hul, Seneschal of the Rogue Trader vessel Frozen Talon
quotes.text: A Space Marine is not born, but made by the good grace of the Emperor from flesh, blood, and a will to do the Master of Mankind’s bidding.

quotes.speaker: Haakon Draugrsbane, Captain of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: This foul rock has haunted our space for far too long, and by my hammer Doombringer we will shatter it and empty the vermin inside once and for all! For Russ! For the Eternal Vigil!

quotes.speaker: Hassar, Cadian 7th Colonel
quotes.text: Fenrisians are taught to value comradeship and loyalty to their tribes from birth. Within the Space Wolves, their loyalty is given to their Wolf Lord and the Company of his followers. As warrior-barbarians they set great store by personal bravery and great deeds, having little but contempt for authority that is bestowed rather than earned through the proven virtues of combat.

quotes.speaker: Hauis Argento, Chaplain of the Crimson Fists 2nd Company
quotes.text: If your life is given in service to the Emperor, your death shall not be in vain.

quotes.speaker: Helbrecht, High Marshal of the Black Templars
quotes.text: Where the Neophyte sees only the difficulties and the Initiate only the advantages in any great enterprise, the hero sees both.

quotes.speaker: Heledorn, Imperial Fists Brother-Captain
quotes.text: Stand fast brothers! We are the Emperor’s Fist, closed and ready to strike!

quotes.speaker: Helveticus, Ancient of the Ultramarines Honour Guard
quotes.text: A foe without honour is a foe already beaten.

quotes.speaker: Holt, Commissar
quotes.text: Space Marines are men in the same way a knife is a piece of metal. Space Marines are forged, not born. They are the finest weapons of the Emperor’s will.

quotes.speaker: Hyperion, Grey Knights
quotes.text: The Wolves aren't animals. No beast is bound by honour, nor does it sacrifice itself for others outside its pack.
quotes.source: The Emperor's Gift (Novel), ch. Twenty-Four, pg. 284

quotes.speaker: Imperial Armour Volume Ten - The Badab War - Part Two, pg. 84
quotes.text: You may cleave to your courage but that will fail you, look to your weapons but you will find them not enough. Run, fight, hide, pray, cry out or cover - it does not matter, for we are come.
quotes.source: Fafnir Rann, Captain of the Imperial Fists Legion and founder of the Executioners Space Marine Chapter

quotes.speaker: Imperial Maxim
quotes.text: Ritual and Responses, Tome of Truths Foretold, Legiones Astartes, Library Copy
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000 Chapter Approved - The Book of the Astronomican, pg. 71

quotes.speaker: Imperial Maxim
quotes.text: The enemies of the Emperor fear many things. They fear discovery, defeat, despair, and death. Yet there is one thing they fear above all others. They fear the wrath of the Space Marines!

quotes.speaker: Initiate Rammius of the Black Templars
quotes.text: It is the duty of the Initiate to pass on what he has learned of the craft of death and thus pave the way for the heroes of the future.

quotes.speaker: Iron Thane Mazos Dhule of the Sons of Medusa
quotes.text: I trust these automata-weapons far more than any mortal ally. Their machine spirits have served our Chapter faithfully for centuries, and are sure to stand where mortal hearts succumb to fear. Just like us, they know no fear.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two - Second Edition, pg. 198

quotes.speaker: Isador Akios, Librarian of the Blood Ravens
quotes.text: Blessed is the mind too small for doubt.

quotes.speaker: Isador Akios, Librarian of the Blood Ravens
quotes.text: Through the destruction of our enemies do we earn our salvation!

quotes.speaker: Jaghatai Khan, Primarch of the White Scars
quotes.text: Do not value strength and power too much, for they alone are no guarantee of victory. Value speed and accuracy first: If you do this, then power and strength will do naught but snap at your heels.

quotes.speaker: Jaghatai Khan, Primarch of the White Scars
quotes.text: My sons. More than seventy years since we cast the traitors from walls of the Imperial Palace and began the restoration of our home. We stand on the brink of victory, one that your every sacrifice has counted towards. Today we cleanse the Galaxy of those who crossed our boundaries, violated our home and took our people. They will be crushed beneath our treads, disembowelled by our blades and left shattered by our guns. I will have their heads on spikes, lining the roads of Chogoris. I will see their foul structures torn to the ground and turned to dust. Of their leader... On that monster I shall inflict a hundredfold the pain it has caused my people. MY sons, you shall each do the same.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: White Scars (8th Edition), pg. 27

quotes.speaker: Jaghatai Khan, Primarch of the White Scars
quotes.text: Warriors of Chogoris! Brothers of the Great Tribe! The star hunt calls you, do you not hear it? The battle’s red edge is your home, the respect of your kinsmen your hearth. Plunge into the enemy’s breast like a blade, cut out his heart, and you will know fulfillment. The Emperor has given us strength. In return we give him victory!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: White Scars (8th Edition), pg. 6

quotes.speaker: Jubal Khan, Great Khan of the White Scars
quotes.text: Barutai Khan, Khan of the White Scars 2nd Company
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: White Scars (8th Edition), pg. 56

quotes.speaker: Jubal Khan, Great Khan of the White Scars
quotes.text: Kyublai Khan, Great Khan of the White Scars(Missing)
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: White Scars (8th Edition), pg. 62

quotes.speaker: Jubal Khan, Great Khan of the White Scars
quotes.text: Long before, when we roamed the Empty Quarter amongst the folk of our tribes, there were storms to be weathered. They were ferocious, these storms, violent, tearing apart the ger, scattering the yat... yet we endured them, and we shall endure this. For then we were but men. Now we are the sons of the Great Khan, the tribe of the Warhawk himself, and no storm shall be our undoing.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: White Scars (8th Edition), pg. 33

quotes.speaker: Judd Clausel, Chaplain of the Ultramarines 4th Company
quotes.text: What is the terror of death? That we die with our work incomplete. What is the joy of life? To die knowing our task is done. I stand alongside warriors of honour, and the warrior who acts out of honour cannot fail. His duty is honour itself. Even a warrior's death—if it is honourable—is a reward and can be no failure, for it has come through duty. Seek honour as you act, and you will know no fear. A fortress is a living thing: the commander its brain, the walls its bones, the sensors its eyes and ears, the troops its blood, their weapons its fists. If one organ fails, the whole dies. And if the whole dies, no single organ can survive alone. And above all, remember this... for a warrior the only crime is cowardice.

quotes.speaker: Kalae Korvydae, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 10th Company
quotes.text: Corax knew his sons, for he knew himself. More than any other Primarch, he saw his own limitations and failings, and took what steps he could to save us from them. This was his greatest legacy. It is why we walk the Trifold Path that shields our souls from the sorrow and fury of the Sable Brand. It is why we master shadow, ambush and vigilance. It is why our enemies perceive their peril only when they cannot hope to escape it.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Raven Guard (8th Edition), pg. 57

quotes.speaker: Karrsak, Iron Father of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: There are those who call us cruel. For assessing the military value of those who cry for aid, for disdaining those too weak to look to their own survival, we are damned by countless bleeding hearts. Yet who truly is more cruel? He who saves only those who make him strong, or he who squanders his might in self aggrandising mercy-missions and thus stands helpless when the enemy strikes their final blow? There can be only one logical solution to this cogitation.
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 57

quotes.speaker: Kayvaan Shrike, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 3rd Company
quotes.text: If you see us, it is because we want you to see us. If you know our location, you see only that which we want you to see, draw only the conclusions we wish you to draw. It is when you do not see us, cannot detect us, that you should fear us the most.
quotes.source: Codex: Space Marines (8th Edition, 2nd Codex), pg. 45

quotes.speaker: Kayvaan Shrike, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 3rd Company
quotes.text: In the darkness all men are equal, save those that embrace it.

quotes.speaker: Kayvaan Shrike, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 3rd Company
quotes.text: We are coming. Look to the skies for your salvation.

quotes.speaker: Kayvaan Shrike, Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard 3rd Company
quotes.text: Wherever you tread, tread lightly. We are closer than you think, and our blades are sharp.

quotes.speaker: Kell, Dark Angels Sergeant
quotes.text: This is the holy bolter. It is a Godwyn-pattern model with an ammo counter, sinister/dexter locking mechanism, and a sickle magazine containing 30 rounds. Each round is a.75 calibre bolt with a diamantine tip, depleted deuterium core and mass-reactive detonator. It fires in four-round bursts.

quotes.speaker: Kill Team: Salvation,pg. 38
quotes.text: They are the future of the Chapter. When I look into the eyes of each, I know any one of them could one day be known as Chapter Master. For that reason must we grant them no kindness. They must be made strong.
quotes.source: Gregor Dessian, Chapter Master of the Imperial Fists

quotes.speaker: Kill Team: Salvation,pg. 48
quotes.text: We crawled through mud for four days, biting rain pouring down upon our heads in torrents. At night, several of our number froze to death. We left their corpses behind. The sergeants called them unworthy - they were right. It was only after this that we arrived at our destination, where our examination would truly begin.
quotes.source: align=center Bargus Urloch, Battle-brother of the Iron Hands Chapter

quotes.speaker: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. Eight, pg. 95
quotes.text: Aeonid Thiel. 135th Company.Marked for censure?
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Engagement, ch. Four, pg. 244

quotes.speaker: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. Eight, pg. 95
quotes.text: If he wasn't my brother, he'd be a political embarrassment and an impediment to the effective rule of the Imperium. I know what I'd do with him.I'm sure I could demonstrate how, lord.Was that a joke, sergeant?I may have just made a very unfortunate attempt at humour, lord.It was actually quite funny.
quotes.source: Aeonid Thiel, Sergeant of the Ultramarines 13th Chapter, 135th Company

quotes.speaker: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. Eight, pg. 95
quotes.text: Secundus Thryn, Librarian of the Doom Eagles

quotes.speaker: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. Eight, pg. 95
quotes.text: Their advantage is shock and surprise. Our counter-advantage must be a lack of convention.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Engagement, ch. Four, pgs. 246-247

quotes.speaker: Kor'sarro Khan, Khan of the White Scars 3rd Company, Master of the Hunt
quotes.text: Surround yourself with the greatest warriors at your command, or cower in the deepest and darkest hole you can find. It matters not. I shall take your head for the Great Khan and for the Emperor.

quotes.speaker: Korel Tyr, Sergeant of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: I have witnessed the exceptional performance of the Salamanders’ armoured vehicles, the exquisite calibration of their brothers’ arms and the adamantine integrity of their cuirasses. The Salamanders’ fascination with ornament is endemic, yet does not invalidate the conclusion that the function of the Chapter’s materiel is almost flawless.

quotes.speaker: Kyrin Torvaec, Raven Guard Scout Shadow Sergeant
quotes.text: Mankind has feared the dark since our ancestors could percieve the difference between night and day. No generation has escaped this. We, however, look into the black and feel no fear. We look into it and see home, and a weapon we can turn upon the Emperor's enemies. We are Raven Guard and we are one with shadows. We have looked mankind's most base fear in the eye and made it bend to our will.

quotes.speaker: Kyrin Torvaec, Raven Guard Scout Shadow Sergeant
quotes.text: The Emperor protects, this is the mantra preached throughout the Imperium. But in what manner does he do so? Through his Angels of Death - us. We are his knives in the dark, his wardens of the shadows and his ever watchful eyes. We are instruments of his will, created by him to punish his foes.

quotes.speaker: Leitz, Exorcists Captain
quotes.text: 'We shall never rest while a single renegade draws breath. Let them fear our names.'

quotes.speaker: Lion El'Johnson, Primarch of the Dark Angels
quotes.text: Look to your brothers. Know their hearts as you know your own. For if you do not, then all will be broken and lost to darkness.

quotes.speaker: Logan Grimnar, Great Wolf of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Follow me, Sons of Russ! This night our enemies shall feel the fangs of the Wolf!
quotes.source: Codex: Space Wolves (5th Edition), Back Cover

quotes.speaker: Logan Grimnar, Great Wolf of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Logan Grimnar, Bloody-Handed WarriorHe piles the skulls of his enemiesHe builds a mound of the fallenHis foes weep rivers of bloodLogan Grimnar, strong wolf of the packHis Sword hungers for red fleshHis guns thirst for battleHe laughs amidst the war-dinLogan Grimnar, father of wolvesHis sons haunt his enemiesSlay them where they falterAnd bring their pelts to Fenris.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 280 (UK),pg. 26(republished in Codex: Space Wolves (5th Edition),pg. 58)

quotes.speaker: Lord Solar Macharius
quotes.text: If they are so eager to die, and they will not heed the advice of their superiors, then let them rush headlong into the jaws of the lion. We can only hope some of them get caught in its throat.

quotes.speaker: Macer Varren, Loyalist Captain of the World Eaters
quotes.text: Garro, heed me. We have lost so many of our brethren to this schism, this damned bloody war! A traitor I will kill without hesitation, but we do not speak of a traitor! Our kinsman is lost.
quotes.source: Garro: Legion of One (Audio Book), Track 7: Aftermath

quotes.speaker: Macer Varren, Loyalist Captain of the World Eaters
quotes.text: Hell? There is no such thing, Garro! It's a figment of old idolatry, nothing more. We have no need for a place of horrors beyond death. Horus makes it for us, here, in the real!
quotes.source: Garro: Legion of One (Audio Book), Track 4: The Battlefield

quotes.speaker: Macer Varren, Loyalist Captain of the World Eaters
quotes.text: I was not cut out to lead like this, Garro. I am a killer, a meat cutter, not a brood mare struggling to protect a brood of weak and feeble runts! I lead warriors, not common folk!
quotes.source: Garro: Sword of Truth (Audio Book), Disc 2, Track 2: The Daggerline

quotes.speaker: Mad Chainsaw Johnson, White Scars Commander
quotes.text: Problems? Problems? There is no problem I cannot solve with this...

quotes.speaker: Major Goff Kurtane, 9th Radial Sarpoy Regiment
quotes.text: I, for one, was bloody glad to see the Snakes power in. Beautiful bastards, they are. I owe them my life and my soul.
quotes.source: The Sabbat Worlds Crusade (Background Book), pg. 24

quotes.speaker: Malcador the Sigillite
quotes.text: But without a purpose, an Astartes is nothing. A warrior without a war is no warrior at all.
quotes.source: Garro: Oath of Moment (Audio Book), Track 5: Flashback

quotes.speaker: Malkaan Feirros, Master of the Forge of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Heretics. Stand against us and we shall crush you. Capitulate, and we shall purge you for your weakness. Flee from us and we shall pursue you untiring until you can run no further. Attempt to trick us, to outmanoeuvre or misdirect us and by our logic we shall see through your pitiful ruse. There is no version of events in which you survive what now comes...
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Iron Hands (8th Edition), pg. 63

quotes.speaker: Malum Caedo, Ultramarines, Sternguard Veteran
quotes.text: Beg your dark gods for mercy. I have none!
quotes.source: The Art of Warhammer Video Games,pg. 112, Boltgun

quotes.speaker: Maratio, Epistolary of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: It is no secret that the gifts of Sanguinius have grown ever more pronounced amongst our brotherhood as the centuries have passed. Why this should be eludes even the wisest, but I say to you that we should not revile this power. It is a blessing bestowed by the Primarch himself, and nothing that flows from his illustrious legacy could ever be evil.

quotes.speaker: Markov, Sergeant of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: You may call me perverse, but few sights bring me greater joy than that of an enemy’s fortress looming vast and dark before me. It is the nature of Dorn’s sons to relish a challenge, and the higher these heretics raise their spires, the more catastrophic their ruination when we bring them crashing down!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 56

quotes.speaker: Medic Credo
quotes.text: Discourses on the Seventy-Nine Truths of Statecraft

quotes.speaker: Medic Credo
quotes.text: Do not fail your Brothers. Though their bodies die, their spirit must return to the Chapter. That is your charge.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 102 (UK), pgs. 74 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 28)

quotes.speaker: Meditations upon the Fang
quotes.text: The Space Wolves revere the Emperor as the greatest warrior of all time, the only being to have ever bested Leman Russ in hand-to-hand combat. When Space Wolves call upon Russ or the Emperor in battle, it is to witness the deeds of men and to judge the fallen.

quotes.speaker: Mersadie Oliton, Remembrancer
quotes.text: If you know no fear, do you also not know awe? Surprise? Majesty? Have you not seen things so bizarre they left you speechless? Shocked you? Unnerved you even?
quotes.source: Horus Rising (Novel),Part Two, ch. Nine, pg. 172

quotes.speaker: Motto
quotes.text: Forget nothing - forgive no-one.

quotes.speaker: Myrin Stormdawn, Corsair Baron,Lord of Light of Heaven
quotes.text: He was being rather tiresome, and very repetitive. I find that the more fanatical the followers of their Emperor are, the more limited their vocabulary becomes.

quotes.speaker: Nassio, Scout Sergeant of the Blood Angels 10th Company
quotes.text: The wrath you feel within? That is a weapon of last resort. Before you can learn to wield it, to kill with ferocity and abandon, you must first master fighting with skill, subtlety and restraint. After all, my brothers, the honed blade cuts the deepest.

quotes.speaker: Nathaniel Garro, Death Guard Captain
quotes.text: My name is Nathaniel Garro, and I am a Legion of One.
quotes.source: Garro: Oath of Moment (Audio Book), Track 3: The Field of Battle

quotes.speaker: Nico Trevellias, Captain of the White Consuls
quotes.text: They believe they are too good for the Codex Astartes, these... these wolf-men? Too good for ten millennia of tested and refined battle wisdom? Then let them fight as savages, I say. Let them fight and die.

quotes.speaker: Nicomaeus, Sanguinary Guard of the Blood Angels
quotes.text: The sons of Sanguinius fear neither heretical abomination nor xenos monstrosity. We have faced the terrors that lurk within our own minds, and seen the snarling beasts that seek to claw their way up from the depths of our souls. What can the foe threaten us with compared to that?

quotes.speaker: Nikhola Kalus, Inquisitor of the Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Knowledge is a sword, as sharp in hilt as it is in edge and commensurately dangerous to those without the skill or strength to wield it. One hopes that the Ultramarines understand this, and that they possess the requisite qualities to handle the blade they have drawn. Still, we of the Ordo Xenos will be watching. Closely.

quotes.speaker: Ortan Cassius, Master of Sanctity of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: The blasphemy of the Tyranids is such that only one solution is acceptable. Extermination. There can only be two sides in a fight - choose carefully, lest you and I find ourselves on different sides.

quotes.speaker: Pasanius Lysane, Ultramarines 4th Company Sergeant
quotes.text: That's why you summoned us here. To know where we came from is to know where we are and where we are going.

quotes.speaker: Popular Ultramarines Aphorism
quotes.text: To take a town, send a legionary. To take a city, send a squad. To take a world, send a company. To take a culture, send a Chapter.

quotes.speaker: Priad of the Iron Snakes
quotes.text: "Mingle"? What in the Emperor's name does that mean?
quotes.source: Brothers of the Snake (Novel), Part Six - II, pg. 171

quotes.speaker: Priad of the Iron Snakes
quotes.text: One warrior usually suffices.
quotes.source: Brothers of the Snake (Novel),Part One - XII

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: A fortress circumvented ceases to be an obstacle. A fortress destroyed ceases to be a threat. Do not forget the difference.

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: A fortress is built with blood and toil. Only by blood and toil may it be taken.

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Arm the bastards and they might win a few bloody worlds for us in between verses.
quotes.source: Horus Rising (Novel), Part One, ch. 3, pg. 46

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Beat your thoughts to the mould of your will.

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Do not underestimate the Squats. They survived for millennia cut off from the Imperium and assailed from all sides. Their determination and resilience is an example to all.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 111 (UK), pg. 30 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 167)

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Harden your soul against decadence. But do not despise it, for the soft appearance of the decadent may be deceptive. One need only consider the Harlequin dancers of the Eldar to see the truth of this proposition.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 106 (UK), pg. 13 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 194)

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Here I am and here shall I die.

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: Listen closely Brothers, for my life's breath is all but spent. There shall come a time far from now when our chapter itself is dying, even as I am now dying. Then my children, I shall list'n for your call from whatever realms of death hold me, and come I shall no-matter what laws of life and death forbid. At the end I will be there. For the final battle. For the Wolftime.
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000 Chapter Approved - The Book of the Astronomican, pg. 8

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: No man nor beast could best the Wolf-King,No tribe could stand against his armies.Within Russ' kingdom a truceExisted between man and wolf,His court was attended by the fiercest ofWarlords and the most beautiful of maidens.

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: There are those who undervalue the Penal Battalions. But they should consider this: should a man who has wronged the Emperor be allowed to wrong Him further? For each man executed is a man who can no longer serve, and to fail in service to the Emperor is the greatest of sins – Meditations on Imperial Command, Book XXI
quotes.source: White Dwarf 109 (UK), pg. 46(republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 148)

quotes.speaker: Primarch Leman Russ of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: You strive for victory. That is obvious. What may be less obvious is the nature of victory. There are circumstances in which you can destroy the enemy utterly, without loss to your own forces, and yet the victory may be his. In all situations, you must first decide on the nature of victory, and then take steps to secure it. Avoid the instinct of fight first and think later.

quotes.speaker: Principle Lessons of the Iron Hands
quotes.text: Only the spirit is pure.The mind may be swayed, the body may fail.Only the soul cradles vengeance purely.Become the weapon of the soul.Where the mind hesitates, overcome it.Where the body fails, replace it.Aspire to the condition of the Primarch.Emulate his union with steel.Never waver.Never retreat.Never doubt.The flesh is weak.The weak shall be purged.What remains is strength.

quotes.speaker: Proverb
quotes.text: A fortress won't stop the Marines. But it may slow them down.

quotes.speaker: Proverb
quotes.text: Let the repentant enjoy a merciful end.

quotes.speaker: Pugilis, Commander of the Iron Knights
quotes.text: We attack without warning, mercy or hesitation. Let the enemies of Mankind know that we will purge every last one of them, and let them shake in fear.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two - Second Edition: War Machines of the Adeptus Astartes, pg. 10

quotes.speaker: Ra’Tsen, Chaplain of the Salamanders
quotes.text: There remain five populated systems behind us, containing billions of the Emperor’s subjects. There yet remain hundreds of these T’au, seeking to enslave them under the pretence of unity. I see the fire in your eyes, my brothers, you who have endured the heinous firepower of the foe. It is not an easy path we tread, not a simple objective we strive for, but it is the one we choose. For Vulkan! For the Emperor!

quotes.speaker: Ragnar Blackmane, Wolf Lord of the Space Wolves
quotes.text: We may be few, and our enemies many. Yet so long as there remains one of us still fighting, one who still rages in the name of justice and truth, then by the Allfather, the galaxy shall yet know hope.

quotes.speaker: Red Fury (Novel), ch. Thirteen
quotes.text: For he today that sheds his blood with me shall be my battle-brother eternal.

quotes.speaker: Red Fury (Novel), ch. Thirteen
quotes.text: We are disorder, that is true. But that is what we were made to be. The wild and the random. If each Successor embodies the traits of The Great Angel, then that is what the Flesh Tearers are, just as the Blood Swords are his martial prowess, the Sanguine his secrecy, the Flesh Eaters his carnivore's fangs! But the Blood Angels are the melding of all of those things, and that is why I will always envy you, cousin.
quotes.source: Gabriel Seth, Chapter Master of the Flesh Tearers

quotes.speaker: Rheshhan of the Salamanders, 4th Company
quotes.text: The Storm Giants’ ways are ancient, though their origins are lost to the ravaging inferno of time. I witnessed their neophytes on Torquar being led in what their sergeant called the Doctrine of Ash, and felt a sliver of recognition. My old sergeant of the 7th was as patient and wise, and I knew then the strength of our allies.

quotes.speaker: Rhetoricus of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: DO WE BEMOAN SUCH LOSSES? NO! WE ARE THE FISTS! WE DO NOT NEED TO HIBERNATE OR SPIT VENOM. WE CRUSH OUR ENEMIES!
quotes.source: Codex Supplement: Imperial Fists (8th Edition), pg. 21

quotes.speaker: Rhetoricus of the Imperial Fists
quotes.text: The Emperor commands us. Dorn guides us. Honour shields us.Fear our name, for it is vengeance.

quotes.speaker: Rites of Girding
quotes.text: Bless this plate, that it may deflect the blade of my enemy and the bullets of the unrighteous.

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: It is the great irony of the Legiones Astartes: engineered to kill to achieve a victory of peace that they can then be no part of.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. 3, pg. 30

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Let them bestride the galaxy like the gods of old, sheltering Mankind from destruction at the hands of an uncaring universe.

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Lorgar of Colchis. You may consider the following. One: I entirely withdraw my previous offer of solemn ceasefire. It is cancelled, and will not be made again, to you or to any other of your motherless bastards. Two: you are no longer any brother of mine. I will find you, I will kill you, and I will hurl your toxic corpse into hell's mouth.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), System//Kill, ch. 3, pg. 195

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: My brother, hear me. Warriors of the XVII Legion, hear me. This violence is against the code of the Legiones Astartes and against the will of our father, the Emperor. In the name of the Five Hundred Worlds of Ultramar, I implore you to cease fire and stand down. Open communication with me. Let us speak. Let us settle this. This action is an error of the most tragic kind. Cease fire. I, Roboute Guilliman, give you my solemn pledge that we will deal with each other frankly and fairly if these hostilities can be suspended. I urge you to respond.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), System//Kill, ch. 1, pg. 167

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Never wish for danger. Danger needs no help. There is no such thing as fate that can be tempted, but morale is never improved by an active lust for war.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. 3, pg. 40

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Space Marines excel at warfare because they were designed to excel at everything. Each of you will become a leader, a ruler, the master of your world and, because there is no more fighting to be done, you will bend your transhuman talents to governance and culture.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel), Target//Acquisition, ch. 3, pg. 30

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: They shall be pure of heart and strong of body, untainted by doubt and unsullied by self-aggrandisement. They will be bright stars on the firmament of battle, Angels of Death whose shining wings bring swift annihilation to the enemies of Man. So it shall be for a thousand times for a thousand years, unto the very end of eternity and the extinction of mortal flesh. Codex Astartes opening
quotes.source: Codex: Space Marines (6th Edition)(e-book), pg. 246 Deathwatch Core Rulebook, pg. 11

quotes.speaker: Roboute Guilliman, Primarch of the Ultramarines
quotes.text: Two things to note there, Sergeant. One is that there is no such thing as a minor detail. Information is victory. One cannot and should not dismiss any data until one is in a position to evaluate its significance, and that only comes with hindsight.
