quotes.text: Even Imperial cogboys aren’t exactly demonstrative, unless they’re getting angry about how you’ve disrespected a machine spirit. Besides, they’re experts in telling you to take a walk into a fusion reactor, but hiding it behind jargon and pretending they don’t understand how normal people communicate.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Behold thou corruption, and unthinking, end it.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Blessed is the mind too small for doubt.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Deny the enemy his works and means, then deny to him his life.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Eternal vigilance is the duty of all the Machine God's servants. Thus do the Skitarii have their eyelids removed upon inception, that they might behold the Omnissiah's works until the day their servitude is ended.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Fury has its uses.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: This cogitator provides a strategic overview of the battlefield. By which I mean, of course, the entire planet.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Toll the bell for the spark of life, Sing the praise of the forge's heart, Stoke the fires of eternal war.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: Your petty claims of primacy disgust me, xenos. This world bears the mark of the almighty Machine God. It is there in the fascium deposits left for us to extract. It is evident in the wells of trans-sulphur, in the liquid diamonite that rains from the sky and the seam of transuranium. This world belongs to Mankind.

quotes.speaker: Vanguard Unit 09-TYBA, Radtainment Division
quotes.text: Let our enemies come. Let their blood be the unguent that greases the wheels of our endless blessed artifice.

quotes.speaker: Verses Fulminatus 59.31
quotes.text: The broken in body can blame the bullet or the sword, but the broken in mind have all creation to curse.

quotes.speaker: Verses Macrologica 17.4
quotes.text: The only logical response to the presence of the xenos, is hatred.

quotes.speaker: Vox Servitor Th3ta
quotes.text: I am a Child of the Omnissiah, cultist of the Machine God. The rites of manifold applications, the liturgies of ignition and the songs of Engine-seeing are mine own to know. I speak to the spirits of ancient tech-machines, from the warrior heart of a battle tank to the secret wisdom of the cogitator.

quotes.speaker: Vox-thief record retrieved from Combat Servitor 58438-B, cover as Labour Unit GT67x comprimised: eliminate threat to salvage inspection mission
quotes.text: Look at it, girl. Dead eyes, grey skin, rancid stink: a clicking, clacking labour-mech. Don't be fooled. I've seen 'em in the dead hours, on errands no one told 'em to do, wearing tech you never see in the day. Honest salvage-runners like me have a nose for strangeness like that. Sometimes, it's even like they're listening to you...

quotes.speaker: Warnings of the Cult Mechanicus Axioms XV/XVI
quotes.text: Flesh is fallible, but ritual honours the machine spirit. To break with ritual is to break with faith.

quotes.speaker: Yordus Tan, Ministorum Preacher
quotes.text: Deluded blasphemers all — would you rather believe in our evident God-Emperor, or some spirit in the machine? And you’d get a better conversation from a slab of plascrete. Though I must admit, they did a wonderful job on my new eyes.

quotes.speaker: Zelith-Delta-27, Skitarii
quotes.text: The odds of success are approximately 3752 to 1. As the Omnissiah wills, I shall find a way.

Orks are everything Aeldari are not, our very antithesis. Short-lived, multitudinous, ferocious, and utterly ruled by their primal whims. The gods play a cruel joke in that Orks never suffer for their lack of foresight or their caprice. Indeed, Orks never suffer at all: they revel in pain and danger and charge joyfully towards doom, be it theirs or their enemy’s. They’re perfectly engineered for the galaxy as it is; a brutish horde rampaging across the cruel, war-torn worlds of a dire age.

The ancient and indefatigable enemy. While the rest of the galaxy crumbles, Chaos flourishes. It is a perversion of everything that has ever been and ever should be, allowed to run rampant in every unchecked emotion. In an age of perils, Chaos is the greatest and most pressing danger. One aspect of Chaos, She Who Thirsts, owes their existence to our former empire — the lasting shameful legacy of the Aeldari. Our decadence birthed a god and simultaneously doomed our souls, and the Aeldari may be shackled to this fate for eternity.

The doomed Necrontyr traded their souls and essence for survival. The oldest and most powerful among the Necrons remember some of what came to pass in long-forgotten millennia. The depth and breadth of their knowledge is every bit as dangerous as their soulless warriors. We find no solace in the fact that the Necrons collapsed under the weight of tragedy, for more of these grim parodies of life still lie buried across the galaxy. Should they ever rise in unison, we are all doomed.

The Imperium is doomed. As doomed as the old Aeldari empire. So it has been for many lifetimes, but the Humans won’t allow their corpse of a culture to pass. They have lost their history and lack the wit to see their future. Their civilisation crumbles and decays, and the corruptive touch of Chaos festers ceaselessly in the darkened cracks. We do not fail to see the parallels between the fate of the Imperium and our own. We can only watch in horror as the Mon-keigh, with their cruelty, barbarism, and dogma, fall blindly into the same mistakes that have left the remnants of our own empire sundered across the galaxy.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 67

quotes.text: The T'au have a high opinion of themselves; one that we do not share. For such a young race they take unwarranted pride in their rapid advances. While we know from our own bitter experience that hubris leads inevitably to catastrophic mistakes, the T'au have yet to truly endure the cruel nature of the galaxy. The T'au’s greatest failures are ahead of them. Should they learn humility, wisdom, and regret, then the we may be willing to extend our sympathies.

Tyranids are an abominable horror from beyond the galaxy. Unfathomable and terrifying in equal measure, their hive fleets leave nothing but a lifeless shadow in their wake as they sweep across planets and systems. The destruction they bring casts a monstrous shadow in the warp that cripples the mind and sickens the senses. They exist for no purpose other than to devour life and must be curtailed and contained at every opportunity; unchecked, they’ll smother the galaxy under their teeming advance. That cannot come to pass.

quotes.speaker: Abriel Hume, Imperial Commander
quotes.text: Trust not in their appearance, for the Eldar are as alien to good, honest men as the vile Tyranids and savage Orks. There is no understanding them for there is nothing to understand - they are a random force in the universe.

quotes.speaker: Ahzek Ahriman,Arch- Sorcerer of the Thousand Sons
quotes.text: For the ancient aeldari, life was a cycle of birth, the fulfilment of desire, and a comfortable death, safe in the knowledge the soul would live again. The birth of their nemesis, the dark god Slaanesh, shattered that cycle forever. Now these once-great starfarers cower in the shadows, too afraid of their own lusts to embrace the Full spectrum of sensation. It is a fate they justly deserve. In truth, there can be no escape from the doom they have brought upon themselves – not this side of the grave. Fate is a cruel mistress, and not to be courted lightly.

quotes.speaker: Alaril Adderi,Chieftain of the Vorp-adders Wild Host
quotes.text: Hear me, my kinsfolk! We have sworn the knife-pledge, we have eaten the fruit of the kybald, we have painted our faces red with the blood of the slain. There is naught left to do now but ride. Ride, my kin, to glory, to victory or to an honourable death. Spare only their leader - for he is mine.

quotes.speaker: Alek Lir,Excavator Engine Driver
quotes.text: Honestly! Being spooked by a few unknown xenos skulking around near the dig site! That hill is hundreds of metres away, and at that range, a few infantry can hardly pose a threat to -- Final words before being killed by a Shadow Spectre
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Koronus Bestiary, pg. 51

quotes.speaker: Amon Broinach, Guardsman survivor of the Sten Hill Massacre
quotes.text: Doc said I’d never hear again — but I still hear that screaming in my nightmares.-regarding the Howling Banshees
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 27

quotes.speaker: Anthrillien Morningchild, Autarch of Yme-Loc
quotes.text: Only when you have soared through the morning skies on wings of flame can you understand the Hawk. Only when you have fallen screaming upon those who know they are already dead can you understand the Banshee. Only when you have annihilated those who would oppose you can you truly understand the power of the Dragon. And only one who has travelled but ultimately turned away from each of these paths can understand the Autarch.
quotes.source: Codex: Eldar (4th Edition),pg. 29 Codex: Aeldari (9th Edition),pg. 39

quotes.speaker: Arenal, Farseer
quotes.text: Every moment of anger, hate, deceit, pain, suffering, pleasure, and desire is mirrored in the power of Chaos. When its legions march, they march to return upon us a ruin that is of our own making.
quotes.source: FFG Teaser for "The Final Gambit", supplement for Conquest Card Game (last accessed 8 May 2016)

quotes.speaker: Asurmen, Phoenix Lord of the Dire Avengers,founder of the Path of the Warrior
quotes.text: To strike! That is fulfilment distilled into a single perfect moment. Be the first to strike.

quotes.speaker: Aulirel Doomhand, Striking Scorpion Exarch
quotes.text: Attack swiftly, and without warning. This is the mantra of the Striking Scorpions – it is the way of all our kind.

quotes.speaker: Autarch Intrisiel,of Biel-Tan
quotes.text: Our race has not had the luxury of debating moral philosophy in millennia. When it did, our ancestors laughed at the thought. Now I dismiss it also, as we attempt to survive their folly.

quotes.speaker: Autarch Lorinth of Biel-Tan
quotes.text: Strike them all down. Our task will be complete only when every last one of the creatures has been slain, their filthy presence upon this planet washed clean only by their blood. Their crude factories and very footsteps have contaminated this maiden world. They have sullied that which was never theirs, but ours by right. Cast their shallow souls into the black void that awaits them. Do not waver in your duty to the Swordwind. Do not pity the mon-keigh, for their end will be quick, and their war will be over. Save your pity, save it for ourselves. Yes, my kinfolk, pity only ourselves. Despite all that has come to pass here, our war has only just begun.

quotes.speaker: Autarch Relethere of Alaitoc
quotes.text: They seek to give rise to the god of death, these Ynnari, and in doing so become the masters of all mortality. Are the lessons of the aeons so easily cast aside?

quotes.speaker: Autarch Shamai Miennan
quotes.text: To strike at will, swiftly and surely, and stride beneath the sky – that is freedom.

quotes.speaker: Autarch Talyesin Fharenal, Craftworld Saim-Hann
quotes.text: FFG Announcing "The Final Gambit" - War Pack of the Conquest game - (last accessed 4 December 2015)

quotes.speaker: Avatar of Khaine
quotes.text: Behold, the fiery destroyer of worlds.

quotes.speaker: Avatar of Khaine
quotes.text: I walk again, a god among mere mortals.

quotes.speaker: Avatar of Khaine
quotes.text: Meshruuk avasha solkara Khaine!
quotes.source: Hammer and Bolter (Animated Series), ep. 6 - "In the Garden of Ghosts"

quotes.speaker: Avenelle, Farseer
quotes.text: FFG Card Doom from the game Warhammer 40'000 - Conquest (last accessed 8 May 2016)

quotes.speaker: Avichor, Swooping Hawk Exarch
quotes.text: We, who have conquered the heavens above, do not fear the vermin below.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 29

quotes.speaker: Baharroth
quotes.text: FFG Card Baharroth from the game Warhammer 40'000 - Conquest (last accessed 16 December 2015)

quotes.speaker: Barbarius, Confessor
quotes.text: Vile, unnatural witchcraft pervades all elements of the Eldar - their minds, their culture and technology.

quotes.speaker: Battle-chant to Khaine the Bloody-Handed God
quotes.text: Blood Runs. Anger Rises. Death Awakes. War Calls!

quotes.speaker: Battle-chant to Khaine the Bloody-Handed God
quotes.text: Eldar Ranger,prisoner of the Inquisition,tortured and subsequently executed

quotes.speaker: Battle-cry
quotes.text: For the tears of the living! And the souls of the dead!
quotes.source: Hammer and Bolter (Animated Series), ep. 6 - "In the Garden of Ghosts"

quotes.speaker: Beac-dair, Ranger of Alaitoc and liaison to Imperial forces.
quotes.text: Make no mistake mon-keigh, we do not fight for you, or for your corpse-Emperor. We are allied here today because destiny has seen fit to bind our fates together. We do not relish that our futures are intertwined, but if you would live to see another day, then you will do as I say. Order your soldiers to cease firing their primitive artillery upon the ridge line, for it is there the Asuryani warhost will arrive. My kin will drive the tainted ones back towards your lines, where you would do well to be prepared. And please, above all else, stand down wind from me.

quotes.speaker: Brin, Colonel
quotes.text: Some call the Eldar decadent. If that is true, the Imperial Army could do with that kind of decadence.-Last words, executed Heretic 436. M38

quotes.speaker: Caerys, Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: They shall come, expecting the obvious, the simple, the artless. They shall stab at the shadows with confused minds and troubled hearts. Meanwhile, we shall appear unseen from ten directions, and from every one strike a fatal blow.

quotes.speaker: Caerys, Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: We have made battle in this place before. We have fought and won. Fought because we knew that we must. We won, and brought upon ourselves glory that will not fade as long as Ulthwé thrives. Now we are called to battle again. Must we perform a duty which we have already accomplished? If we fight and fail, are our former victories tarnished?

quotes.speaker: Calmainoc, Ulthwé Seer, on Exarchs
quotes.text: Behind our consciousness lies a profound abyss, about which we riddle and dance through the Paths of our kind. The Aspects of Khaine are sprinkled around the rim like garrisons of our sanity. The exarchs are the champions of our souls, keeping the darkness at bay. Beware the Menshad Korum; the hunter who stalks himself. Although trapped in the Path of the Warrior, this exarch owes his soul to no Aspect and knows not who he is. None is closer to Khaine than the Lost Warrior, none closer to the abyss in our souls.

quotes.speaker: Captain Steinback, after-action report
quotes.text: Those Eldar ships were all over us. I've never seen anything move quite like them. They'd rush in and hit us with volleys of laser fire and missiles, and then they'd kneel over and be away before we could engage them properly. They didn't have shields like our void shields, and their ships were fragile too, so if we could hit we'd do them some serious damage. But our augers just couldn't get a proper fix on them, so hitting them wasn't easy, not easy at all. If the rest of the flotilla hadn't got back when it did I think we'd have been doomed.

quotes.speaker: Captain Winderson Vorsingrass, of the Lunar-class Cruiser Benevolent Fury, 51st Gothic Fleet, Journal Extract
quotes.text: There is little that gives a commander of the Navy such a feeling of dread as the sight of a vessel of the accursed aeldari. Fear, yes – we should not baulk at admitting our fear, for we are not the Emperor’s Angels, and weakening nerves can be steadied by catechisms of faith. The twisted ships of the traitor fleets, the brutal monstrosities of the orks, and other, stranger things can all inspire fear. However, they call only for a simple response – battle.The aeldari are the great unknown of the void. At times they will strike without warning, murder-swift and with merciless precision, until some unknowable aim has been achieved, at which point they will withdraw. At others, their vessels will slide by without acknowledgement, ignoring us as we might ignore braying livestock caged for transport in port. Sometimes they will appear as saviours and drive away our enemies, though only the witless and faithless would think that they perform such actions for our benefit. And yet when the mood takes them, they engage in the cruellest of sport, harrying and disabling our ships before boarding to make red ruin of the crew, and taking many more for some foul purpose or amusement in whatever hell they call home.This is why I feel the cold fingers of dread when I see those wasp-waisted vessels flickering in and out of view against the stars, for how can I make the best strategic decision when this enemy is so unknowable? A traitor or an ork or the creatures of a hive fleet are always an enemy, whereas today, aeldari interests may align with ours. At the least, to engage them without thought is to invite retribution, and perhaps find our fleets crushed between them and some other threat against whom, on another day and without a second enemy, we might have prevailed.Against whom, on another day, they might even have aided us.And yet, my duty is to safeguard the Imperium against all threats, and these arrogant xenos most certainly count as one. If I do not strike first, I might surrender the advantage to them if war is their intent. If I do not strike at all, and they leave us be in turn, might yet those same ships not darken the skies of an Imperial world tomorrow, or next week, or next year?They are a conundrum, and a scourge, and most vexatious. Those scholars I have met who claim some knowledge of the aeldari declare that they are but the remnants of an ancient empire, and even now are fading from the stars regardless of our actions.I can only pray to the Emperor that they fade faster.

quotes.speaker: Caslith the Fireheart, Dire Avenger
quotes.text: Too long have the Children of the Stars wept bitter tears. Today, we unleash our vengeance!
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 25

quotes.speaker: Dalhesh, Howling Banshee
quotes.text: Ours are the screams that pierced the mind of Khaine. You shall hear our coming – for we shall announce it – and upon hearing our cry you shall know that your death approaches.

quotes.speaker: Death Chant recited in preparation for a Soul-Graft
quotes.text: Though my body dies, I will remain. Evermore.My spirit stays on in this flesh of metal. Evermore.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 101 (UK), pg. 53 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 73)

quotes.speaker: Dismali, Dark Reaper
quotes.text: They think they are safe. They think they are beyond the cold grasp of death. I am a servant of death, and they have underestimated my reach.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 24

quotes.speaker: Egarathiel the Grey,from A Legacy Most Bitter
quotes.text: We were blessed in so many ways by our creators, fashioned with so much care. Naught were we incapable of creating or achieving. Our ancestors' triumphs likely exceed what we can even imagine. What wonders did they bring about, that are now lost to time? What glourious heights did they reach? What more could they have done? That, none of us will ever know, for their accomplishments and victories were only exceeded by their pride and short-sightedness.

quotes.speaker: Elarique Swiftblade, Autarch of Alaitoc
quotes.text: There is no corner of the galaxy that has not felt the eagle-keen gaze of Alaitoc.

quotes.speaker: Eldar message to Colonel Brand at the Third Battle of Belafon I
quotes.text: Each time I think of our ancestors, what they achieved and how they all let it crash down upon themselves, I weep because I know that, had any of us been in their place, we would have done the same.

quotes.speaker: Eldar message to Colonel Brand at the Third Battle of Belafon I
quotes.text: Hone your blade, sharpen your mind. Don your war helm, feel the wrath of Khaine grow within you. It is a time for war, a time for bloodshed, a time for the dance of death.

quotes.speaker: Eldar message to Colonel Brand at the Third Battle of Belafon I
quotes.text: Many Aspects are there of Khaine, the Lord of Murder. Ebon Talon, Crystal Dragon, Slicing Orb, Blinding Claw, Shadow Spectre, Coiled Serpent, Golden Raptor. These, and dozens more.

quotes.speaker: Eldar message to Colonel Brand at the Third Battle of Belafon I
quotes.text: Your understanding is not required mon-keigh, merely your surrender...

quotes.speaker: Eldorath Starbane
quotes.text: The gaze of Alaitoc sees all, there will be no escape for you.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: All the stars in the sky cannot blot out the hateful glare of the red moon's eye. The birthing place of the Great Enemy pulses with all the malice of a daemon that is dreaming, casting its shadow over all we have ever done and all we ever shall.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: He who sees his own doom can better avoid its path. He who sees the doom of others can deliver it.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: I have made mistakes. This I must — and do— acknowledge. My efforts have been thwarted by foes. In eagerness and passion I did not see the terrible danger. I pay for my failures with exile, but I do not apologise for my motives. All that I have done has been for the survival of our species. As the galaxy grows ever more perilous, we must become more willing to act decisively. I know that I am.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: I yearn to take my place within the Dome, but there is so much yet to do.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: It is a sad truth that in order to secure life we must become servants of death. Such are the depths that our race has been pushed to. But through this we are to become like the phoenix, arising anew out of the ashes of our former selves.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: Since the time of The Fall, our race has been haunted by what we, in our reckless pursuit of hedonistic indulgence, gave birth to. Though our dreams once overturned worlds and quenched suns, we are now but fitful shadows clinging to the edge of existence. All the stars in the sky cannot blot out the hateful glare of the Red Moon's Eye. The birthing place of The Great Enemy pulses with all the malice of a daemon that is dreaming, casting its shadow over all we have ever done and all we ever shall. Every twisted strand of Fate and casting of the Runes leads me to this time, to this place, and it is clear that the final battle awaits me at the ancient Crone Worlds. A conflict the likes of which has not been seen since the Mon-keigh warred amongst themselves, and their corpse of a seer fell to his traitorous son, is coming and all my steps lead towards it, no matter that I walk other paths. I see the stars stained red with the blood of the Mon-Keigh and, though their wars do not concern me and I would gladly let them destroy one another, I know that to avoid this fight is to condemn my race to inevitable doom. And though all I see is darkness, I know that I will not flinch from my destiny.
quotes.source: Codex: Eye of Terror (3rd Edition), pg. 2

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: The Aeldari psyche is a powerful weapon. It can slay a distant foe with a pulse of thought. Like any other, it must be used with skill. Since the coming of the Dathedian its edges grow sharper than ever before, its grip more perilous to grasp. The slightest misstep can see its wielder and even the reality in which they stand, laid open and bloody.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: What do Humans know of our pain? We have sung songs of lament since before your ancestors crawled on their bellies from the sea.
quotes.source: Codex: Eldar (4th Edition), pg. 4 Codex: Eldar (2nd Edition), pg. 8

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: When there is no other way, the perilous path is the only road to salvation.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: You make a grave error. We work here to fight a Dark God that casts shadows upon both our cultures. Your war is not with us, Artemis of Posul. I call parley. Let us combine our efforts against a mutual foe.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran,Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: You wish to be free of the influence of my kind. You see the armour as a gaoler holding you hostage to our whims. Know this – the fates of Humanity and Aeldari are bound together. Either both species will survive, or neither will. Your Emperor understands this. There are greater enemies than the Primordial Annihilator. In the times to come, you will see. The struggle is only beginning. The old war returns. Remember this conversation, and reconsider carefully, on the day realisation comes, whether you wish to stand alone.

quotes.speaker: Eoheran Quilherna,Last Child of Dilherran
quotes.text: What she showed me was indescribable. Just as well, as I'd sooner die than be forced to describe it.-concerning a Shadowseer
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 49

quotes.speaker: Exarch Quaillindral
quotes.text: The time for using the knife to remove this cancer is long gone. Bring forth the torch.

quotes.speaker: Farseer Kelmon
quotes.text: Gather the dead for war, let them join our ranks, lest we are forced to join theirs.
quotes.source: Iyanden: A Codex: Eldar Supplement, pg. 16

quotes.speaker: Farseer Taldeer, Craftworld Ulthwé
quotes.text: I hear the song of the celestial heavens, and the music is cacophonous. It is strange but I find comfort in its dissonance. Righting the discord of the universal opus is what’s given my people purpose when, by all rights we as a race should have collapsed in upon ourselves. It is in such moments when species find their greatness or settle back into the muck that spawned them... For myself I have purpose if only to correct one particular stray note in a symphony ran amok. Win or lose, this battle is already my victory, my triumph. It is now fate which decides whether I am there to celebrate that fact or not.
quotes.source: Dawn of War - Dark Crusade Classics

quotes.speaker: Farseer Torc Silvereye
quotes.text: It is our duty to rekindle the fires of hope in our people, not to quarrel with one another about who breathes life into the flame.
quotes.source: Iyanden: A Codex: Eldar Supplement, pg. 22

quotes.speaker: Farseer Ulthos of Alaitoc
quotes.text: ... and the Engines of Vaul will smite them, and bring fire upon them, and in agony they will depart this realm, their souls screaming into the black void that awaits their pitiful race. And with their banishment there will be peace in this place, and we will be one step further along the road we must tread...
quotes.source: Imperial Armour II - Ork, Eldar and Dark Eldar Vehicles for Warhammer 40,000, pg. 15

quotes.speaker: Farseer Vyndere
quotes.text: All other xenos are crude and barbarous, but none to the degree of the Orks. I fought them four millennia ago, and if anything, their intelligence has diminished further since then. We will easily outmaneuver their charge, then strike the killing blow.'—Final battle briefing given by Farseer Vyndere
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 80

quotes.speaker: Fuegan
quotes.text: Control the Dragon, channel it's power, and in all things maintain absolute focus.

quotes.speaker: Fuegan
quotes.text: You are the hand that must cast the spear of flames. Control the Dragon, channel it's power, and in all things maintain absolute focus. Only the concentrated beam can penetrate, and only that which is tempered will not break. Finally, be not afraid. In the end, everything burns.

quotes.speaker: Gek Facekrakka, Ork Nob
quotes.text: Oh, they’s ded shooty all right, hard to catch as well, an’ there’s barely enough on them little bones for anyfin’ like a good crumpin. Go for the weird lads wiv funny masks and strange, thin choppas if you’re afta a propa fight.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 76

quotes.speaker: Ghost Captain Tyniss of the Crow Spirits
quotes.text: The humans think they conquered the stars and brought order and civilisation to the chaos of the void, when all they have is what we left for them; the bitter scraps of the most powerful empire this galaxy has even known.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Koronus Bestiary, pg. 43

quotes.speaker: Giladrea Serenti, Dire Avengers Exarch of the Shimmering Blade Shrine, Saim-Hann Craftworld
quotes.text: Slight are the numbers of the Asuryani. So swindled is our race that many of my kin are drawn to tears should they dwell on that sad fact for a fraction too long. But we have learned the lessons taught by being so few. Each of my warriors is equal to a dozen Mon-keigh or more. With the thrust of a sword at the right moment I can dictate whether a system will still live in a millennium's time. With a burst of my pistol's shuriken I can preserve a world for generations more.

quotes.speaker: Grand Master Roger Evars
quotes.text: You ask me what my Order needs? I will tell you. Give me the Pulse Lasers of the Eldar to mount on my Warlords.

quotes.speaker: Historicus Ostalan Varus
quotes.text: The Path of the Warlock wends along the past, present and future alike. His mind is free of the constraints of time, and his destiny and promise are morged into a single state of being.

quotes.speaker: Hrythar Dreamweave, Wild Rider
quotes.text: The wind whipping across your face as your blades whip across the throats of the foe. It makes the blood sing.

quotes.speaker: Ieldan Soect, My Time Amongst the Eldar, or How I Visited Iyanden Craftworld and Lived,Chapter IX — The Living Craft,Excerpt
quotes.text: Having spent so much time amongst the Eldar, I can say that I am thankful for this; the Eldar as a race can be cruel, callous and unfeeling but, speaking as one who has spent much of his time travelling the Galaxy (all be it unwillingly), I can say without reservation that the Eldar craftworlds are one of the most beautiful and wondrous creations I have ever seen. Their loss would be a calamity not just for the Eldar but for all civilised creatures.

quotes.speaker: Illithrys Goldenmane, Everguard of Alaitoc
quotes.text: The howling scream of a Hemlock's passage...once heard, it can never truly be forgotten. Every night it echoes in my mind like a trapped soul; I know I will carry it to my grave. It was the sound of pure anguish, of the dead torn from their rest to terrify the living.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Alyssana Nero
quotes.text: I protect the Imperium, Chetta. The aeldari are a weak, spent race – spiteful and resentful, yes, but incapable of threatening us on a large scale, and often intelligent enough to realise that what threatens us also threatens what remains of them. You can speak with them, exchange information, even trade with them, so long as you remember never to truly trust them. Of all the great space-faring civilisations, the aeldari can be exterminated last.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: A craftworld is sentient being, with a hundred thousand minds.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: After the great cataclysm known as the Fall, the Eldar craftworlds were scattered across the galaxy. The Eldar path was begun as a means of controlling the raging emotions and desire for perfection that is the lure all Eldar must resist. The Path of the Warrior was founded on many craftworlds, and grew into the Aspects that survive to this day. These original Aspect templates were created by the mighty Eldar fighters, the original Exarchs of the Bloody-handed God. The Eldar believe that these ancient figures survive to this day, continuing their fight for the Eldar cause whenever and wherever they appear. Many Eldar also believe that these Pheonix Lords, or Asurya as they are known to the Eldar, are no longer truly alive, that their armour is animated by the spirits of Exarchs who have been absorbed into the consciousness of the Phoenix Lord. If this is true, then the PHeonix Lords would indeed by mighty warriors, with many thousands of years experience.-Extracts from Inquisitor Czevak's Ancient Wanderers - the Phoenix Lords of the Eldar examined

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Against the Great Enemy the Eldar have no hope of victory. They hang on to existence, yet their grip upon the universe is slipping, their hold becomes more precarious with every passing year.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Ask not the Eldar a question, for they will give you three answers, all of which are true and terrifying to know.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Compared to them we are but children. Sadly, it is the way of things that children never learn from the errors of previous generations, and are ever doomed to repeat them.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: It is from Khaine that the Eldar have learned the greatest secrets of war and bloodshed. It is from him that they draw their strength in battle, giving them the courage and determination to fight. When the Eldar enter battle they divorce their minds from surrounding events, allowing the spirit of Khaine to infuse them. In this way they can perform horrific acts of violence, whilst keeping their mind pure.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Listen not for the approach of the Eldar, nor look to the skies for their coming. Their craft are silent as midnight, swift as dawn, and bring naught but death to the enemies of that forlorn race.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: The first thing one must learn about the Eldar is that they are a race of fragments, broken and scattered across the galaxy. In culture, geography and technology, the disparate elements of the Eldar race vary wildly. Even within a single sub-race (the so-called kindreds of the Craftworld Eldar, Exodites, 'Dark' Eldar and the mysterious Harlequins) there is great diversity of tradition and attitude - Teachings of the Unholy
quotes.source: Eldar & Dark Eldar Collectors' Guide, pg. 3 Codex: Craftworlds (8th Edition), pg. 42

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: The mind of the Farseer is utterly inhuman in its depth and complexity. Without mercy or moral feeling, his consciousness stands upon the edge of spiritual destruction. That he does not fall must be a result of constraints and balances that only an Eldar could understand. To a mere Human it is yet another reminder that we are but children in comparison to that ancient and powerful race.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Though I have seen within the Black Library and spoken to its most terrible guardian, I can never reveal what happened there; not to any man nor even the Emperor himself for I am so forsworn to powers beyond your knowledge. I can only say that a time of inconceivable horror is about to begin. A time when mankind with all the might of the Imperium cannot endure when the strength of the Eldar fails. Even now, our doom stalks us across the stars.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Warp Spider; Fire Dragon; Dire Avenger. These are no mere titles. They are identities, and represent the entire lives of those who hold them. When a warrior dons their mask, this identity consumes them, and they become a blade of their war god. Their minds race through fighting techniques they have mastered over a thousand-thousand repetitions. They become more than killers. They become death.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: Without mercy or moral feeling, the Farseer’s consciousness stands upon the edge of spiritual destruction.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Navis Primer, pg. 125

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gründwald, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Perfidious Eldar! These aliens had the stars in their grasp and now are left to sift the dust of their once fabulous realm. For all their intellect and mysticism they could not contain the beast within them, nor tame the wild monsters of the Shadow. Why should we pay them any heed?
quotes.source: Codex: Eldar (4th Edition), pg. 24 Codex: Eldar (9th Edition), pg. 11

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Nomandes
quotes.text: When the Eldar make their attack, the enemy will already be half-defeated, having spent days or even weeks chasing the shadows of the Rangers.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 320 (UK), pg. 90 - Index Xenos: Eldar Rangers

quotes.speaker: Iradriel,The Mourning Sun Farseer of Alaitoc
quotes.text: Some futures are unavoidable. Anything less, I shall bend to my will.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers,pg. 28

quotes.speaker: Iraelun, Farseer of Alaitoc
quotes.text: The portents are good, the Warp flows strong and pure here. Our sorcery will undo the foul slaves of the dark powers.

quotes.speaker: Isengal, Craftworld Bonesinger
quotes.text: That is a weapon only by virtue of assaulting my senses. It is a misshapen lump of metal, twisted and bent against its will into a disrespectful abomination. I would never wish death at the hands of your Mon-keigh ‘weapons’ upon my greatest rival.

quotes.speaker: Ishtá Indomi, Autarch of Ul-Khari
quotes.text: It is not enough to command the deployment of my own forces. My strategies will instruct the movement of my enemy, or I am not worthy of my title.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers,pg. 33

quotes.speaker: Ishyandra, Warlock of Il-Kaithe
quotes.text: Understanding? What understanding is there to be gleaned by you mon-keighs? You cannot even recall the mistakes your race made that brought you to this precipice, Inquisitor. Divination is not a science you can master with hymns or influence with your ointments and trinkets. It is an artistry woven from what was, what may be, and the lies the beings of the Immaterium speak regarding both. It is a wonder you retain an understanding of fi re. No, better you clutch tight to your primitive baubles and leave the insight to more enlightened races.

quotes.speaker: Iyanna Arienal, Spiritseer,the Angel of Iyanden
quotes.text: It is a simple truth. Only the dead can save the living.

quotes.speaker: Iyanna Arienal, Spiritseer,the Angel of Iyanden
quotes.text: Not even the dead have seen the end of war.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 32

quotes.speaker: Iyanna Arienal, Spiritseer,the Angel of Iyanden
quotes.text: The universe is tripartite: the sunlight of the material plane, the darkness of the spirit plane, and the twilight of the spaces betwixt the two.
quotes.source: Codex: Eldar (4th Edition), pg. 46 Codex: Aeldari (9th Edition), pg. 42

quotes.speaker: Jaden Hoyt, Void Master of the Winterscale’s Lament
quotes.text: Pitiless aliens all. Don’t be fooled by their graceful movements or soft voices; they might look a bit like us but they are every bit as dangerous as an Ork and will kill you just as quick should it suit their purposes.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Koronus Bestiary, pg. 60

quotes.speaker: Jain Zar, Phoenix Lord of the Howling Banshees
quotes.text: I bring only death and leave only corpses.
quotes.source: FFG Teaser for "Against the Great Enemy", supplement for Conquest Card Game (last accessed 25 May 2016)

quotes.speaker: Jain Zar, Phoenix Lord of the Howling Banshees
quotes.text: The doom that devoured our people... She Who Thirsts will never be sated, no matter how many other souls you feed her.
quotes.source: The Art of Warhammer Video Games, pg. 129, Warhammer 40,000: Warpforge

quotes.speaker: Jain Zar, Phoenix Lord of the Howling Banshees
quotes.text: This one speaks with many voices. She is our salvation. Listen well.
quotes.source: Gathering Storm: Fracture of Biel-Tan, pg. 37

quotes.speaker: Karandras
quotes.text: Attack Swiftly and Without Warning.

quotes.speaker: Kaulios, Guardian of Alaitoc at the Assembly of Guided Daggers
quotes.text: From Sha’iel itself are her powers conjured; and into Sha’iel itself would I follow her without complaint.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 31

quotes.speaker: Kelseth the Exodite
quotes.text: The great wheel turns, the cycle of suffering and revelation continues. The past shows us what the future shall be: bitter ashes and broken dreams. We try to mask our nature, we try to control it, hide from it. But in the end our fates are inexorable.

quotes.speaker: Kysaduras the Anchorite, Introspections Upon Perfection
quotes.text: The young do not desire the discipline of the Path, but rather their curiosity drives them to try every fruit from the tree. Thus it is that so many take the Path of Wandering or the Path of Damnation in their first years of adulthood, and so the great tragedy of our kind is played out again and again as the number of our people shrink from generation to generation.

quotes.speaker: Kysaduras the Anchorite, Introspections Upon Perfection
quotes.text: When war calls we all ought to answer. Would that it were not this way, but for our people to survive we must tread the darkest of paths. Yet I regret that many of our kin have grown to see such obligations not as a duty, but a joy. I do note love the blade for its form or its keen edge, but only for that which it defends. Must we pay for existence with our very souls?

quotes.speaker: Laconfir of Biel-Tan
quotes.text: There is no art more beautiful and diverse as the art of death.
quotes.source: Codex: Eldar (4th Edition), pg. 41 Codex: Eldar (3rd Edition),pg. 3

quotes.speaker: Lathriel,High Farseer of Biel-Tan
quotes.text: The Reborn are the only hope to our people. They seek to unite the Aeldari entire

quotes.speaker: Lieutenant Pharaik on Eldar Rangers
quotes.text: We used to think of them as wandering vagrants. Well, those vagrants held up my whole platoon for five days.

quotes.speaker: Liurth, Farseer Lugganath Craftworld
quotes.text: What disasters have they averted? How many Aeldari lives have they saved? How many foes of ours have they doomed to oblivian? Across all the craftworlds we cannot be sure. I know this, however, without a shadow of a doubt. Were it not for the Blades of Khaine, the Asuryani would be no more. The warriors that I have commanded alone have prevented multiple incursions into th webway that would have severed vital ties to Exodite Worlds and Harlequin troupes. I can only imagine what others have done for our people.

quotes.speaker: Llevrien,Aeldari Farseer
quotes.text: They have as much faith in their ignominious weaponry as they do in the shortsighted seer they call Emperor. But they outnumber us ten to one; underestimate them and they will bury you beneath the weight of their own corpses.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 60

quotes.speaker: Lord-Phoenix Dreamspinner, Ghost-Warrior of Iyanden Craftworld
quotes.text: No barrier, no enemy will thwart my purpose. This night I bring him the dream of death!
quotes.source: White Dwarf 101 (UK), pg. 53 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 73)

quotes.speaker: Lord-Phoenix Ironstorm, Spirit-Warrior of the Biel-Tan Craftworld
quotes.text: My only remaining pleasure is to bring death to the enemies of my Craft World.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 101 (UK), pg. 52 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 72)

quotes.speaker: Lord-Phoenix Madallaith, Biel-tan
quotes.text: Make no mistake, Human. We do not fight for your Emperor. We fight against Horus.

quotes.speaker: Loremaster Thielle of Alaitoc
quotes.text: There is a story of a mere handful of Eldar warriors so inspiring and poignant that it has already entered the realm of legend. On the ice field of Atticus, a million-strong swarm of Tyrannies poured down from the ice plains towards the chasms that held the last of the open web way portals. The Rangers of Alaitoc alerted the forces stationed below of their approach, but the perimeter forces left to protect the Farceurs had become so thin that there was no real hope. A few score Dire Avenger Aspect Warriors took up position in the narrowest part of the chasm leading toward the web way gate. As the ice turned dark with the sheer number of vile, scuttling beasts coming for them, the Dire avengers began to Kill. They unleashed storm after storm of sliver-thin blades, and the bodies of the alien killer-beasts piled high. Though they fell, one by one, the Dire Avengers slaughtered thousands of beasts in the space of an hour. The chitinous bodies of the alien organisms became a mountain that the Dire Avengers scaled step by step, raining a curtain of fire down upon those that tried to clamber up the bodies of their fallen kin. The foe was still without number, and here and there an alien talon would bypass the defenses of the Exarch and take another Eldar life. But, as the final Dire Avenger bled their last, the Tyrannies broke into disarray, scattering to the four winds. The only alien taint to reach the chanting Farseeers was the river of stinking, black blood that flowed down the chasm. This day, in the halls of the Craftworlds, the heroes of the Black River are passing into legend. They represent the sacrifice made by all the fallen on Medusa V, and their hard-won prize; the sanctity of our realm

quotes.speaker: Maegrah Worldsinger
quotes.text: We squandered our best chance to rise by hiding so long in the dark. No more, my kin. No more.

quotes.speaker: Maelariothian, Starstorm Duellist
quotes.text: The sooner we leave, the better. The air in this thing the Mon-keigh call a “ship” is vile. The stench already sullies my fine coat.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers,pg. 53

quotes.speaker: Marie Von Xardt, Ordo Xenos Inquisitor
quotes.text: Masters of Warp-witchery, arcane technologists, and deadly — if frail — combatants. Truly we can learn much from these sordid creatures. I’ve no doubt their corpses will be just as revelatory as any conversation.'
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 76

quotes.speaker: Maur Saim-Ingrelli, Striking Scorpians Exarch of the Onyx Claw Shrine, Biel-Tan Craftworld
quotes.text: We Aeldari have fallen far. Those not of our race who have heard our story might even pity us, if they don't hold us in contempt for our own folly. I spit on their scorn. Our time is far from over. We have been masters of war since our enemies' forebearers were but amoebae drifting aimlessly in barren seas. The galaxy once shook in awe upon the mention of our name, and it shall do so again.

quotes.speaker: Mauryon of Biel-tan
quotes.text: There’s only one true path in life – the path that leads to war.

quotes.speaker: Meliniel, Autarch of Biel-Tan
quotes.text: These Ynnari are a curse upon our fractured race, a mockery of our aeldari forebears. How can we return to those days, unite behind the false glamour of a lost supremacy, when the follies of that age were so profound they scarred the universe? We have forged a path that leads away from damnation, tried and true. Those that would lead us back at the behest of a fanatic, a mute and a daemon are so deluded they should be sent to embrace the macabre shadow-god they serve.

quotes.speaker: Message from Biel-tan to remnants of Explorator Fleet over Gavris Minor
quotes.text: The soil of this planet is not for your feet to tread. Only death awaits you here.

quotes.speaker: Message received prior to the Assyri Devastation
quotes.text: We warned you of the price of your actions, now you must pay it in full - in blood.

quotes.speaker: Meyennan, Ul-Khari Guardian Defender
quotes.text: Worry not of the portents — if they come to us, they have chosen death.

quotes.speaker: Mirehn Bielann Farseer
quotes.text: The stars themselves once lived and died at our command, yet you still dare to oppose our will.

quotes.speaker: Naval saying
quotes.text: You may as well try to catch starlight as bring Eldar to battle.

quotes.speaker: Obelius Mung the Fourth, Echoes of Paravax
quotes.text: The Aeldari are a complex and fractured people, more so than even the Mung xenolexicors can comprehend. That said, there are distinct and divergent cultures that have spun outwards from the great cataclysm of the Aeldari. The most far-sighted sought refuge before the Fall, becoming those we call Exodites. As the birth of the Dark Prince came ever closer, the ark-like craftworlds took to the void; many survived the apotheosis that laid the Aeldari low. Others roam the stars as Corsairs, wanderers and Outcasts, or make their lair in dark Commorragh. Amongst them are the savage slavers known as Drukhari, the enigmatic Harlequins of the Black Library, and the Ynnari, who seek to reclaim the glory of ages past.

quotes.speaker: Odinell Ciaradh, Howling Banshee of Biel-Tan Craftworld
quotes.text: It has been our way for millennia to sacrifice our present so that we may have a future. My Banshee sisters and I know that our every battle may be our last. We face that truth without fear, for through our efforts may more of our people live and see brighter times for the Aeldari. One day the star will once more bow to our race, and that outcome will be achieved one battle, one kill at a time.

quotes.speaker: Oelanaris, Spiritseer of Saim-Hann
quotes.text: The stars are aligned and the crimson moon rises. The hour of blood and clashing blades has come. Ride now, Wild Host. Ride forth to glory and to vengeance!'

quotes.speaker: Paean of Ul-Khari
quotes.text: Better a million Humans should perish than a single Aeldari be harmed.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 108

quotes.speaker: Phoenix Lord Maugan Ra
quotes.text: Let not the fear of death stay your hand nor defeat your courage. The warrior who will prevail is the one who conquers death, who becomes one with death as we have.

quotes.speaker: Phoenix Lord Maugan Ra
quotes.text: War is my master; Death my mistress.

quotes.speaker: Prince Yriel, Corsair Prince of the Eldritch Raiders, High Admiral and Autarch of Iyanden
quotes.text: Death eases the spirit.

quotes.speaker: Prince Yriel, Corsair Prince of the Eldritch Raiders, High Admiral and Autarch of Iyanden
quotes.text: The dead must join our ranks, lest we be forced to join theirs.

quotes.speaker: Prince Yriel, Corsair Prince of the Eldritch Raiders, High Admiral and Autarch of Iyanden
quotes.text: We may have won the battle, but our ancestors have lost their souls.
quotes.source: Iyanden: A Codex: Eldar Supplement, pg. 19

quotes.speaker: Prince Yriel, Corsair Prince of the Eldritch Raiders, High Admiral and Autarch of Iyanden
quotes.text: With Ynnead’s grace, we gave the humans their demigod. A king reborn, with a deathly blade, just as the Anchorite’s prophecy foresaw. Will it will buy us enough time to defeat our true foe? That is a truth that even Morai Heg herself could not forsee.
quotes.source: Psychic Awakening: Phoenix Rising, pg. 18 Codex: Aeldari (9th Edition), pg. 88

quotes.speaker: Qelaine Palemoon, jetbike pilot
quotes.text: We are unchained, we go where we wish, and where we desire. The stars are ours, and like them, we will persist. We will not become a memory.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 52

quotes.speaker: Querosq the Lambent, Fire Dragon
quotes.text: Let them gather the wreckage of their precious tank. It would please me to destroy it a second time.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 26

quotes.speaker: Ralamine Mung, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: It is too easy for an Eldar to embrace the obscene virtues of Chaos, for Slaanesh is nothing more than a manifestation of the Eldar mind in its most wild and unconstrained form. Human morality is meaningless to the Eldar, and to the dark side of the Eldar mind all live is to be expended at a whim. Cruelty and generosity are but the impulse of a moment. Beauty and sensuality are virtues that can be expressed in bloodshed just as easily as in song. To an unfettered Eldar mind there is neither sanity nor madness, but merely a wave of perfect existence fulfilled by its own savage momentum.

quotes.speaker: Reflections of a Clouded Eye
quotes.text: Keep me from death. Keep me from the chill of the void. Keep my soul from She Who Thirsts.—Contested translation of the 73rd stanza of Reflections of a Clouded Eye, an epic poem of Aeldari origin

quotes.speaker: Reqhiel of the Sons of Fuegan
quotes.text: We bring only death, and leave only carrion. It is a message even a Human can understand.

quotes.speaker: Sergeant Prtyr Welricht, Ventan XXI Heavy Infantry, Recording of Last Words
quotes.text: They were on us so damned fast we never had a... Colonel Rincards said we'd be fine. But they got him too! Terrible they were, so fast so graceful. Killed everyone, tanks all wrecked by their guns. Tried to hold the line. We tried to hold the line...but they came with their shrieking faces and their swords, killing and shrieking and killing...
quotes.source: White Dwarf 302 (UK), pg. 65 - Index Xenos: Swordwind

quotes.speaker: Shadow Lord Arain
quotes.text: We should have exterminated you before you polluted the stars with your presence. (on Humans)

quotes.speaker: Skaelow the Crafter, Guardian Starcannon operator
quotes.text: I once gave my heart to creation; the beauty of days before. Now my art is the flash of a starcannon, my music the fury of war.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 22

quotes.speaker: Skalagrim Phar the Twice-Damned
quotes.text: Never trust the knife-ears. They are lies made flesh. Worse than Neverborn.

quotes.speaker: Skariel the Huntress, Striking Scorpion
quotes.text: Patience is a virtue duly rewarded; now is the time of blood. Slaughter them all, in Khaine’s name!
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 28

quotes.speaker: Spiritseer Baluithe of Craftworld Kaelor
quotes.text: A warrior may become skilled with a blade as he wishes, but he is still subject to the fickle whim of his destiny, and his doom. We who walk the Path of the Seer take destiny into our own hands, and wield doom as our blade.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Navis Primer, pg. 120

quotes.speaker: Swooping Hawk blessing
quotes.text: May your wings be strong and the winds of fate blow ever in your direction.

quotes.speaker: Taldar Asuleth
quotes.text: To wander the stars without destination is to fall to the deepest agonies. The light of each moment is too fleeting, too fragile a thing to discern purpose from.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook, pg. 363 - Ranger

quotes.speaker: The Lament of Ba've Matha
quotes.text: The Skies will weep with the ash of your kind's passing, and none but the dust shall remember.

quotes.speaker: The Visarch
quotes.text: In Ynnead is our final hope. Of this I am certain. For countless years did I fight for the resurgence of our race as a scion of Biel-Tan, and never in all my years was progress made. Yet with the God of the Dead, we can halt out extinction.

quotes.speaker: The Visarch
quotes.text: The tapestry of fate has been wrenched, torn, and unravelled, just as when Dariachna tore at her woven masterwork in a fit of madness. The question remains – in such times of upheaval and unpredictability, can new strands of hope be found?

quotes.speaker: Theuria of Ulthwé
quotes.text: The Rhana Dandra. The end of our race. How long have we said it is inevitable? Every prophecy points to its culmination. Yet can it not be postponed? And is our fatalistic acceptance of our destruction the very thing that will bring it about?

quotes.speaker: Thirianna,of Craftworld Alaitoc
quotes.text: Where do you put that rage you feel when someone angers you? What do you do with the hatred that burns inside when you think upon all that we have lost? You have not learnt to control these feelings, merely ignore them. Becoming one with Khaine, assuming one of His Aspects is not about confronting an enemy, it is about confronting ourselves. We should all do it at some time in our lives.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 23

quotes.speaker: Tishriel Ysbwrieli, Dark Reapers Exarch of the Shrine of the Twilight Moon
quotes.text: Our rune symbolises the scythe that harvests the living. We invert the triangular warrior rune, ensuring that what is associated with us is morbidity, merciless intent and unconventional talent.Our baleful gaze haunts the battlefield from afar, and death blooms whereever our scrutiny falls — no matter the apparent strength, speed or endurance of our victims.

quotes.speaker: Triesce, Ranger of Craftworld Ul-Khari
quotes.text: The home I dreamed of returning to is a broken, frozen ruin. What good fortune, then, that I have a century’s experience exploring broken, frozen ruins.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 23

quotes.speaker: Unknown Warlock, shortly before his death
quotes.text: On this twisted, trying path, I have learned to see the skeins of the future and walk towards the fate I choose. And yet, now that I find my decisions are my own, I discover that I have no choice. There is only one course. I am a warrior, so let the battle be joined.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Navis Primer, pg. 120

quotes.speaker: Ven'reshac Dhalamemnos, Autarch of Yme-Loc
quotes.text: Speak not to me of the Ynnari. They are a blight upon our society, and they bring with them only strife. Where did they come from? Are they not some Drukhari ploy, sent to sow division and tear us apart from within? Even if there is truth in their claims of union, they would have us die to achieve it. What good is victory in extinction, but for the cause of spite alone?

quotes.speaker: Warhammer 40,000: Conquest, FFG Card Foresight
quotes.text: I have seen the infinite possibilities of the future, their twisting paths and hidden truths.

quotes.speaker: Warlock Lorith of Alaitoc
quotes.text: Eradicate them all, we can no longer tolerate their filthy presence here.

quotes.speaker: Warlock Sabria Vayalin of Craftworld Ul-Khari
quotes.text: As ever, it falls to us to instruct the ignorant rabble. Very well. But teach them the name of death with Falcon’s swiftness, my kin. We must be on our way; we race against Fate.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory: Church of Steel, pg. 92

quotes.speaker: Ynntellien, Ul-Khari Ranger
quotes.text: That ludicrous mon-keigh offered eight hundred cubits of salted grox meat in exchange for five of our Spirit Stones... truly their audacity knows no bounds. How could they have known we were so short of sustenance?
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 66

quotes.speaker: Yrlla Starchild, Ul-Khari Bonesinger
quotes.text: I can say with neither pride nor falsehood that this is the finest blade I have ever created. Beautiful as Isha, unyielding as Asuryan, keen as Khaine’s murderous intent. What tragic irony, that something so fine must be stained with Mon-keigh blood. That it must be wielded in battle at all is a crime for which they must pay.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 77

quotes.speaker: Yrule, Born of Twilight, Warp Spider
quotes.text: I have trespassed in the realm of daemons and seen the things within. You cannot fathom the price we pay to deliver death to our foes.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 30

quotes.speaker: Yvraine,Emissary of Ynnead
quotes.text: The Dark Gods rise. We must rise higher, the better to cast them down.

quotes.speaker: Yvraine,Emissary of Ynnead
quotes.text: The Whispering God gives new life, just as he takes it away.
quotes.source: White Dwarf May 2019, pg. 24 Gathering Storm: Fracture of Biel- Tan, pg. 85

quotes.speaker: Yvraine,Emissary of Ynnead
quotes.text: We Aeldari are being tested, as the blade Asha'qui was tested beneath Vaul's anvil. Will we break? I think not. From this time of psychic trauma, we will emerge stronger than ever before- In regard to the events of the Psychic Awakening

quotes.speaker: Yvraine,Emissary of Ynnead
quotes.text: We once risked our sanity to rule the stars, and paid the price. Now we risk our lives, our homes, even our souls. But the phoenix will rise from the flames, of this I am certain. What sacrifice cannot be borne in the name of redemption?
quotes.source: Psychic Awakening: Phoenix Rising, pg. 64 Codex: Aeldari (9th Edition), pg. 53

quotes.speaker: Zaire Obasi Varonius, Rogue Trader
quotes.text: Nah, they never drop that pompous air of smug superiority. And they won’t sell those gorgeous stones for anything — almost as if their life depends on them. But spend a decade or five convincing them you’re honest, and show them a stunning Death World vista, then you’ve got a lethal companion that’ll follow you to the edges of unknown space.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 76

quotes.speaker: " Stitcher" Kristan, Kasrkin 27th, Fire-team Gamma
quotes.text: Would you rather we all die? That we wait for the planet to break apart under our feet? We’ve stood long enough, the frekking planet broke before we did.
quotes.source: Fall of Cadia (Novel), Phase 7 Evacuation, Part 1

quotes.speaker: "Trench Head” Jones
quotes.text: Life is war. And I’m gonna win!

quotes.speaker: (no origin given)
quotes.text: Cadia broke before the Guard did.
quotes.source: Battlefleet Gothic: Armada II Launch Trailer - 2019

quotes.speaker: Acting Sgt. Valimes Lox, 87th Kantarri Rifles
quotes.text: The Ganthe Ridges cost us many lives. The dead rotted in the insufferable heat as insets buried themselves into the spoiling flesh. but with each furious assault and desperate defence, we learned more. When we finally planted the standard at the peak, we were the finest troops in the regiment, renowned.

quotes.speaker: Anonymous
quotes.text: Any Cadian who can't field-strip his own lasgun by age ten was born on the wrong planet.

quotes.speaker: Anonymous
quotes.text: The duty of a Commander is to judge what means should be undertaken to achieve each objective. He must be aware of what is to be gained and what may be lost. A commander who places his troops in a position where he may likely loose more than he may likely gain risks more than the lives of his men. He risks far more; he risks failure. Loss is acceptable, failure is not.

quotes.speaker: Arienne Wcojevich, Acolyte of Inquisitor Tytrona Dikaisune
quotes.text: I quivered for but a moment in the face of that daemon, calling on the Emperor for aid. Before I could open fire, that veteran was already making his charge. What kind of war did you pull this dog from?
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 60

quotes.speaker: attributed to Colonel Kaldenbach’s commissars, facing the Blood Pact at the Civitas Beati, Herodor, 773. M41
quotes.text: If you are the last man standing, you’re not fighting hard enough.

quotes.speaker: Ayla Hartin,77th Gilead Gravediggers
quotes.text: Father Yakob stood on the front lines bolder than any of us, spitting oratory as vicious and beautiful as las blasts. He gave us hope when we faced those horrors, and led our charge with only a holy book in his hand! I’ll certainly miss him on the next assault.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 58

quotes.speaker: Bone’ead Kopp, Ogryn tactician
quotes.text: Der mishunn is to run across dat field and make all dem green fings dead. Any kweschuns?
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory: Forsaken System Player's Guide,pg. 115

quotes.speaker: Captain Al'rahem, Tallarn 3rd 'Desert Tigers'
quotes.text: Be swift and silent - as the breeze that crosses the dunes without stirring a grain of sand.

quotes.speaker: Captain Forgus Merd
quotes.text: I'd check my instruments if I were you. No-one in his right mind would lay a minefield in a place like...

quotes.speaker: Captain Rock of the Catachan III 'Green Devils'
quotes.text: We've run into scorpions the size of battle tanks, three men died from Eyerot last week. I've sweat enough to fill a lake, my boots got sucked into a sink-swamp and the trees are so thick in places, you can't squeeze between them. Emperor help me, I love this place! It's just like home!

quotes.speaker: Captain Shambas, Valhallan 597th
quotes.text: I love the smell of promethium in the morning.
quotes.source: For The Emperor (Novel)– Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), pg. 212 (Editorial Note following ch. 12)

quotes.speaker: Catechism of the Founding, Gunmetallicus 104th
quotes.text: Your home is no longer a place, but a people. The Imperium is yours, and you are the Imperium’s.”

quotes.speaker: Colonel Aram Shayan, Tallarn military philosopher
quotes.text: Do not ask how you may give your life for the Emperor. Ask instead how you may give your death.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Aram Shayan, Tallarn military philosopher
quotes.text: In war, best know what enemies are around you in your camp, before you step out to face the foe and wonder why you do so alone.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Aram Shayan, Tallarn military philosopher
quotes.text: The beati was a warrior. She exemplifies the Imperial creed, and the human spirit better than any figure in the annals. As a boy, she inspired me. I take this crusade as a personal matter, a duty greater than any I have yet undertaken for the Golden Throne. To repay her inspiration, to walk in her path and make free again the worlds she brought from darkness. I feel as if I am... a pilgrim, Ibram.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Aram Shayan, Tallarn military philosopher
quotes.text: The desert, from a distance, can seem to be little more than a barren waste, devoid of feature or variation. We who know the desert can see past this façade, for the dunes can conceal much of significance. Like the sandstorm, we can rise up swiftly to ravage our foes, and vanish as swiftly, leaving no trace of our presence but the damage wrought. As the burning sun and open sky, to walk before us unprepared is to invite doom. We are the desert, and the desert is merciless.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Aram Shayan, Tallarn military philosopher
quotes.text: We have been given our crusade, my sons... the Sabbat Worlds!

quotes.speaker: Colonel Jago Kallowen
quotes.text: Of course they’d come to Mordant for the saviors of the galaxy! We’re the hardest gakking troopers you’ll find in all the Segmentums! I’m proud to lead these warriors of the Emperor where ever he needs us to fight. We’ll stand fast against the myriad of foes that batter at the gates of the Imperium. I’ve scoured the mines and shanty-towns for the fiercest low-lives I could find. As I speak to you now, my men are already suffering through the most rigorous boot camp you could imagine. No soldier of the Lucky 13s will be grist for the mill.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Joachim Pfeiff, Krieg 14th Armoured Regiment
quotes.text: Your foe is well equipped, well-trained, battle-hardened. He believes his gods are on his side. Let him believe what he will. We have the tanks on ours.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour - Imperial Vehicles for Warhammer 40,000, pg. 31

quotes.speaker: Colonel Johanna van Meer, Elysian 13th "Helldivers" Drop Troops
quotes.text: The enemy commanders think themselves safe from the Emperor's Wrath behind kilometres of fortifications and legions of warriors. They did not account for us. Make your peace with the Emperor, men — we drop in two minutes.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Kostan Drescher, Mordian 18th Iron Guard
quotes.text: No remorse, no mercy, no forgiveness. Not a single step back, not a single moment of hesitation. You will not succumb to fear or doubt, and you will relent only after you have given your last moment for the Emperor. Nothing less than this will be tolerated.

quotes.speaker: Colonel Marus Cullen, Pannonia 5th Infantry Regiment
quotes.text: Children you call them? They can pull a trigger just as well as veterans, and they have the spirit of a bull narthax. Call them children if you wish - I call them troops.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 111 (UK), pg. 16 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 41)’’

quotes.speaker: Colonel Pannet, on Catachan Warriors
quotes.text: As swift as a lash mamba, as sharp as a spineleaf, as stubborn as a wild grox.'
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Talisman of Dominance

quotes.speaker: Colonel Rorkins, Imperial Guard(retired)
quotes.text: Sir, will do. I've never liked that particular title. It seems to foster unhealthy ambitions.
quotes.source: Cain's Last Stand (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus), ch. 9, pg. 605

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: Choose, for courtesy's sake, summary execution or a firing squad of your own men.

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: Give any man the power of a god, and you better hope he's got the wisdom and morals of a god to match. There's nothing feeble about my moral line. I value life. That is why I fight to protect it. I mourn every man I lose and every sacrifice I make. One life or a billion, they're all lives.

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: I am a Commissar. I am empowered to deliver justice wher ever I see it lacking. I am empowered to punish cowardice. I am granted the gift of total authority to judge, in the name of the Emperor, on the field of combat.

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: I don't pretend to understand the technology, but I know that's an intact Standard Template weapons maker. And I know that's as unheard of as a well-manicured ork.
quotes.source: First and Only (Novel),Part Six: Menazoid Epsilon, ch. 23

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: I have a reputation, Modile, a reputation as a fair, honest man who treats his soldiers well and supports them in the face of darkness. Potentially, that reputation makes me soft. It seems I understand failure and forgive it. Some, like Kowle, believe me to be a weak commissar, not prepared to take the action my rank demands. Not prepared to enforce field discipline where I see it failing. I am an Imperial commissar. I will enflame the weak, support the wavering, guide the lost. I will be all things to all men who need me. But I will also punish without hesitation the incompetent, the cowardly, and the treasonous.

quotes.speaker: Colonel-Commissar Ibram Gaunt, Tanith First and Only
quotes.text: Men of Tanith! Do you want to live forever?
quotes.source: First and Only (Novel),Part Two: Fortis Binary Forge World, ch. 3

quotes.speaker: Colonel"Iron Hand" Straken, Catachan II
quotes.text: Damn it, follow me. I'll show you how it's done.

quotes.speaker: Commander Fleyitch
quotes.text: Ogryns. If we time it just right, the Eldar will all be going "What the..." just when what's left of the Company breaks cover over here and piles into them. No spreading out, no fancy stuff, just smash through. Let's see how those degenerate sophisticates handle a healthy dose of pure unreasoning violence.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 110 (UK), pg. 11 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 48)’’

quotes.speaker: Commander Rango Thaxx
quotes.text: Walls of stone do not a fortress make. But they're not a bad start.

quotes.speaker: Commander Tulathan, Vastadt 4th Independent Tank Regiment
quotes.text: It is always better to find your enemy, before your enemy finds you.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour - Imperial Vehicles for Warhammer 40,000, pg. 27

quotes.speaker: Commissar Anton Gebbet, 1st Kronus Regiment
quotes.text: If you will not serve in combat, then you will serve on the firing line.
quotes.source: Dawn of War: Dark Crusade Classics

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: As I try to impress on the young whelps in my charge these days, it isn't the scarlet sash and the fancy hat that makes you a commissar, it's the way you wear them. The troops you serve with are never going to like you, but if you can get them to respect you that can be almost as good. Remember, you're going to spend most of your career on a battlefield with them, and they've all got guns, so making them think you're a liability is never going to be a very good idea.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: Frak this, for my faith is a shield proof against your blandishments!

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: Frak this! My soul's my own and I'm keeping it!

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: I thought the acolytes of Khorne were supposed to be warriors, not a bunch of pansies.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: I'd faced the servants of the Great Enemy more often than I cared to contemplate, and in my experience it was never wise to underestimate them. Particularly a ten thousand year old madman, who'd been marinating himself in the raw stuff of Chaos for most of that time.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: I've come across a number of planetary governors in the course of my erratic progress around the galaxy and, on the whole, the ones who weren't incompetent were either corrupt, insane, or both; on at least one occasion sufficiently so to have mortgaged their soul and the world they administered to the forces of the Ruinous Powers.
quotes.source: Traitor's Gambit (Chapbook) UK Games Day 2009, pg. 6

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: Sorry. I prefer blondes.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: The noncom in charge of the squad must have been on something, I thought. Nobody spoke like that outside badly-written combat novels.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: Well then, I seem to recall a rather pleasant little restaurant not far from here. Care to see if it's still standing?

quotes.speaker: Commissar Exen Treuer, 23rd Moradia Regiment
quotes.text: A small mind is easily filled with faith.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 110 (UK), pg. 13 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 50)’’

quotes.speaker: Commissar Fenstrum Holt
quotes.text: Those men, whose lives you spent with such cavalier disregard - those men were Imperial Guard. They were Munitorum property. They belonged to the Segmentum Command. They belonged to the Emperor.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Groden Bevro
quotes.text: Blessed is the mind too small for doubt.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 110 (UK), pg. 12 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 49)’’

quotes.speaker: Commissar Holt
quotes.text: A finely crafted weapon is like a finely-crafted battle plan. Both will serve you well.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Holt
quotes.text: No foe is more pernicious when dug-in than the forces of the Archenemy.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Holt
quotes.text: No one man, however exalted or base, is more important than the mission.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Holt
quotes.text: The coward represents as great a threat as the traitor. The traitor at least stands by his convictions.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Konrad
quotes.text: The flames of hatred rage across the galaxy. Like a forest, humanity stands before it all but ready to be consumed. Despite the darkness that has cut our galaxy in twain, we must find unity in faith, and stand against those that would destroy us, our weapons roaring our defiance. We will cross the scar of chaos and reunite humanity step by bloody step.Commissar Konrad at the launch of the Indomitus Crusade

quotes.speaker: Commissar Krieglust
quotes.text: Weep for him - for his faith was not sufficient.Rejoice for yourselves, for my faith is bottomless!Forward, for the Emperor!

quotes.speaker: Commissar Otto Xavier
quotes.text: ''Who told you to die? Keep fighting!

quotes.speaker: Commissar Sebastian Yarrick
quotes.text: Heroes of Armageddon! You have withstood the evil savagery of the Orks, and they have nothing left for you to fear. So raise high the black banners of vengeance - now is our time.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Sebastian Yarrick
quotes.text: There will be no retreat from Hades Hive. We will fight to the end.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Sebastian Yarrick
quotes.text: When the door drops, give them a taste of the Emperor's fire!
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Deploy to the Front

quotes.speaker: Commissar Tona Ursakar
quotes.text: I’ve seen her lift a Rhino with a gesture, and turn an Ork inside-out with a look. She’s far more than a soldier — that’s why I keep this pistol trained on the back of her skull. Just in case.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 53

quotes.speaker: Commissar Tyreen, Versimmon Dragoons
quotes.text: All men need something to fight for, a cause to believe in. Tell them that the Emperor needs them to hold that ground at all costs, and they’ll fight like lions.

quotes.speaker: Commissar-General Delane Oktar
quotes.text: Always, always lead from the front. Never expect a man under your command to undertake an action you're not prepared to make yourself.

quotes.speaker: Commissar-General Delane Oktar
quotes.text: The first trick a political officer of the Commissariat learns is: learn to lie. The second is: trust no one. The third: never get involved with local politics.

quotes.speaker: Commissar-General Delane Oktar
quotes.text: Winning is everything, but the trick is to know where the winning really is.... We're political animals, Ibram. Through us, if we do our job properly, the black and white of war is tempered. We are the interpreters of combat, the translators. We give meaning to war, subtlety, purpose even. Killing is the most abhorrent, mindless profession known to man. Our role is to fashion the killing machine of the human species into a positive force. For the Emperor's sake. For the sake of our own consciences.
quotes.source: First and Only (Novel)- Gaunt's Ghosts: The Founding (Omnibus),A Memory: Darendara, pg. 233

quotes.speaker: Common saying amongst the Havarth Heavy Infantry
quotes.text: Emperor protect my soul, flak protect the rest.
quotes.source: Dark Heresy Second Edition Core Rulebook,pg. 167

quotes.speaker: Corporal Webb, Cadian XIV Shocktroopers
quotes.text: I don’t get it. How does a tank that big just disappear? What sorcery can do... Holy Throne! Heavy weapons, right flank, right flank! Fire now.

quotes.speaker: Dravin Gratz, 14th Tharinga Regiment, Imperial Guard
quotes.text: I have seen war in all its forms. I have seen feral world savages braining each other with stones, and I have monitored the death of a whole planet at the hands of a virus bomb. I have seen Space Marines drop to certain death, and win. I have seen Titans crush whole platoons underfoot. But there is no more stirring sight in war than the charge of massed cavalry.

quotes.speaker: Field-Commandant Hayshal Ferrindon, Supreme Commander of Kusolst Defence Regiments
quotes.text: Such a burden rests upon our shoulders! How many worlds after ours will be consumed if we fail? How many billions lost? Of all the planets in all the systems that border the Octarian Empire, the xenos hordes had to fall upon our world, our Kusolst Prime.
