quotes.text: Now, my ascension is complete. Feeble flesh has been cast out, and in its place is power manifest. Let me show you.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 118

quotes.speaker: Mortarion, Daemon Primarch
quotes.text: Hold your bitterness deep within, and there let it fester. Let it roil and squirm and churn, until you are filled with bile so poisonous that all you touch falls to ruin.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Bile Blight

quotes.speaker: Musim, Advisor to Arn-Ord the Merciless
quotes.text: Those who ignore the blessings of the gods of the warp are doomed to suffer at their hands.

quotes.speaker: Noise Marines
quotes.text: Altus Nox, Word Bearers Traitor Legion

quotes.speaker: Noise Marines
quotes.text: Lyrics.com: D-Rok - Noise Marines(last accessed 13 December 2022)

quotes.speaker: Oath of the Malignant Order
quotes.text: Only in the corruption of decay can we return to the purity from which life sprang.

quotes.speaker: Octavius Kohl,Battlelord
quotes.text: It is not enough to defeat your foe. His people must be ravaged, his homeland laid waste, and his reputation laid bare. Only then will your name be known as a true servant of the gods.

quotes.speaker: P'tor Hesh, Traitor Flenser
quotes.text: Blood and skulls for the Red Hound! Rot and ruin for Father Filth! Lies and cunning for the Shadow in the Eye! Always more for Lady Plenty! Tribute, brothers and sisters! Give tribute!

quotes.speaker: Pater Michail Gethsame
quotes.text: The warp... It is not like real space. Some of my order think of its tides and currents, its very substance, as a reflection of our emotions. It is a metaphor, they say, but I am not so sure. Where strong enough emotions gather, they can… well, they can coalesce.
quotes.source: The Woman in the Walls (Short Story)- The Wicked and the Damned (Anthology)

quotes.speaker: Raceen D’Uborll,Despot of Ardenz IV
quotes.text: To watch their faces as their bullets fl icker and fall at my feet is to also watch the faith in their corpse-god fall.

quotes.speaker: Renegade Harkan Ironfist
quotes.text: I salute you! For though our path has been long and bloody, you have served our Lord with unflinching courage and the honour of true warriors. We have seen many fall today and must remember, even as we die, that our blood too is welcome...
quotes.source: Realm of Chaos: Slaves to Darkness, pg. 234 Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition),pg. 138

quotes.speaker: Rorath'rath the Skullweaver
quotes.text: Slay without pity; triumph without remorse. You are the legions of Khorne, His favourite warriors. You shall bring defeat and death to His enemies. You shall crush.
quotes.source: Wrath and Glory Core Rulebook, pg. 346 Bloodletter

quotes.speaker: Sanctifier LeVay, Cult of the Sacred Union
quotes.text: Embrace your hunger, your lust, your desire. The universe is ours for the taking!
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000,Talisman of Indulgence Black Crusade Core Rulebook,pg. 86

quotes.speaker: Sanctifier LeVay, Cult of the Sacred Union
quotes.text: Plumb the depths of the warp, embrace its madness, and show its glory to those who fear it.

quotes.speaker: Saqqara Ur-Damak Thresh, Word Bearers
quotes.text: Of course. One must have a bellyful of spite, to contend with the Neverborn. A heart full of spite, a mind full of hatred and a will of iron. These are the three qualities outlined by Lord Erebus in his seminal treatise...

quotes.speaker: Savona,of the 12th Millennial
quotes.text: He is one with the Dark Prince. His thoughts are Slaanesh’s thoughts. His deeds, Slaanesh’s deeds. He is a sliver of a god, wearing a man’s face.

quotes.speaker: Sektoth, the False Whisperer
quotes.text: The only lie is that there has ever been anything but Chaos. The only ones who have misled you are those who sent you here to die for a cause you cannot understand. But I shall grant you a new purpose. Accept your deaths as the will of the Ruinous Ones and I shall open your eyes the true nature of this universe.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 112

quotes.speaker: Seriadne Hekht, Traitor General, Styxx 777th
quotes.text: I gave the God-Emperor my blood. He rewarded me with suffering, and misery, and with comrades turned corpses. I spit on him. He is a parasite, a fatted leech that will drain humanity dry and still expect their fawning gratitude. But he will never taste my veins again. I have new masters now, and for them I will spill the blood of the faithful fools until the monster on the Golden Throne starves!

quotes.speaker: Seventh Tenant of the Blood Child
quotes.text: Strength is an illusion conjured by man’s cunning, the weapons he wields and his willingness to use them to tear the faces from his foes.

quotes.speaker: Sha'ris, Deacon of Lust
quotes.text: I can show you indulgence without regret, delight without boundaries, gratifications beyond count.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000,Herald of Slaanesh

quotes.speaker: Sindri Myr, Alpha Legion Sorcerer
quotes.text: All power demands sacrifice... and pain. The universe rewards those willing to spill their life's blood for the promise of power.
quotes.source: Dawn of War,Mission 9 (Single player campaign) Classics

quotes.speaker: Skalagrim Phar the Twice-Damned
quotes.text: A blind man could see it. Oh, he boasts of his New Men – his apex beasts – and how they will replace us and the mortals both, but for all his talk of dying with dignity, he has no intention of doing so. You’re a hypocrite, Fabius. And I grow tired of humouring you.

quotes.speaker: Skaveth Merenkar, herald of Yrukhal
quotes.text: 'The ready spoils in The Maelstrom lure many a rogue to piracy. Those who seek to survive soon learn their place.
quotes.source: The Maelstrom: Lair of the Tyrant, Lair of the Tyrant, pg. 24

quotes.speaker: Skyrar,Master of the Thirst for Vengeance
quotes.text: Join me, brothers, and we will make the minions of the undying Emperor pay for the lies and betrayal they have dealt us!

quotes.speaker: Sloughskins, Plagebearers Legion
quotes.text: Bursting with life. Bursting with life.
quotes.source: Conquest 17,Invaders of Ultramar - Forces of Chaos,pg. 8

quotes.speaker: Solomon Akurra, Alpha Legion
quotes.text: There is a phenomenon known as “transhuman dread”, where mortals are overcome by fear in the presence of a warrior of the Astartes. I sometimes regret that the effect wears off with time.

quotes.speaker: Soloria Half-Blind of Furia
quotes.text: To become strong, you must be eternally tested.

quotes.speaker: Sorot Tchure, Captain of the Word Bearers Legion
quotes.text: For years I have despised Lorgar... look at your primarch, Honorius. So singular in aspect. So noble. I have envied you, envied the Imperial Fists, the Luna Wolves, the Iron Hands. And I am not alone. We struggle with a mercurial mind, Honorius. We labour under the burden of a brilliant but fallible commander. We no longer bear the word, my friend. We bear Lorgar.
quotes.source: Know No Fear (Novel),Target//Acquisition, ch. Eight, pg. 101

quotes.speaker: Svane Vulfbad
quotes.text: I murdered thousands for the Emperor and he gave me nothing except his damning silence. Now his lapdogs yap for every life I take, while the gods promise me the galaxy.
quotes.source: Shadow War: Armageddon rulebook, pg. 126 Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition),pg. 156

quotes.speaker: Talasta Veletin,First Lady of the Gilded House
quotes.text: Remember this: our alliances were never more than skin-thin. By essence. By design. See, you disagreed with yourselves – we knew that. The Martians feuding with the Terrans, the Astartes at odds with the Custodes, on it went. But, as pressure was applied, you were able to put those divisions aside, at least for a while. We, on the other hand – well, division was our business. Our creed, you might say. Only Lupercal held it together, and by the end even he was failing. It was all about timing – we had to win before we turned on ourselves. And when we ran out of time, oh, then it was bloody. We killed more of our own than you did, I reckon. And what made it worse was that we knew so little about anything. We knew Horus was gone, but we didn’t know how, or why. We had no one to blame, no reasons to grip on to. So we looked around us, and only saw our own kind, all with their blades drawn. A miracle any of us made it out, you might think. But some did. You’ll have to kill them all now, won’t you? Or, one day – imagine it – they might come back.

quotes.speaker: Talasta Veletin,First Lady of the Gilded House
quotes.text: True power is not what you can do for yourself but what your minions can do for you.

quotes.speaker: Talien Meers, Traitor Legionnaire
quotes.text: My reward exists nowhere on this mortal plane. Instead, my everlasting reward lies in the warp.

quotes.speaker: Taln’ar of the Night Lords
quotes.text: My flesh for your power. My soul for your strength.

quotes.speaker: Talos Valcoran
quotes.text: The warp is not sentient, neither is it malicious. It simply is, and it responds to human emotion. Most of all, it responds to suffering, to fear, to hatred, for in such moments humanity is at its strongest and most honest. Suffering colours the warp, and the suffering of psychic souls is like a beacon. Your Emperor uses that suffering as fuel for his Golden Throne, to project the Astronomican.

quotes.speaker: Teachings of Gaiak Krustellam, Dark Magos
quotes.text: The revelation of spirit when encountering the power of Chaos is as freeing for the machine as it is for the mortal mind. It is our duty to create this blessed state of union.
quotes.source: Codex: Chaos Space Marines (4th Edition),pg. 44 Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition),pg. 151

quotes.speaker: Thalien Hax, Charm-master of the Reaving Triad
quotes.text: A silver tongue is often a better solution to a problem than a strong arm.

quotes.speaker: The Book of Magnus
quotes.text: Without the light of Chaos, the universe would stagnate and collapse. Only through this struggle, can any advancement occur.

quotes.speaker: The Book of Tzeentch
quotes.text: It is my cry that wakes you in the night, and my body that crouches in the shadow. I am Tzeentch and you are the puppet that dances to my tune.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000,Talisman of Creativity

quotes.speaker: The Newborn of the Iron Warriors
quotes.text: Without him I would not exist. Without his genetics I would have been one of the Unfleshed, an aborted monstrosity left to die on Medrengard.

quotes.speaker: The Nine Heads of Nurgle
quotes.text: Let us play hide and slay!

quotes.speaker: The Prophecy of Ruin
quotes.text: Only the strongest shall arise to lead the Black Crusade.

quotes.speaker: The Tome of Blood
quotes.text: Servants of the Lord of Slaughter stood from horizon to horizon, filling the air with their shrieks, gibbers and howls of blood. Their battle banners strained in the coppery wind that blew across the ranks. It was a banner of deepest, darkest red, with but a single rune and a legend of simple devotion: Blood for the Blood God. At some secret signal all fell silent and then came a single shriek of dark and bloody loyalty, a pact of hate and death. It echoed from leathered skins, and grew to shake the clouds... and far above the daemons’ ranks, there was an answering roar of bloody approval, torn from Khorne’s brazen throat.

quotes.speaker: Torestus,The birth of Tor,author unknown
quotes.text: And so they descended, the Daemon-Gods. They gathered warped metals from the battlefields of Nissen with which to shape his flesh. They procured flame from the funerary pyre to give him life. But, in their infinite wisdom, they gathered the fruit of the Tree of Demitre, that which all man covets, and placed it where otherwise would be a heart, that he may desire dominion over the souls of man...
quotes.source: Black Crusade Core Rulebook,pg. 376,Chapter XII: False Prophets

quotes.speaker: Typhus the Traveller, Herald of Nurgle
quotes.text: I shall reap a terrible bounty from the death that I sow in your name, Father Nurgle...

quotes.speaker: Typhus the Traveller, Herald of Nurgle
quotes.text: What is a plague, but the cleansing flame in another form? Only the strong survive its passing. Only those fit to claim a kingdom from the ashes.

quotes.speaker: Tyrell, Renegade Lord of Arden IX
quotes.text: Take care, lest your protests grow tiresome. I have asked for so little! Anyone would think that I have asked you to sacrifice yourselves and your sons! And yet, in Slaanesh's boundless and pleasing mercy, I have asked only for your daughters. Surely you would not deny me my small enjoyments?
quotes.source: Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition), pg. 52

quotes.speaker: Tyzek V’th, Changebringer of Tzeentch
quotes.text: I am many made one.
quotes.source: Wrath and Glory Core Rulebook, pg. 348 Pink Horror

quotes.speaker: Ulkair, Great Unclean One
quotes.text: There, feel the glory of necrosis, and rejoice! Nurgle loves you!
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II - Retribution Classics

quotes.speaker: Ungrol Onceman, Beastman Shaman of the Rustpelts
quotes.text: Pile the offerings high! Make a tower of the bones, the skulls and the weapons of the defeated blasphemers! Show the Gods who you have killed for them, who you have butchered and punished in their name. Roar your praises to the Gods for giving us the victory. Once that is done, bring me our prisoners. Their blood will be our greatest gift to the Gods.

quotes.speaker: Urgloth Rotheart, Plague Champion of the Death Guard
quotes.text: Sickness, disease, plague and pox, suffering and the slow, living rot. Such wondrous gifts does Nurgle seek to bestow upon the unworthy human cattle of the Imperium. We are merely the vectors by which his virulent beneficence may be spread to the undeserving masses.
quotes.source: Dark Imperium (Game), Death Guard Booklet, pg. 11 Warhammer 40,000 8th Edition Rulebook,pg. 189

quotes.speaker: Valash Hex, Arch Heretic of the Scarlet Intent
quotes.text: Human minds are weak and feeble—is it any wonder then that the Warp confounds and corrupts them?

quotes.speaker: Valdrekk Elias, Dark Apostle of the Word Bearers
quotes.text: When brother fights brother, it is called rivalry. When brother kills brother, it is called succession.

quotes.speaker: Vandar Herroth,the Architect of Blasphemy
quotes.text: The grand horror of the warp touches many minds; it is not merely the mind of the witch that can delve into the depths of the Immaterium. Through grand rituals and arcane rites, the devoted can speak to the Gods and beseech them for favour.

quotes.speaker: Vashtorr the Arkifane
quotes.text: Alas that your erasure must I engineer herein this place, for you are now but grit in the flywheels to be whisked away. Machines have you made for such purpose as I cannot condone.And yet, uses I shall find for your nodal matrix, little king. Lengths yet are there to which you have not been driven. Mayhap I shall wield the goad.-Vashtorr talking to himself after his victory over the Skahren System.

quotes.speaker: Vashtorr the Arkifane
quotes.text: Danger is a downpayment on success.

quotes.speaker: Vashtorr the Arkifane
quotes.text: There is only one price, and the price is always the same. The price is souls, the souls of machines or the souls of men, I care not. Souls are the currency of power, and I deserve all the riches this bountiful universe has to offer. Collect them for me, and you shall prosper. For souls you shall have armour, and machines of war, and more allies to swell the pitiful forces you offer me.

quotes.speaker: Vexal Tav, Wyrd
quotes.text: The creatures I have seen are glorious and vile in their own measure. Their form is a perfect vision of the Gods’ dark will. None can match the majesty of these vicious beasts.

quotes.speaker: Vhostokh, Warpsmith, Scourge of Diesos
quotes.text: Daemonic fury, bound into the shell of a metal beast and set loose upon the galaxy. Tell me, mortal – have you ever seen such a glorious sight?-about the Defiler
quotes.source: Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition), pg. 77, Defilers

quotes.speaker: Warlord Crox
quotes.text: Come. My axe thirsts.

quotes.speaker: Warlord Crox
quotes.text: My name is Crox, Warlord of Warlords. Look upon my works, ye mighty, and despair!–Inscribed upon a shattered monument on Ch’Kar

quotes.speaker: Warmaster Varan the Undefeatable
quotes.text: That was unexpected, wasn't it?
quotes.source: Cain's Last Stand (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus), Chapter 24, pg. 756

quotes.speaker: Warmaster Varan the Undefeatable
quotes.text: Well, that was unexpected...
quotes.source: The Traitor's Hand (Novel) Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium, Chapter 18

quotes.speaker: Welbec Ironlock,servant of Khorne
quotes.text: Pain paves my path to glory!
quotes.source: Wrath and Glory Core Rulebook,pg. 341 Possessed Mortal

quotes.speaker: World Eaters motto
quotes.text: "Attack" is the only order worth remembering.
quotes.source: Realm of Chaos Slaves to Darkness, pg. 252, FFG Preview of the Forbidden Stars Game Codex: Chaos Space Marines (8th Edition),pg. 164

quotes.speaker: Zygthu Hel, Anointed Sorcerer of the Burning Path
quotes.text: Knowledge is a sharpened blade in willing hands and the eager mind a razors edge to sink deep into the flesh of your foes.
Akrep Xie, Watch Master of Eye of Octos
quotes.source: War Zone Octarius - Book 1: Rising Tide, pg. 10

quotes.speaker: - Inquisitorial Maxim
quotes.text: Trust No One.

quotes.speaker: Arbitrator General Bex van Sturm
quotes.text: Inquisitors? They’re sneaky bastards. Useful, yes, even necessary, but I wouldn’t buy a used aircar from any of them.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), ch. 8, pg. 133

quotes.speaker: Arnaut Cisneros, Interrogator
quotes.text: Heed my warning, Acolyte. The Imperial Creed teaches us that xenos are foul and corruptive beings, and that is true. But as servants of the Inquisition, we must stand as examples of utmost purity and unwavering devotion. You will be punished for showing any shred of leniency. You will be cleansed for showing any notion of corruption. How do you think Inquisitor Serranus lost his arm? He did that to himself.

quotes.speaker: Artemis, Watch Captain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: Do not ask, “Why kill the alien? Rather ask, “Why not?”

quotes.speaker: Athocles Van Roth, Inquisitor of Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: +++ What the greenskins achieved with this'crown' is not just fascinating, it is tantalising. The ability to disrupt the power of synapse creatures with a technological device... If we could replicate it, even to just a small extent, we could change the face of the galaxy for all Mankind. Will this be difficult? Yes. Will it be hazardous in the extreme? Undoubtedly. We must pursue this opportunity regardless. I could be Humanity's saviour. +++
quotes.source: War Zone Octarius - Book 1: Rising Tide, pg. 24

quotes.speaker: Azkariel, Codicier of Deathwatch
quotes.text: With a thought I can shatter bone and crumple steel, shield my comrades behind a banner of impenetrable force or augment their might with my own. I am a living weapon – honed, deadly and versatile enough to take the fight to the xenos foe and prevail.

quotes.speaker: Brand, Watch Captain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: Amongst a hundred men, there may be none fit for the Adeptus Astartes. Amongst a hundred Space Marines, there may be one fit for the Deathwatch.

quotes.speaker: Brother-Captain Cynewolf of the Deathwatch
quotes.text: The ethics of booklearning and scholarship I leave to the savants. But for what it's worth, I would have burned it without a second thought.

quotes.speaker: Caecilius Elbegdorj, Interrogator
quotes.text: Aion Escher of Ordo Xenos, Grandmaster of the Damocles Conclave
quotes.source: Fire and Ice (Short Story), Title Page

quotes.speaker: Caecilius Elbegdorj, Interrogator
quotes.text: You think you know heresy? You think the warnings of the Ministorum, the edicts screeched from habway Lex hailers encompass the whole bounds of the law, and that the heretic merely exists beyond these strictures? There are laws so natural, so innate, that no good citizen of the Imperium could comprehend what it is to break them. The Heretic is an unnatural thing, a beast as foul as any mutant or xenos. To slay them is a mercy to be delivered without sentiment, without hesitation, and without doubt.

quotes.speaker: Canticle of Absolution
quotes.text: Our will shall be our weapons, our faith shall be our armour.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Grey Knight Paragon

quotes.speaker: Carrhe, Second Voice of the Silent, Explicator
quotes.text: It’s true that the stain of ruinous knowledge can weigh heavy on a woman, but upholding His word and law will always be its own reward.

quotes.speaker: Caspiel Rex, Inquisitor
quotes.text: Strategy Cards for Planetary Empires expansion game, card Hold At All Costs - Minor Stratagem", (saved archive page, dated February 2011, last accessed 6 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Ciaphas Cain, Commissar
quotes.text: I'm sure most of the men in the galaxy are familiar with the sinking feeling that accompanies the words "Do you think you could do me a little favour, darling?", but when the woman asking the question is an inquisitor it's even less wise than usual to say "No".
quotes.source: Duty Calls (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus), ch. 6, pg. 303

quotes.speaker: Codex: Deathwatch (8th Edition), pg. 71
quotes.text: Hatred. It is our greatest weapon against the endless xenos threat. Hatred of their unclean forms, their unnatural weapons, their rapacious appetites and their false deities. Hatred of every aspect of their alien nature. Let hatred lend strength to your arm and steel to your resolve. Let hatred carry you to victory over these unclean creatures, until nothing remains of them but butchered meat and burning ruin.
quotes.source: Toreus of Genesis Chapter, Chaplain of Deathwatch

quotes.speaker: Corvean, Watch Captain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: To fall upon the enemy with meteoric fury, death blazing from your clenched fists, armoured body impervious to shot and blade, and to tear the heart from the foe – that is the way of the Inceptor, and it is a doctrine that has seen the foul xenos defeated time and time again.

quotes.speaker: Damien Savantas, Interrogator, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Get yourself together before the Inquisitor gets a look at you. Lesson number one: fear her just as much as you would your enemies. She’ll throw you out the airlock if you’re not presentable for her court.

quotes.speaker: Damien Savantas, Interrogator, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Look upon this hammer I hold before me, it is far more than a weapon. It is a symbol of the Imperial Justice that smites the diabolic enemies of the Imperium. It is a symbol of my Holy Order and my office, of the authority granted to me by the God-Emperor and the great Inquisitor Ruad. Obey me without question, or it will also symbolise your punishment.

quotes.speaker: Declaration of Inquisitorial mandate, Inquisitorial representative, Senate Imperialis
quotes.text: So great has been the calamitousness of these times, and such the inveterate malice of the heretics, that there has been nothing ever so clear in our statement of faith, nothing so surely settled, which they, at the instigation of the enemy of the human race, have not defiled by some sort of error. For which cause the holy Inquisition hath made it Its especial care to condemn and anathematise the principal errors of the heretics of our time, and to deliver and teach the true and Imperial doctrine; even as It has condemned, anathematised, and decreed.

quotes.speaker: Fidus Kryptman, formerly Lord Inquisitor of Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: One cannot consider the fate of a single man, nor ten, nor a hundred, nor a thousand. Billions will live or die by our actions here, and we have not the luxury to count the cost.

quotes.speaker: Fidus Kryptman, formerly Lord Inquisitor of Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: We are neither spies, soldiers, nor enforcers, yet we are all these at once. We are whatever the Imperium needs us to be. We alone know the horrors that await Mankind should the Imperium fall, so we alone are willing to do whatever it takes to prevent it. Whatever that makes us.
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 5

quotes.speaker: Galan Noirgrim, Master of the Ordo Malleus
quotes.text: Heresy is like a tree, its roots lie in the darkness whilst its leaves wave in the sun and to those who suspect nought, it has an attractive and pleasing appearance. Truly, you can prune away its branches, or even cut the tree to the ground, but it will grow up again ever the stronger and ever more comely. Yet all awhile the root grows thick and black, gnawing at the bitter soil, drawing its nourishment from the darkness, and growing even greater and more deeply entrenched.Such is the nature of heresy, and this is why it is so hard to destroy, for it must be eradicated leaf, branch, trunk and root. It must be exorcised utterly or it will return all the stronger, time and time again, until it is too great to destroy. Then we are doomed.

quotes.speaker: Galan Noirgrim, Master of the Ordo Malleus
quotes.text: Now the past must unveil one of its darkest secrets, the story of the Plague of Unbelief and its most heinous vector Bucharis the Apostate Cardinal of Gathalamor. Never has the Imperium endured such a crisis of faith, not since the dark days of the Horus Heresy itself.

quotes.speaker: Galthor Nox of Mortifactors, Watch Sergeant of Deathwatch
quotes.text: There are those who speak of the xenos in terms of their cultures, their desires and motivations. Such asinine prating misses the point entirely. The xenos can and must be viewed as a singular whole, a disease afflicting the Emperor’s realm. And we, the Deathwatch, are the cure.

quotes.speaker: Garrvac, Veteran of Deathwatch
quotes.text: When I fought the alien scourge alongside my brother Iron Hands, I was but a warrior. Now, as a Veteran of the Deathwatch, I am a weapon, optimised to slaughter xenos in the Emperor’s name.

quotes.speaker: Ghalis, Watch Sergeant of Deathwatch
quotes.text: How many have given their lives in service to Humanity, their names never to be recorded, their deeds never to be celebrated? The Deathwatch seek not laurels; it is sufficient that the Emperor knows our names and our deeds.

quotes.speaker: Grand Master Mandulis of the Grey Knights
quotes.text: Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of the daemon, I shall fear nothing. For I am what the daemon fears.

quotes.speaker: Grand Master Osma of the Ordos Helican
quotes.text: Gregor Eisenhorn, in fealty to the God-Emperor, our undying lord, and by the grace of the Golden Throne, in the name of the Ordo Malleus and the Inquisition, I call thee diabolis, and in the testimony of thy crimes, I submit this carta. May imperial justice account in all balance. The Emperor protects.

quotes.speaker: Grizmund Sol, Ordo Hereticus Interrogator
quotes.text: I understand the irony, governess, but fear not; I have been trained by the Emperor’s own sages, the Adeptus Astra Telepathica. Through the protection of His shining light, I am made incorruptible. Besides, who better to hunt a witch than a Psyker?
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 53

quotes.speaker: Grunwald,Inquisitor, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Mankind has never been alone amongst the stars. There have ever been the other races of the universe. It is neither in Man’s nature to share his worlds with the aliens nor in theirs to share with Man. And so there has always been conflict and war. An unending series of wars. Wars of annihilation. Wars of genocide. The alien races are an abomination. We must fight them to their last breath; no quarter asked, none given. Even the places of the alien must be abhorred and shunned. Any trace of their being must be erased from the landscape lest its foul taint blight us.

quotes.speaker: Harker Dunne,Forge Labourer
quotes.text: Be silent! They are the eye that sleepeth not — mention their name again and they will appear, and when they do, death will be the least of your worries! We are all sinners here, even the pure!
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 63

quotes.speaker: Heliates, Chaplain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: Three Corvus Blackstars fell upon Karnat, slipping undetected through the planet’s atmosphere and delivering their deadly passengers into the heart of the Aeldari enclave. As battle raged, the aircraft swooped overhead, black shadows of death. As dawn broke they were gone, and their passengers with them, leaving naught but ruin in their wake.

quotes.speaker: Ignerious, Epistolary of Deathwatch
quotes.text: The witches of the xenos races use their abilities with reckless abandon, rending reality like the beasts they are. Ours is a purer power, our minds sanctified by the Emperor himself, our souls fortified in adamant and iron. Thus we bestride the battlefield of the empyrean like conquering heroes, and the filthy aliens scatter before us like shadows before a burning brand.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor
quotes.text: Your objections are irrelevant; I am an Inquisitor, and I answer to none save the Emperor of Mankind. Regarding my methods, He seems content to remain silent.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Aegidius Halikarn, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Assume nothing - no writ no warrant, no seal, save my own - is truly valid. The heretic hides behind the titles and honours of the Imperium just as surely as they lurk among the wretched and the malcontent.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Aegidius Halikarn, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: The soul of the Imperium teeters on the edge of damnation. Our role is not only to prevent its fall, but to ensure that the blessedly ignorant masses never learn just how close we have come to failure.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Alis Ruad, Ordo Hereticus,(possibly Ordo Malleus)
quotes.text: Acolyte, I gave you no more of a stern warning than your fellows. My Rosette is not a child’s toy nor a ticket to be brazenly waved for passage. Though your actions saved Hive Sepkos for now, you have abused the authority I presented you. You will submit for re-indoctrination immediately or be hunted and executed by your peers. Conduct yourself properly, for once.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Alis Ruad, Ordo Hereticus,(possibly Ordo Malleus)
quotes.text: I have clashed with creatures the sight of which would sear your soul to the core. I have bested horrors that chill with a gaze and tempt unreasoning terror. I have faced death eye to eye and blade to blade. I have stared into the eyes of insanity and met their all-consuming stare. I have done all this for you, for your protection, and to guarantee a future for Humanity. You dare, you who cowers, quivering in the safety of your glittering hive, to question me!?

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Allendyne
quotes.text: His loyalty couldn't be bought at any price; but it could be rented remarkably cheaply.
quotes.source: The Traitor's Hand (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), ch. 9, pg. 601

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Amberley Vail, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Even if you could take us both, and I sincerely doubt that, I've lost count of the number of people I've met who thought they could outrun the Inquisition. But you might as well give it a try if you really want to. After all, there's a first time for everything.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium (Omnibus), ch. 10, pg. 172

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Amberley Vail, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: So there it is: unimaginably ancient Xenos artefact somewhere on the planet in the hands of a renegade, the hive fleet poised above our heads ready to rip this world apart, hidden'stealer broods everywhere making an early start on their behalf, and now a bloody Chaos cult crawling out of the woodwork just in case we were getting bored. Welcome to my world, Ciaphas.
quotes.source: Duty Calls (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus), ch. 11, pg. 345

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Amberley Vail, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Some Inquisitors believe the Emperor’s work is best done by charging around the place like a grox in a ceramic emporium, leaving a trail of carnage and destruction in their wake, while others prefer not to let the enemies of all that’s good and holy get clean away by making it blindingly obvious they’re coming for them.
quotes.source: Duty Calls (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus), ch. 12, pg. 349, fn. 1

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Apollyon, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: He who allows the alien to live shares in the crime of its existence.
quotes.source: Warrior Coven (Novel)- Kill Squad: A Deathwatch Omnibus, ch. 2, pg. 202

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Atellus Thrax
quotes.text: Trust in the God-Emperor. But trust your blade, too.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Bastalek Grim
quotes.text: I know not if he represents the greatest threat or greatest hope for the future of the Imperium. I only pray We stop him before We find out.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Brand
quotes.text: Of creations most foul I beheld the Lord of All and knew that I was dead.
quotes.source: Codex: Daemonhunters (3rd Edition), pg. 18; Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Czevak
quotes.text: You are not free whose liberty is won by the rigour of other, more righteous souls. You are merely protected. Your freedom is parasitic; you take and offer nothing in return. You who have enjoyed freedom, who have done nothing to earn it, your time has come.–Inquisitor Czevak’s address to the Inquisitional council of Ryanti

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Defay to Sarthos
quotes.text: Chaos is the great devourer, the enemy of all humanity, and I have been made its pawn. My duty to the Emperor has been manipulated and corrupted from the very start. So just kill me, if that is what you intend.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Felroth Gelt
quotes.text: Now, you face a somewhat better class of heretic.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gideon Ravenor
quotes.text: Chaos claims the unwary or the incomplete. A true man may flinch away its embrace, if he is stalwart, and he girds his soul with the armour of contempt.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Globus Vaarak
quotes.text: There shall be no mercy for those too weak to adhere to the correct course. For mercy shall destroy us if it erodes our strength and weakens our resolve. Find courage in your hatred of the xenos, the impure and witch. To do otherwise is to shame the very foundations of the institution you represent.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: I am forty-two standard years old, in my prime by Imperial standards, young by those of the Inquisition. All my life, I have had a reputation for being cold, unfeeling. Some have called me heartless, ruthless, even cruel. I am not. I am not beyond emotional response or compassion. But I possess - and my masters count this as perhaps my paramount virtue - a singular force of will. Throughout my career it has served me well to draw on this facility and steel myself, unflinching, at all that this wretched galaxy can throw at me. To feel pain or fear or grief is to allow myself a luxury I cannot afford.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: I'm an inquisitor. I do not get angry.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: I'm excusing a lot because I recognize the trauma and the loss you have suffered. But my patience isn't limitless... unlike my authority.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: My lord, what foetid corner of the Imperium breeds these fawning idiots? Now you really owe me.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: The guilty and the polluted fight. They deny. They struggle. In my lifetime, I have brought down nine marked Diabolus. None went quietly.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn
quotes.text: Who do you work for?

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregorina Praxia of the Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Governor, let me speak plainly. The hero of the Battox Sector campaign lends me his armies freely. I am allied with the Arbitrators of a dozen sub-sectors. Adepta Sororitas Canonesses Superior speak my name in praise and offer me aid without my asking. You rule a backwater that barely avoids economic collapse. And yet, you refuse my demands? I invite you to reconsider.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Gregorina Praxia of the Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: The ignorant consider the Imperium Sanctus safe. To them I say look upon the Nephilim Sector. Once a bastion of Imperial order, now it is the Pariah Nexus—a somber graveyard presided over by soulless Necrons who see humanity only as a verminous infestation to be eradicated.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Havelock Brundsted
quotes.text: Turning the weapons of the enemy against him is, of course, of grave danger to one’s soul. It is nonetheless a satisfying thing to see.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Herrod
quotes.text: We are fighting a war, the beginning of which none can remember and the end of which none of us shall ever see. It is a war without boundaries, without rules or principle, a war that must be fought without conscience, without remorse, and without limit. There is no reward for victory, only a price for failure.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Historical Galbus Heer
quotes.text: You must face the truth squarely and without flinching from duty. Our Enemies are mortal no longer. Mercy for such as they is a chimera, self-deception is its only ally. Dedicate this weapon, given unto your hand at the behest of the Emperor, to their destruction. Regard its function as your only duty: you live only to bring cleansing fire. Take up your rod and staff, your armour and psycannon, and go forth.
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 8

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Horst
quotes.text: Admiral Spire, it is said that heresy is like a tree. It's roots lie in darkness while it's leaves wave in the sun. You can prune away its branches, even cut the tree to the ground but it will grow again, ever stronger. Such is the nature of heresy and why it is so difficult to destroy. Some may question my right to destroy a world of ten billion souls, but those who understand realize I have no right to let them live. No sacrifice is too great; no treachery too small.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Kartize, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: There is no end to the abomination of the alien. Do not allow any guise of sentience to stay your hand – there can be no hesitation when the survival of humanity as a species is at stake.
quotes.source: Fantasy Flight Games- Teaser to "Enemies Without", Supplement to Dark Heresy Second Edition, (last accessed 9 August 2015)

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Katarinya Greyfax, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Faith. In the right hands, it is humanity’s greatest weapon. yet even the finest blade is rendered fallible by its wielder. In the wrong hands, the mighty sword of faith can do more hurt to the Emperor’s realm than can all the treacherous daemons of the warp combined. This is why those who profess to be the most holy amongst us must be put to the sternest test.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Lord Fyodor Karamazov
quotes.text: There is no such thing as a plea of innocence in my court. A plea of innocence is guilty of wasting my time. Guilty.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Malden
quotes.text: You get more with a kind word and an Excruciator than with just a kind word.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), ch. 2, pg. 47

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Malich
quotes.text: Though Mankind is at the brink of this great apotheosis, you must be ever vigilant for the stillborn harbingers of this change. Where you seek the pure and strong, there too will you find the impure and weak. Where you find but one worthy of life, you will also find a million twisted, deformed monstrosities for whom death by your hand is a mercy. Therefore, look first for the mutant, for he may never hide his sin from you, and in his midst there will be revealed to you that which you seek. But be ever vigilant, for even should you find one with the gift, only one in a thousand psykers will be strong enough to resist the perils of the Empyrean and be allowed to live.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Quixos
quotes.text: My enemy’s enemy is a problem for later: In the meantime, they might be useful.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), ch. 12, pg. 201

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Taarn
quotes.text: Aliens are scum, they must be eradicated from our worlds, purged from the galaxy, destroyed wherever they appear. However, on occasion they can prove a useful tool...

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Titus Drake
quotes.text: Things are very seldom what they seem. In my experience, they’re usually a damn sight worse.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Hero of the Imperium(Omnibus), ch. 9, pg. 155

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Whitlock
quotes.text: I am minded to raise you to the position that you have craved and dreamt of for so long. I am, though, reticent. My friend, you are the finest apprentice I have ever tutored and there is no one I would trust more between here and the Golden Throne. Because of this I am loathe to place the burden of our high office on you: Its glitter is a lie and its rewards no more than the dry ash of a funeral pyre”.

quotes.speaker: Interrogator Caprice Valentino
quotes.text: Why should you help me, General? In five years’ time, I’ll be an Inquisitor, and you won’t.

quotes.speaker: Isembard Shoal,Inquisitorial Acolyte
quotes.text: In all the years I’ve known you, not once have you turned down an opportunity to boast about your collection. Just between the two of us, how did you acquire anÆonic Orb?

quotes.speaker: Issad, Brother-Captain of the Grey Knights
quotes.text: As my battle-brothers are clad in armour of Truesilver, so shall they ride to war in gleaming chariots, protected against all harm until the moment of battle is come.
quotes.source: Imperial Armour Volume Two (Second Edition), pg. 204

quotes.speaker: Jerran Dynaskus, Interrogator serving Lord Inquisitor Dezah
quotes.text: Scour every rat hole, pressure every official, root out every weakness, and twist until they scream the confession we need. Xendenarius, I want the city’s cogitator-screed picked clean. Ghreen, see what skulls you can crack among the Enforcers. Skharen, go and play with the syndicates, cartels, and gutter crews that the worshipful adepts tells us do not exist — see what truths your skin-peeler can elicit. We must miss nothing. Our master demands as much, and his patience has limits.

quotes.speaker: Josef Karta, Acolyte
quotes.text: I was a farmer for most of my life. We didn’t know the xenos were already living among us. I was forced to take up arms against the invasion when the war began. I needed to learn all I could about them to lead the fight; I had no choice. When the Inquisitor came and purged the xenos from our land, they spared my mind from being purged too, said I knew the enemy too well, and that my fight continues elsewhere...
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 10

quotes.speaker: Julius Scipio, Deathwatch Librarian and Loremaster of the Kabiri Archives
quotes.text: Be thankful the God-Emperor wills you die but once in His service! Beware those who serve any other, for they shall die a thousand times in the name of their master, and yet shall not know victory!

quotes.speaker: Justicar Alaric of the Grey Knights
quotes.text: We do not know what our chances of survival are, so we fight as if they were zero. We do not know what we are facing, so we fight as if it was the dark gods themselves. No one will remember us now and we may never be buried beneath Titan, so we will build our own memorial here. The Chapter might lose us and the Imperium might never know we existed, but the Enemy- the Enemy will know. The Enemy will remember. We will hurt it so badly that it will never forget us until the stars burn out and the Emperor vanquishes it at the end of time. When Chaos is dying, its last thought will be of us. That is our memorial - carved into the heart of Chaos. We cannot lose, Grey Knights. We have already won.

quotes.speaker: Justicar Alaric of the Grey Knights
quotes.text: You had to use the whole Trail. Saint Evisser, the cardinals, every single citizen, you had to move them all into position to beat us. Remember that. You put your plan in motion before the Trail even existed, because you knew it would take nothing less. We made you work, daemon. You feared us so much you had to move star systems to make us dance to your tune.

quotes.speaker: Khaj’katai of White Scars, Veteran of Deathwatch
quotes.text: In the breach at Caltagula the Tyranids outnumbered us thirty to one. It hardly seemed a fair fight... for them.

quotes.speaker: Khaj’katai of White Scars, Veteran of Deathwatch
quotes.text: Khyron, First Grand Master of the Eighth Brotherhood
quotes.source: The Emperor's Gift (Novel), ch. Twelve, pg. 153

quotes.speaker: Konstan Serranus, Inquisitor, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: Flame, bolt, and blade - all are but tools. Your true weapon against the xenos is contempt. Let it fester in your heart, let it strengthen your will, and their wiles will not assail you.

quotes.speaker: Konstan Serranus, Inquisitor, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: The darkest hour has come. Lothos, go to the fleet commander and have them blockade the planet – no one gets off this rock until the last foul xenos has been purged from the surface. Skarr, rally the Ministorum to gather and safeguard the people and tell that damned Magos to shut down their manufactorum. Karpath, ascend the spire and take control of the planetary relay, kill the chatter. I will remain on the flagship — know that the Deathwatch are coming. Do not fail me.

quotes.speaker: Ksenia Baek, Inquisitor, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Be saved, mutant, filthy as you are. Even a wretch like you can earn salvation serving the God-Emperor’s Inquisition.

quotes.speaker: Ksenia Baek, Inquisitor, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: Fear not, for the wards I've placed upon your bones will preserve your soul even if the flesh is flensed from them by the horrors duty demands you face.

quotes.speaker: Lady Olianthe Rathbone, Inquisitrix Prima
quotes.text: Ah, I see that you think to take this matter up with the Lord Marshall? Yes? Oh, but I am afraid that Lord Gorman and myself have a mutual understanding of considerable pedigree. You understand, I am sure, my dear.—words of Inquisitor Rathbone in confrontation with Senior Arbitrator Drusil

quotes.speaker: Lady Olianthe Rathbone, Inquisitrix Prima
quotes.text: There is surprisingly less to most men than meets the eye.

quotes.speaker: Lauraedian, Veteran of Deathwatch
quotes.text: In the Emperor’s name I have fought across burning plains and amidst lightning-wreathed peaks, in the cold void of space and through the ruins of cities laid low. I have done battle with the mutant, the heretic and the alien, and I have become their bane.

quotes.speaker: Lias du Ortise, Inquisitor, Ordo Malleus
quotes.text: They are beyond comprehension, beyond mortal flesh, beyond even time and mortality. They are beyond many things, but never my hate!
quotes.source: FFG Site Teaser for Enemy Beyond Supplement for Dark Heresy Second Edition- (last accessed 16 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Lichtenstein,Inquisitor,declared Excommunicate Traitoris 998. M41
quotes.text: Do not presume to judge me or the methods I choose to employ, petty-minded fool. You cannot comprehend the magnitude of the task I have undertaken nor the consequences of my failure.

quotes.speaker: Loffengar, Inquisitor, Ordo Xenos
quotes.text: If you can confront the xenos, look upon the xenos, even think upon the xenos, without revulsion, then you are as damned as they.
quotes.source: FFG Site Teaser for Enemy Without Supplement for Dark Heresy Second Edition(last accessed 11 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Lord Hephaestos Grudd
quotes.text: Look upon this hammer I hold before me, for it is far more than a weapon. It is a symbol of the Imperial justice that smites the diabolic enemies of the Imperium wherever they are found, just as I. Though it has banished even a mighty Greater Daemon to the hell from which it was spawned, it remains true and pure, just as I. Furthermore, it is a symbol of my order and my office, of the authority granted to me by the divine will of the Emperor. By that authority, I am commanding you and your entire regiment to obey me without question or hesitation. Advance, or it will not be daemonic blood that stains my hammer this day.

quotes.speaker: Lord Hephaestos Grudd
quotes.text: The daemonic are without number, and their legions span the galaxy. But faith does not tire. Should it take us an eternity, the Ordo Malleus will find and exterminate them all.

quotes.speaker: Lord Varlak
quotes.text: Curses! Just when you've finally managed to bring the whole world under your evil influence some pathetic little Inquisitor goes whining off to the Adeptus Terra about rogue psykers and daemonic possession. I mean, do I look possessed? Well, do I? DO I???

quotes.speaker: Malcador the Sigillite
quotes.text: In this age of darkness, only one thing is certain. Each of us, without exception, must choose a side.

quotes.speaker: Malcador the Sigillite
quotes.text: Those who can overcome the pride born of conquest, who can temper glory with modesty, fashion shields against the blades of those who seek to bring them low. Those who let pride blind them instead find humility only upon their death bed, when it is already far too late.

quotes.speaker: Malcador the Sigillite
quotes.text: You are Astartes indeed, and that will never alter. But you are a Death Guard no longer. You are a ghost, a figure that stands between light and dark, trapped amid the grey. And I have need of such a man.
quotes.source: Garro: Oath of Moment (Audio Book), Track 5: Flashback

quotes.speaker: Marr, Inquisitor
quotes.text: There is no better measure of a man than the quality of his enemies.

quotes.speaker: Neve, Inquisitor-General
quotes.text: The place breeds recidivists like a pond breeds scum.

quotes.speaker: Oath of the Grey Knights Seventh Brotherhood, attributed to its first Grand Master
quotes.text: Rubric of Protocol,by Inquisitor Crowe,Standard actions of interrogation as described

quotes.speaker: Oath of the Grey Knights Seventh Brotherhood, attributed to its first Grand Master
quotes.text: We die, though our war is eternal,We are doomed, but we walk in darkness,We are forgotten, yet the future is our gift to humanity.'

quotes.speaker: Peytr Strolke, Acolyte
quotes.text: Disregard the gaps you feel in your memory – know that you have seen too much, and removal was necessary. The completed their mission on; the threat led by were effectively purged by their supreme will. But take heed; perhaps we overshot our ambitions this time.

quotes.speaker: Poltus Hale, Ordo Malleus Interrogator
quotes.text: A small mind is a tidy mind, that is true. But those Gangers are so doltish they’d summon a daemon before they’d realise they were reading a heretical text. Thank the Emperor they’re all illiterate.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 68

quotes.speaker: Pontius Glaw
quotes.text: Your daily work brings you closer and closer to the warp, increases your understanding of orderless powers. Gradually, without noticing it, even the most puritanical and rod-stiff inquisitor becomes seduced... The first step is the knowledge. An inquisitor must understand the basic traits of Chaos in order to fight it. In a few years, he knows more about the warp than most untutored cultists. Then the second step: the moment he breaks the rules and allows some aspect of Chaos to survive or remain so that he can study it and learn from it... The third step... the third step is the line itself. When the inquisitor becomes a radical. When he chooses to use Chaos against Chaos. When he employs the agencies of the warp. When he asks the heretical for help... So... are you going to ask me to help you?

quotes.speaker: Qualtak Shoran, Inquisitor
quotes.text: The creatures of the Warp have but one trait with which you need concern yourself - their undying contempt for the Emperor. It is your task to quell the rebellion they preach, and the only sure way is to destroy them utterly.
quotes.source: White Dwarf 114 (UK), pg. 11(republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 5)

quotes.speaker: Retinue Oath
quotes.text: By this oath, I pledge to stand by thee, against traitor, beast, or fiend, for only in death does my duty end.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Bastion Protector

quotes.speaker: Shahin Vane, Acolyte, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: May foul blood feed my blade this night.

quotes.speaker: Sister Revati of the Order of the Sanctified Shield
quotes.text: Their methods are oft impious, and many take on the sinister aspects of those they are duty bound to destroy. Our creed is of cleansing flame and holy light; remember that when you work with those agents of the shadows.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 63

quotes.speaker: Sliff, Trackless Chain Routerunner, Cruciator
quotes.text: A doc’s hands can help or hurt, easy as anything. All comes down to who’s holding the leash.

quotes.speaker: Soldevan, Inquisitor, Ordo Hereticus
quotes.text: What is your duty? To look the universe in the face and then spit in its eye. To die with a curse on your lips and your finger on the trigger. To know that you are doomed, but try anyway.

quotes.speaker: Stormtrooper Sergeant Havlock
quotes.text: Understanding is overrated.

quotes.speaker: Tandariss, Watch Captain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: And yet you accuse me of being a madman, you who have never had your sanity tested so sorely. What right have you to call me heretic and blasphemer, who have not heard the whisper of the dark gods in your ear? You are weak. Vulnerable. Human in your frailty. I am strong, and yet you still judge me for my sins, thou who art most sinful to the heart? Every scholam child knows, “Only the insane have strength enough to prosper: only those who prosper truly judge what is sane".
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 6

quotes.speaker: Tandariss, Watch Captain of Deathwatch
quotes.text: We are all of us noble, faithful, loyal. We are devoted servants of the Emperor and combatants of exceptional might. Yet we all possess our own doctrinal peculiarities, our own ego. Amongst lesser warriors, such differences would prove insurmountable, tearing the squad apart. Amongst we Adeptus Astartes, overcoming such differences only tightens our bonds, and makes us stronger still.

quotes.speaker: Tarn Laggarian, Confessor
quotes.text: One world. Nineteen days. A hundred damned souls absolved in death by my hand.

quotes.speaker: The Book of Exorcisms, the Verses of Inquisitor Enoch
quotes.text: We are at War with forces too terrible to comprehend. We cannot afford mercy for any of its victims too weak to take the correct course. Mercy destroys us; it weakens us and saps our resolve. Put aside all such thoughts. They are not worthy of Inquisitors in the service of Our Emperor. Praise His name for in our resolve we only reflect his purpose of will.
quotes.source: Realm of Chaos: Slaves to Darkness, pg. 220

quotes.speaker: The First Book of Indoctrinations
quotes.text: The Daemon has many forms. You must know them all. You must tell the Daemon from his disguise and root him out from the hidden places. Trust no-one. trust not even yourself. It is better to die in vain than to live an abomination.The zealous martyr is praised for his valour: the craven and the unready are justly abhorred.
quotes.source: Realm of Chaos: Slaves to Darkness, pg. 222

quotes.speaker: The First Book of Indoctrinations
quotes.text: The weak will always be led by the strong. Where the strong see purpose and act, the weak follow; where the strong cry out against fate, the weak bow their heads and succumb. There are many who are weak; and many are their temptations. Despise the weak for they shall flock to the call of the Daemon and the Renegade. Pity them not and scorn their cries of innocence - it is better that one hundred innocently fall before the wrath of the Emperor than one kneels before the Daemon.
quotes.source: Realm of Chaos: Slaves to Darkness, pg. 230

quotes.speaker: The Rhyme of the Ancient Acolyte
quotes.text: If you knew back then, what you know now, you wouldn’t ask ‘Why, my lord?’, but ‘How?’

quotes.speaker: The Rhyme of the Ancient Acolyte
quotes.text: Inscription upon Conclave chamber doors, Mare Cinis, Suluk

quotes.speaker: Thraviam Flast, Inquisitor of the Ordo Malleus
quotes.text: I carry with me an Inquisitorial Seal. It is a small, unassuming object contained in a neat box of Pluvian obsidian. It is a modest thing. Relatively plain, adorned with a single motif and a simple motto. Yet with this little object I can sign the death warrant of an entire world and consign a billion souls to Oblivion.
quotes.source: Codex: Daemonhunters (3rd Edition), pg. 21 Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide,pg. 20

quotes.speaker: Tiamach, Venerable Dreadnought of Deathwatch, formerly Keeper of the Solemn Archive
quotes.text: Though it has been lifetimes since I last knew true fear, no undertaking inspires such disquiet, such potent unease of the spirit as to venture onto a Space Hulk. To be alone within the carcasses of the lost, it is the truest test of one's mettle, and even amongst the mighty Astartes there are those who have not the stomach for it.
quotes.source: Deathwatch: Ark of Lost Souls, pg. 92

quotes.speaker: Torquemada Coteaz, Inquisitor Lord
quotes.text: Let none judge my deeds but the Emperor himself, for no other possesses the right nor the vision.
quotes.source: White Dwarf November 2019, pg. 27 - Index Imperialis: Inquisition: Heroes and Villains

quotes.speaker: Torquemada Coteaz, Inquisitor Lord
quotes.text: There are those amongst our Ordo who say that I seek advancement for my own purposes, and they are correct. My purpose is to destroy the daemonic and if I must rise to command an entire sector to do so, then so be it. I am a servant of the Emperor and only those who consort with Warp-spawn need fear my ambition.

quotes.speaker: Transcription of suppressed texts, from the archives of the Gethsemene Reclusium
quotes.text: Know this adept, for your journey has been long and you are to be trusted as few will ever be. Psykers represent Humanity's future, the ideal creature into which mankind will evolve; a more powerful, more intelligent and more capable life form. As yet this race is weak, its members lacking the mental strength needed to face the dangers of the psychic universe. Were the new race of psykers permitted to develop free and unprotected, the whole of Humanity would soon be destroyed. We few who are privy to this truth have guarded it selflessly for nigh on four millennia, since the end of the great schism from which our order was born. It is our sole concern, for the very future of our race depends upon us, and us alone.

quotes.speaker: Unattributed Ordo Malleus fleet member upon initiation of the Exterminatus of Typhon Primaris
quotes.text: We have arrived, and it is now that we perform our charge. In fealty to the God-Emperor, our undying Lord, and by the grace of the Golden Throne, I declare Exterminatus upon the Imperial world of Typhon Primaris. I hereby sign the death warrant of an entire world, and consign a million souls to oblivion. May Imperial Justice account in all balance. The Emperor Protects.
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II - Retribution Classics

quotes.speaker: Utorian Denash, Watch Master of Fort Pykman
quotes.text: I am vigilance. I am the hunter’s patience and the castellan’s fortitude. I am the burning brand that banishes the shadows, the shield that holds the monsters at bay and the sword that pierces their foul black hearts. I am oath, duty, victory. I am the Deathwatch, and my vigil shall never end.

quotes.speaker: Vaedrian Shenol, Watch Master of Watcher Keep
quotes.text: There are those who believe that, because the alien too is threatened by the rise of Chaos, we can somehow find common cause. These xenophiles are heretics who would have us put our trust in the xenos beasts, leaving us helpless as they plunge their blades into our backs. There can be no tolerance, no compromise, no peace with such animals. There can be only war, and the endless purgation of the xenos threat.

quotes.speaker: Voxanitor, Apothecary of the Absolvers
quotes.text: Were anyone other to suggest our exalted gene-seed be mutated, I would have their head, Inquisitor. I respect your authority, and will permit your snooping zealots to pry but one time — but know that this will not be forgotten.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 63

quotes.speaker: Xerxes Melnyk, Interrogator serving Inquisitor Ksenia Baek
quotes.text: That reverence you hold for the Emperor, the stuff you’ve been taught since you were a kid? Treat the Inquisitor just the same. They are an extension of His Will. Inquisitor says jump, you say, ‘off what spire?’. Inquisitor says purge, you use tooth and nail if that’s all you’ve got. Inquisitor says die; you run headfirst into whatever monstrous thing is staring back at you.
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 11

quotes.speaker: Xerxes Melnyk, Interrogator serving Inquisitor Ksenia Baek
quotes.text: Throne damn these wretches! Mortis, they’ve got your name and description on the vox. Castella, manufacture him a new cover story and a disguise before the worship ceremony at the Cathedrum — the circumstantial evidence is pointing towards Deacon Tzarelle, but whoever these “ Shifting Eye” heretics are, they’ll go to ground if we aren’t careful. Take your weapons and your seal, but by the Emperor’s Teeth, keep them concealed.
quotes.source: Imperium Maledictum: Inquisition Player's Guide, pg. 120

quotes.speaker: Yorin XIII, Ordo Xenos Confessor
quotes.text: An excellent companion she was, very droll indeed — if a little imprudent. Saved my life in that daemonic interlude on Charybdion. Of course, we recompensed her with those scintillating art pieces we found, I imagine she made a few Thrones on those. ‘Tis a shame I had to purge her. The story of how she acquired that blasphemous xeno- servitor must have been fascinating.—Relating to a Rogue Trader
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook (2nd Edition), pg. 66

quotes.speaker: Adept Corteswain
quotes.text: We are born for a darker purpose than that of mere existence. There will come a time when stygian night never ends, where dead stars will spread before us like islands that slumber on the ocean, and when the beings that hid like shadows will feed on us forever.

quotes.speaker: Anrakyr the Traveller
quotes.text: Cast off the shackles of your slumber! The galaxy shall be ours once again!

quotes.speaker: Anrakyr the Traveller
quotes.text: Our people are awakening, and soon all of the galaxy shall be ours once more.

quotes.speaker: Anrakyr the Traveller
quotes.text: Though it may take an eternity, I will see the myriad dynasties united once more.

quotes.speaker: Excerpted from the Dogma Ommiastra
quotes.text: That we, in our arrogance, believed that Humankind was first among the races of this galaxy will exposed as folly of the worst kind upon the awakening of these ancient beings. Any hopes, dreams or promises of salvation are naught but dust in the wind.

quotes.speaker: Excerpted from the Necron codes of battle
quotes.text: Strike directly at your adversary only when a formal declaration has been made. Victory without honour is worth nothing.

quotes.speaker: Illuminor Szeras
quotes.text: Immortality is not quite the same as invulnerability, but it is close enough.

quotes.speaker: Illuminor Szeras
quotes.text: The secrets of the universe are within my grasp. You cannot stop me now.

quotes.speaker: Imotekh the Stormlord
quotes.text: Order. Unity. Obedience. We taught the galaxy these things long ago, and we will do so again.

quotes.speaker: Inquisitor Kessel
quotes.text: We are less than cattle to these beings, chattel to be cast aside, consumed or made sport with for their pleasure. There is not one amongst them that would pay heed to a world of Man as I would to an ant on my boot.

quotes.speaker: Maechu, Farseer of Ulthwé
quotes.text: They will seek to harvest us at first, for they will be hungry after their long sleep. Then they will turn their efforts to enslaving the survivors. The galaxy will bleed the stench of death into the void, and it will attract the others of their kind. We must choose unity or death.

quotes.speaker: Morillia, Harlequin Shadowseer
quotes.text: It was silent as the void, and to look upon it was to know terror. It drifted above us with slow, liquid grace, and its gaze caused madness and despair wherever it fell. Those it came near took their own lives rather than endure its hellish presence.

quotes.speaker: Paullian Blantar, Iron-Father, Iron Hands
quotes.text: The Machine is strong. We must purge the weak, hated flesh and replace it with the blessed purity of metal. Only through permanence can we truly triumph, only through the Machine can we find victory. Punish the flesh. Iron in mind and body. Hail the Machine!

quotes.speaker: Szarekh, the Silent King
quotes.text: All mortal endeavor is just the universe's futility wearing a mask of self-delusion.

quotes.speaker: Szarekh, the Silent King
quotes.text: While we slept, our inferiors infested the worlds that belong to us. Now, we awaken once more.

quotes.speaker: The Book of Mournful Night
quotes.text: When the Silent King saw what had been done, he knew at last the true nature of the C'tan, and the doom they had wrought in his name.
quotes.source: Magic: The Gathering's Universes Beyond Warhammer 40,000- Their Name Is Death

quotes.speaker: The Chronicle of Szarekh
quotes.text: They came to us as gods, and we, like fools, took them at their word. Mephet'ran the Deceiver, Aza'gorod the Nightbringer, Iash'uddra the Endless Swarm; I curse their names, and the names of all their malevolent brethren.

quotes.speaker: Trazyn the Infinite
quotes.text: Let the others bicker over their little worlds and stars. I have an empire of knowledge, and it encompasses all others.

quotes.speaker: Trazyn the Infinite
quotes.text: Rise and serve me once more.

quotes.speaker: Unknown
quotes.text: You mortals turn to dust in the blink of an eye. I only expedite your fate.

quotes.speaker: Unknown Necron Overlord
quotes.text: This galaxy once knelt before us, and it will do so again.

quotes.speaker: 'Fingaz' Rutzeg, Deathskull Loota
quotes.text: Wot, this? Naw, I've had this fer ages. Of course the paint's still wet, it's me favourite. Sell it to ya if you like. One careful owner.

quotes.speaker: An anonymous Ork Boy
quotes.text: Oomans are pink and soft, not tough and green like da Boyz. They'z all the same size too, so they'z always arguing about who's in charge, 'cos no way of telling 'cept fer badges an' ooniforms and fings. When one of them wants to lord it over the uvvers, 'e says "I'm very speshul so'z you gotta worship me", or "I know summink wot you lot don't know so yer better lissen good". Da funny fing is, arf of 'em believe it and da over arf don't, so 'e 'as to hit 'em all anyway or run fer it. Wot a lot of mukkin' about if yer asks me. An' while they'z all arguing wiv each other over who's da boss, da Orks can clobber da lot - on Humanity and its weaknesses
quotes.source: Codex: Orks (4th Edition), pg. 40; Waaargh: Orks, pg. 81

quotes.speaker: Anonymous Flash Git
quotes.text: Look at me flash, Boss!
quotes.source: Dawn of War: Dark Crusade Classics

quotes.speaker: Anonymous Ork describing his custom shoota
quotes.text: Well, 'course dis one’s betta! It's lotz 'eavier, and gots dem spikey bitz on de ends.

quotes.speaker: Bad Moons Klan
quotes.text: You're rich! You're flashy! You 'ave a proppa Orky stoutness about your belly! And you've got more big, shooty, and dead 'ard gear than any 2 other Orks put together. Da uvver clans orta make way for da Bad Moons!

quotes.speaker: Badlug One-Leg on Gargants
quotes.text: S'gorra be dead shooty, wiv loadza gunz all over.

quotes.speaker: Barrukk Life-breaker
quotes.text: Pretty flag. I will strip it from its pole and hang your flesh in its place.

quotes.speaker: Bawbag Gitstompa, Meganob
quotes.text: I'se got 'da teef, I'se got 'da loot, I'se got 'da dakka, an' I'se got 'dis 'ere mega-armour. 'Dat's why wot I sez goes aroun' 'ere, ya runty Grots. Try me any time ya'z feelin like a beatin'!
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 52

quotes.speaker: Big Thunda, Ork Warboss
quotes.text: Games-Workshop, Warhammer 40'000, 2010 'Ard Boyz, Scenario 2(saved 12 Jan 2012, last accessed 21 Jun 2025)

quotes.speaker: Bigmaw, Ork Runtherd
quotes.text: Travellin' through space is boring. Well, boring unless da hulk yer on is full of dem gene-sneakers, or a base fer da chaos lads wiv da spikes, or already has Boyz on it. Or if humie lootas come callin', that's always good fer a bit a sport. Or unless yer have a mutiny or two to pass da time, or unless strange fings start happenin', which dey usually do when yer out in da warp. One time we had some bloody great ugly fing come straight out of Weird Lugwort's 'ed! It butchered half da lads, that was pretty entertainin'. Come ter fink of it, space is a pretty good larf. And that's before yer find yerself a nice world ta crush!

quotes.speaker: Bluddflagg, Ork Kaptin
quotes.text: Dem Inquisishun humies can't be trusted any further dan day can be thrown, which in dat one's case might be a bad example.
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II - Retribution Classics

quotes.speaker: Borzash Geargrinda, Big Mek
quotes.text: Da boss already et 'af a dozen uv 'im Squigs, kilt tree uv 'im Nobz, an' pinned Halshaz's ears to a plank wit a coupla spikes 'dis mornin'. Mad? Nah, 'e's not mad. Dis is a good day.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 48

quotes.speaker: Bugsplatta Defnik of the Red Wheelz Speed Freeks
quotes.text: We's stomped 'umies, we's blown up stuff and we's driven our traks from one end of da world to da uvva. We's gonna come back next year!

quotes.speaker: Burftog Evil Sunz Mekboy
quotes.text: Hmph, dem oomies tink deys fasta then us ladz. Well they sure as Gork aint got Burftog’s Mega Orktane Go Juice and dere wagons don’t even got a red paint job... Lets krump ‘em for tryin!

quotes.speaker: Buzzgob da Burna Boy, Ork Warlord
quotes.text: Hur, hur, hur... look at Lubdog’s lads gettin’ all killed off. Stupid tin ’eds should save some ammo. Roktoof’s gitz will be arrivin’ any second now...

quotes.speaker: Chor, Da Bluddrokk, Speed Freak
quotes.text: Now, where’z we gunna get enough red paint ta make a sumfing as ‘uge as dis Kill Kroozer go fasta?
quotes.source: FFG Teaser to the "Boundless Hate" - Warpack to the Warhammer 40'000 Conquest Card Game

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: Despite their bulk they moved swiftly, and with a kind of grace completely at odds with their appearance, every movement economical and precise.

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: I was bright enough even then to know a serious threat when I saw one.... For one thing, the creatures running towards me were big, and bulging with muscle in a fashion I'd only previously seen on ogryns. Even a Catachan would have looked distinctly puny next to one of these monstrosities. Tiny red eyes glared from beneath an exaggerated brow ridge, but unlike the holos I'd seen, they were alive with malevolence, and what, if not exactly intelligence, was the kind of instinctive cunning which quite often made up for its lack.
quotes.source: Death or Glory (Novel)- Ciaphas Cain: Defender of the Imperium (Omnibus) by Sandy Mitchell,Chapter Six, pg. 75

quotes.speaker: Commissar Ciaphas Cain
quotes.text: I've got to know a great deal more about these creatures over the last century or so, since that first disconcerting encounter, and one thing I've seen time and again is that dismissing them as simple, unreasoning brutes is a fast route to the graveyard (or more likely their stomachs).

quotes.speaker: Commonly held Ork view of warfare
quotes.text: Orkses is never defeated in battle. If we win we win, if we die we die fighting so it don't count. If we runs for it we don't die neither, cos we can come back for annuver go, see!

quotes.speaker: Dagskar Snotgob, Gretchin Slaver-at the battle of Ursus Prime
quotes.text: Strategy Cards for Planetary Empires expansion game, card Displacer Field - Minor Stratagem", (saved archive page, dated February 2011, last accessed 6 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Dedeye, Kaptin
quotes.text: Gork loves me, and Mork finks I is da best. No puny oomies is gonna kill me, not when da greenest gods in da galaxy is watchin’ me!

quotes.speaker: Doc Hacksore
quotes.text: 'Ere We Go: Orks in Warhammer 40,000

quotes.speaker: Dok Gutslash
quotes.text: Right, first I'll take those teef out for yer, dat should help ease da pain in yer leg. Grokkit, hand me that wrench. Now then... Open wide, and say... AAARGH!

quotes.speaker: Fat Druzka, Flash Git
quotes.text: Bought me a deffblasta off Rotskrag earlier. Nice little killa. Just ask Rotskrag, hur hur.

quotes.speaker: Flikka Hammahead, Blood Axes Nob
quotes.text: Ere's wot we's gonna do, right? First I'se gonna yell Waaagh! an' 'den I'se gonna run up to 'em an' krump 'em. 'Den, while I'se runnin' and hollerin' an' before I get to 'da krumpin' part, you lot yells Waaagh! an’ makes a run at 'em too, see? 'Den we'll all krump togevva! 'S a great plan, innit? 'Dem 'umies'll nevva expect it.

quotes.speaker: From Culture vs. Kultur: Thoughts on Orkish Society by Uthan the Perverse, a controversial Eldar philosopher
quotes.text: The Orks are the pinnacle of creation. For them, the great struggle is won. They have evolved a society which knows no stress or angst. Who are we to judge them? We Eldar who have failed, or the Humans, on the road to ruin in their turn? And why? Because we sought answers to questions that an Ork wouldn't even bother to ask! We see a culture that is strong and despise it as crude.
quotes.source: Codex: Orks (4th Edition), pg. 8; Waaargh: Orks pg. 62

quotes.speaker: Gasgrakh, Goff Nob
quotes.text: We're da best. Think diffrent do ya? Come and have a go then, ya runty little wimp!

quotes.speaker: Genetor Aurelius Thoze, Adeptus Mechanicus Xenobiologist
quotes.text: Ork Physiology is fascinating and terrifying in equal measure, demonstrating inhuman degrees of resilience to the point where they can withstand seemingly fatal wounds with little apparent long-term consequence. Indeed, Orks witnessed suffering fatal wounds in the midst of heavy fighting have often been observed again several days later, larger and stronger than they were before their injuries and with no sign of those wounds save for some largely superficial scarring.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: Into the Storm, pg. 58

quotes.speaker: Ghazghkull Mag Uruk Thraka
quotes.text: Humies is all weak scum that deserve ta get stomped. 'Cept for One-Eye Yarrick. He knows how ter fight.

quotes.speaker: Ghazghkull Mag Uruk Thraka
quotes.text: We is gonna stomp da universe flat and kill anyfink that fights back. we're da Orks, and was made ta fight and win.

quotes.speaker: Gibli the Gretchin
quotes.text: We're safe 'ere Gobbitz. There's no way da beakies can get to us at the top of dis cliff...

quotes.speaker: Goff Warboss Bugrat Skumdreg
quotes.text: All Orks is equal, but some Orks are more equal dan uvvas.-on being accused of taking more than his fair share of the booty from the latest battle

quotes.speaker: Goggulz, Red Gobbo
quotes.text: Course we'll lose boss. We'll kill loadsa orks, and den we'll win for a bit, and den some tougher orks will find out wot 'appened and den they'll come and paste us. and den they'll laugh an' tell da stories of da time dey kicked Da Red Gobbo's head in, and den their grots will hear. And dey'll fink "Hang About - Red Gobbo?" And den he - I'll - be back. And we'll lose. And we'll lose again. And we're gonna keep losin' till da very last ork in da universe, all battle-cheered and battle-battered, raises a flag of victory over da corpses of loadsa grots, and den 'e'll fall over dead, and den loads more grots will go froo 'is pockets for anyfing shiny. Dat's da fing about grots. We've lost so much we've gotten really, really good at it.

quotes.speaker: Gogsnag Throatslitter
quotes.text: ’ere boyz, watch this one try to scream.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory Core Rulebook, pg. 355 Kommando

quotes.speaker: Golmak Spannabreaka, Mekboy
quotes.text: Ere, pass me 'dat wossname, 'dat welda. Yeah, 'dat's 'da one. Now, watch 'dis!Last words
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 50

quotes.speaker: Gorgal 'edkicker
quotes.text: Exchange between Warboss Gorgutz 'Ead 'Unter, and Tomas Macabee, Necron Pariah
quotes.source: Dawn of War: Dark Crusade Classics

quotes.speaker: Gorgal 'edkicker
quotes.text: OI! Lissen ta me wen I’z talkin’ to ya! We’z gonna go on ‘dere ship, we’z gonna smash it up an’ kill anyfing dat gets in our way, an’ den we’z gonna get back on da boat an’ go home. Dat’ll show ‘em. Do you lot of ‘umie runts unnastand dat? Good. Now, wiv me: WAAAGH!
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: Into the Storm, pg. 66

quotes.speaker: Gorklaw, Warlord
quotes.text: Wotter yer mean we only got four Stompas? We had a lots of 'em! Roight - we need ammo and scrap, an I know just where ta get 'em.

quotes.speaker: Gorsmak, Big Mek
quotes.text: Strategy Cards for Planetary Empires expansion game, card "Special Combat Weapons - Minor Stratagem", (saved archive page, dated February 2011, last accessed 6 October 2015)

quotes.speaker: Grabthroat Shinkicka, Killa Kan pilot
quotes.text: Yar! Oose 'da biggest an' da baddest now, eh? I'll show ya, I'll show ya all! Just try an' stop me!
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 54

quotes.speaker: Gregk
quotes.text: Da problem wiv gardin' dis algae is dere's nuffink to shoot at.Da lagae's no fun coz it don't shoot back, and dere ain't no 'onner in shootin' sumfink wot can't shoot back. Corse, dere ain't no 'arm in it neither – might be sumfink in dere.

quotes.speaker: Grimtoof Git-Slaver, Warboss of Waaagh! Grimtoof
quotes.text: Look at 'dis, will ya? Alla 'dese worlds is mine, and 'dose wot ain't'll be mine soon enuf. I'm gonna take evry'ting in 'em 'an no one can stop me. Not 'da 'umies, not 'dem fancy gitz, and fer sure not any of 'dem uvver Orks wot tink 'dey can nose around in my galaxy!
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 36

quotes.speaker: Grodd, Snakebite Runtherd
quotes.text: Live off the land. Go to find war. Kill wot comes close. The old ways are best.

quotes.speaker: Grukk, Ork Boy
quotes.text: We don't fight fer food, or fer teef, or guns, or cos we's told to fight. We fight cos we woz born to fight. And win.
