quotes.speaker: Gutwakka, Toofless tribe
quotes.text: I ’ate Humies. Now come ’ere so I can break yer bones.

quotes.speaker: Hit 'em hard, Hit 'em low and give 'em plenty of Dakka
quotes.text: Waaagh from above, ordered by Uzgob (call-sign: Mavarork), Kommanda of the Deff Skwadron

quotes.speaker: Jezza, Blood Axe Stormboy, speaking to a clan yoof
quotes.text: Well, yer gots yer Boyz, 'den you gots yer Oddboyz, see? 'Dey's called 'dat 'cause dey's, well, a little off in 'der head if you knows wot I mean. Dey can still loot an' krump wit' 'da best uv 'em, tho.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, pg. 48

quotes.speaker: Kaptain Parrkurr of the Ork Landa 'Funderbird Too'
quotes.text: Aim fer dat big buildin’ over der. No da uvver wun! NO DAT WUN! Zoggit, just land it ‘ere.
quotes.source: Fanatic Magazine Issue 2, pg. 35 Fanatic Press

quotes.speaker: Kog da Flymek, pioneer of the Dethkopta
quotes.text: Wot's faster than a warbuggy, more killy than a warbike, and flies through da air like a bird? I got no bleedin' idea, but I'm gonna find out.

quotes.speaker: Korporal Snagbrat of the Dreadblade Kommandos
quotes.text: Us Blood axes have learnt a lot from da humies. How best ta kill 'em, fer example.

quotes.speaker: Lanriel Taranlys, Farseer of Ul-Khari
quotes.text: Orks are everything Aeldari are not, our very antithesis. Short-lived, multitudinous, ferocious, and utterly ruled by their primal whims. The gods play a cruel joke in that Orks never suffer for their lack of foresight or their caprice. Indeed, Orks never suffer at all: they revel in pain and danger and charge joyfully towards doom, be it theirs or their enemy’s. They’re perfectly engineered for the galaxy as it is; a brutish horde rampaging across the cruel, war-torn worlds of a dire age.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory - Aeldari - Inheritance of Embers, pg. 67

quotes.speaker: legendary Ork Smartboy Derek Zog
quotes.text: The best means of Attack is Defence, an' the best means of Attack is a really really Big One, right, with lots of Boys an' dead big shooty things an' what have ya.
quotes.source: Deff Skwadron (Graphic Novel), short-story Da big push, first page

quotes.speaker: Lt Hal ‘Kisses’ Silo, Gilead Gravediggers, Weapon Specialist
quotes.text: You ever notice Orks smell different when they’re burnin’? Must be cause’ they’re made of different stuff, ya know? I swear, sets my stomach rumblin‘ every damned time.

quotes.speaker: Lugnut of the Bladed Wheels
quotes.text: Evil Sunz like two fings most: Going fast and krumpin' stuff. Dat's why we'z so good at it.

quotes.speaker: Mad Dok Grotsnik
quotes.text: Operate! Operate! Still time to operate!

quotes.speaker: Morgaash Kulgraz Warlord of Undred-Undred Teef, Kaptin of Da Wurldbreaka
quotes.text: I'z smashed 'umies an' panzies an' birdboyz an' dogfaces; I'z gonna smash anyfing wot gets in my way, an' take wotz left. I iz da biggest, 'ardest boss in all da 'Spanse, da biggest Kaptin ov Undred-Undred Teef, and nuffin kan stop me!

quotes.speaker: Morglum Necksnapper
quotes.text: The only good 'ooman is a dead 'ooman. An' da only fing better than a dead 'ooman'z a dyin' 'ooman who tell you where ter find 'is mates.

quotes.speaker: Nazdakka Boomsnik, Renowned Mekboy
quotes.text: Da best shoota I eva made, dat iz. Loadza barrulz, so dat it’s ded shooty. ‘Sept dat wun, ‘cos dat’s da skorcha, dat’s burny insted. Yeah, good an’ propa. An’ da bullitz is ‘splosiv...dey goez boom inna fings wot you’z shootin.’ An’ dat button dere...dat’s da best bit. Wot it duz, see, iz...iz...oh, zog. Nah, its nuffin’ boss. Nah, you’z don’t need ta see wot dat button duz...‘onist. Don’t push it!

quotes.speaker: Nekbrekka, Warboss
quotes.text: Look lads, dose stoopid oomies have left a bomb behind. Huh, it’s got wire attached to it. Luzgit, help me pull dis fing free.

quotes.speaker: Nork Rivets, Painboy
quotes.text: Stop yer screamin’, ‘dis a good fing! Yer old arms was rubbish, these three will be way more killy!

quotes.speaker: Nuzzgrond of the Black Skulls
quotes.text: Da only lost race I ever heard of woz when Hef crashed his trike in da final stretch of da cross-dessert rally. Cost me a few teef, that.

quotes.speaker: Ogbad, Ork Warphead
quotes.text: Dere's dis fing, see, what comes from all Orks. It goes round us an' through us, and keeps da kosmos togevva. It's called da Waaagh!, an' it makes Orks Orky. It gets bigga when we does, and gets stronger when we's fightin' an' winnin’. An' when dere's enuff of it, lots and lots of it, see, den speshul fings 'appen. Like really 'uge wars!
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Navis Primer, pg. 60

quotes.speaker: Ork Dreadnought Ace 'Jip' Gobsmak
quotes.text: Dere's jus' one fing wiv kans wot ya'av ta bovver about - Sittin' on dat spring gives ya piles!
quotes.source: White Dwarf 100 (UK), pg. 68 (republished as Warhammer 40,000: Compendium, pg. 57)

quotes.speaker: Provost-Major Kyne, at the court-martial of Lt Gordo
quotes.text: Half-glimpsed shadows? Ork wearing camouflage? Do you take us for imbeciles? Orks are barbaric and entirely single-minded. Army dogma, which has served us well for ten thousand years, teaches us this. Greenskins come on in a great horde, they do not slink and sneak in the shade. Guards! Take the prisoner to the holding cells to await execution for cowardice and incompetence.

quotes.speaker: Rekrench da Unlucky, Ork Mekboy
quotes.text: Nah, ya grot, ya don'tz need ta stop shootin' just 'cause I'z gonna fix up yer shoota. Just 'old dat bit for a second, and den I'z can stick in dis 'xtra 'splodey bi-
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Koronus Bestiary, pg. 76

quotes.speaker: Skabgutz, Ork trukk Boy
quotes.text: Oi! Stop runnin' away, yer snivellin' humies, we got a race on here!

quotes.speaker: Skarfang, Pirate-Warboss
quotes.text: Dis ain't no stinkin' scrap against puny gits dat run and hide behind walls when the killing starts, dis is proper fightin'.

quotes.speaker: Sniklat Daggerback, Grot
quotes.text: What’s it? What’s it? Give Sniklat the shiny.

quotes.speaker: Snikskin, Ork Kommando
quotes.text: Da ooman base iz got walls an’ fings, see. So, if I’z goes up to da wall, all sneaky-like, and blows it up wiv me bombs, den dere’ll be an ‘ole in da wall wot da ladz can go fru, see. So, when you’z lot ‘ears sumfing go boom, you charge,’ cos dere’ll be an ‘ole in da wall. Unnastand?

quotes.speaker: Sparnub, Ork Nob
quotes.text: Dis hunk of rock is all we gotz, and I’ll be a filthy grot before we let any introodas tread on what’s ours!

quotes.speaker: Unknown Nob, Battle of Magna Bonum
quotes.text: Orkz is made fer' two fings! Fightin', and winnin'!

quotes.speaker: Varnael Larik, Rogue Trader
quotes.text: Of all the races I have battled throughout the galaxy, the Ork is the hardest to comprehend. They wage war with machines that should not work, care little for strategic gains, and are just as likely to slaughter each other as the enemy. How does one battle an enemy that defies all logic?
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: The Koronus Bestiary, pg. 65

quotes.speaker: Warboss Brakka
quotes.text: Now git out there and krush dem all!

quotes.speaker: Warboss Headkrakka
quotes.text: Oy, iz dis thing on?! Yeah, dis here is a message for da' grayskin bosses and their kroot. If'n you're gettin' any pictures wiff this thing, you can see what we done to your kroot over there. They's all dead and making for nice decorations on me new boss-hut. If'n you're not getting da pictures, just take one of da kroot near you an' rip 'em in half. It's like that. Dis here is Warboss Hedkrakka and it wuz me and me boyz that did this to yer kroot. Dis here is Hedkrakka's land now and we'z all coming to see you'z next! I need some grayskins for me boss-hut!
quotes.source: Dawn of War: Dark Crusade Classics

quotes.speaker: Warboss Uzgul
quotes.text: Need more slaves! I know it don’t look it from up here, but dat’s a big place! Dere’s still a lot of work to do, an’ we need more hands down dere. Humies ain’t much use da gits keel over an’ die after a few weeks. Sure, yoo can easily find more, but dat takes time too. Skrawnies, on da uvver hand... Dey’re da real deal. Stronger’n dey look, an’ a powerful will to keep alive, speshully if yoo take dere stones off ’em first. I don’t fink dere’s an ork in da galaxy wot’s got more skrawnies of his own in one place.

quotes.speaker: Warlord Dragnatz
quotes.text: We’ve got our Gargantz an’ we’ve got our weapons. Wot ain't we got? We ain’t got anyfing for target practice iz wot! So I'll tell you wot we're gonna do. We’re gonna give da Humies a taste of ‘ot metal death is wot. We’s gonna take Big Gork and Big Mork ‘ere an’ we’s gonna stomp Hummie!

quotes.speaker: Warlord Skarmork The Great Despoiler
quotes.text: They got ded big shooty guns dat'll kill tons of boyz, but if you can get near em den you've got a chance. Just gotta make sure you bring loads of boyz, coz you ain't gonna have a whole lot left when you get close enough ta crump em.

quotes.speaker: Wortgrod Mugskab - Ork Boy
quotes.text: DOK! Me arm got shot off! Gimme a new’un!

quotes.speaker: Xanthius, High Lord of Terra
quotes.text: The Orks plague the galaxy from the end to end with their ceaseless warring and strife. They are a race rooted so deeply in war that peace is utterly incomprehensible to them. They cannot be bargained with or bought save with weapons that they will inevitable turn against those who tried to bribe them. I pray with all my faith that some great catastrophe will annihilate them but I fear that ultimately it is they, not we, who shall rule the galaxy.

quotes.speaker: Zargoz, Snakebites Runtherd
quotes.text: Don't poke at 'der Squiggoths ya' snotling-brained oaf! 'Dey don't like it, an’ it hurts 'der feelins. So don't ta holler at me if dey eats yer fingers.
Transcript from alien probe discovered in the Koath System 696.M41

quotes.speaker: Air Caste Philosophy
quotes.text: We do not belong on the ground. Our home is the sky. As the enemies of the Tau Empire know to their cost.

quotes.speaker: Aun'Cho, Tau Ethereal
quotes.text: Beneath the protective umbrella of the shield relay, we are all but untouchable. Once more, our science and enlightenment makes brings us closer to our ultimate aims.

quotes.speaker: Aun'el Shi'Ores
quotes.text: Soldiers of the Fire Caste! Kroot and Vespid allies! Today, we stand as a bulwark against the selfish, the soulless, and the mad! Today, we must drive back our enemies from our city and from our world! Today, more than ever before, we fight for the Greater Good! And we will triumph!
quotes.source: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War - Dark Crusade, Tau stronghold introduction Classics

quotes.speaker: Aun'el T'au Shoh Ko'vash
quotes.text: The Nem'yar Atoll is ours for the taking. It is by a strange twist of science that we now dwell here, and it is a testament to our adaptability and surety of purpose that we thrive in doing so. Some say that this has been among our race's greatest tests - if they are right, I do not hesitate to say that the T'au'va has pass even beyond our highest expectations.
quotes.source: Psychic Awakening: The Greater Good, p. 40

quotes.speaker: Aun'el T'au Tam'ya, Ethereal caste
quotes.text: It is as we join with others, in a way that only the Tau can, in shared engagement to the Greater Good, that we find ourselves able to fully realize our true potential. And that is the final source of our hopes and intentions.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 27 Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 15

quotes.speaker: Aun'ko'vash
quotes.text: Even when broken, a sword may still cut.

quotes.speaker: Aun'O Tsualal
quotes.text: The Imperium wields might without mercy and influence without temperance. Only by following the way of the Greater Good will they ever learn the error of their ways and know order, wisdom and unity.

quotes.speaker: Aun'shi of the Tau Empire
quotes.text: How ironic it is that as fast as we spread progress and hope throughout the galaxy, the Tyranids spread death and despair. Only united can we hope to stand against them.

quotes.speaker: Aun'shi of the Tau Empire
quotes.text: I have taken great pains not to laugh at the actions of aliens, nor to weep at them or to hate them, but to understand them.

quotes.speaker: Aun'ui Yor'i
quotes.text: The Empire grows weak. The integration of humanity into he T'au'va will always be a lie, so long as they speak our words without accepting them in their hearts. Look at what consensus and irresolute appeasement have made of us. We let he gue’la of Cao Quo do as they pleased, and it exposed us to attack. We were lax and trusting. We should have been vigilant and cruel. If I spared you for tour audacity, Fireblade, the same would be true of me. So let today be a lesson in utility. When we outlive our usefulness, we are excised. Commander.

quotes.speaker: Aun'Va, Master of the Undying Spirit
quotes.text: It burns so briefly, the light of my children, so briefly. But, by the Greater Good, it burns so bright!

quotes.speaker: Aun'Va, Master of the Undying Spirit
quotes.text: It saddens me greatly that we must take arms against the peoples of the galaxy. By their deaths, they deny themselves the liberation that is only to be found in total surrender to the Greater Good.

quotes.speaker: Aun'Va, Master of the Undying Spirit
quotes.text: Let none doubt that the Tau Empire will bring unity to all – let none doubt that now is our time. Forward, for the Greater Good!-addressing the Fire caste cadres from a top the ruins of the Agrellan Hive
quotes.source: Codex: Tau Empire (7th Edition), In Unity, Strength

quotes.speaker: Aun'Va, Master of the Undying Spirit
quotes.text: Our Conquest is inevitable, our ascension a matter of time. Let none who are wise deny our destiny.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau Empire (7th Edition), The Tau Empire

quotes.speaker: Bitter Tau remark
quotes.text: Living armour happens.

quotes.speaker: Captain Torvus Grymm,14th Synford Armoured Brigade,concerning Great Knarlocs
quotes.text: It's easy to dismiss such beasts as just that, little more than pests, when you're sitting in the turret of a battle tank. At least until you've seen one rip the turret off a Leman Russ and eat its crew.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, p. 104

quotes.speaker: Chal'ka Nok, Kroot Mercenary
quotes.text: You humans do not understand. You feed upon weak things, because you think you are strong enough, because you feed only to sustain yourselves. We feed upon the strong, because we know that no strength is truly sufficient, because we desire to make our children stronger.
quotes.source: Rogue Trader: Edge of the Abyss, p. 66 - "The Kroot"

quotes.speaker: Commander Farsight
quotes.text: Each must find their own way. If those in our heartland had witnessed the savageries of the void as have we they would know this. The hand of each of the starfarers is turned against the other; none will join their strength together just to see their ancient enemies prosper. Neither should we.

quotes.speaker: Commander Puretide
quotes.text: The question of the timing of the commencement of hostilities is one upon which you must meditate most deeply. Once your decision is arrived at, it must be pursued with the utmost energy.

quotes.speaker: Commander Puretide
quotes.text: The strength of your force may be calculated by multiplying its weight by its velocity. Strive always to maximize both and victory shall be yours.

quotes.speaker: Commander Puretide
quotes.text: There are certain things in war of which the Fire Caste Commander alone comprehends the importance. It is not his right, but rather his responsibility to send thousands to their deaths if millions will prevail. That is the heaviest burden of command, and it must be shouldered, alone.

quotes.speaker: Commander Puretide
quotes.text: Those with superior reach can dictate the terms of battle and impose their will upon their foe. Remember, the first step on the path to victory is often the most important.

quotes.speaker: Commander Shadowsun
quotes.text: The feeling of power a warrior gains from the mastery of his battlesuit can be intoxicating. Remember your duty.

quotes.speaker: Commander Shadowsun
quotes.text: To follow any path other than the Tau’va is to doom us all. Only together and with courage and discipline shall we stand victorious. Fight with fire and courage and nothing can stand against us.

quotes.speaker: Edens Haltorenium, Former Planetary Governor of Braanos V
quotes.text: Give me Tyranids, Orks or heretics; simple hateful foes that will face me with a scream on their lips and bloodlust in their eyes. These blue-skinned devils are just far too reasonable for their own good.

quotes.speaker: Eldrad Ulthran, Farseer of Ulthwé Craftworld
quotes.text: I have followed the myriad potential futures of the Tau with great interest. Though barely even striplings compared to us, I feel a strange protectiveness towards them. In time I believe they will exceed even our greatest feats and master the darkness within their souls.

quotes.speaker: Fire Caste Maxim
quotes.text: A good warrior strikes fast, but retreats faster.

quotes.speaker: Gorok, Kroot Shaper
quotes.text: Go carefully, commissar. And feed on your enemies. May we have no cause for conflict.
quotes.source: For the Emperor (Novel) by Sandy Mitchell, Chapter Three

quotes.speaker: Guardsman, Cauley, 25th Graian Rifles
quotes.text: Our tanks were useless. As soon as we broke cover, their battlesuits' heavy guns were locked on to us. I swear it was as though they had someone nearby aiming for them before they shot. And when they did shoot...Emperor's mercy! Their guns punched through our armour like it was paper. all I could see were trails of fire where the projectiles had ignited the air.

quotes.speaker: Horgok, Kroot Shaper
quotes.text: You have paid us for our service, Imperial, but we are who we are. If you would deny us our meal, then we would be forced to leave your service. We would, of course, return your payment—but then we would kill you, and we would have our meal anyways.
quotes.source: Dark Heresy Second Edition: Enemies Beyond, pg. 130

quotes.speaker: Kor'O Tau'n Viel
quotes.text: It appears to me that, lacking the sense of unity that might inform them of their insignificance, these Gue’la have come to think that they might own the stars themselves, even the spaces in between them. Only by our presence, I think, might we now convince them otherwise.

quotes.speaker: Kor'O Y'Eldi
quotes.text: Control of space is the control of power. Control of power is the essence of the Greater Good.

quotes.speaker: Lord- Consul Lastant at the Treaty of Gharak
quotes.text: Their time shall come, fear not. Let them believe themselves our betters, let them think they can turn our worlds to their sick philosophy. They think in decades; we think in centuries. When the time is come, we’ll crush this so-called ‘Empire’ completely, and grind it to dust beneath our heels.

quotes.speaker: Major Kane, Mordant 607th Heavy Armoured Regiment
quotes.text: One of their light walkers carried a weapon of lethal effect. It fired a form of ultra-high velocity projectile. I saw one of our tanks after having been hit by it. There was a small hole punched in either flank - one the projectile's entry point, the other its exit. The tiny munition had passed through the vehicle with such speed that everything within the hull not welded down had been sucked out through the exit hole. Including the crew. We never identified their bodies, for all that remained of them was a red stain upon the ground, extending some twenty metres from the wreck.

quotes.speaker: O’Sharrek, Commander Darkwind
quotes.text: See the Gue’la, how they arrogantly stride into our trap. We will punish their belligerence, their ignorant lack of foresight.

quotes.speaker: O'Shovah
quotes.text: Learn to shorten your reach! If your foe can come close enough to negate your striking power, all stratagem is lost and when all stratagem is lost, the battle is lost.

quotes.speaker: Por'el Tau'n Ukos, Water caste negotiator
quotes.text: I have received your messages, acquainting me that these worlds belong to your Emperor, your master. In return I am to inform you that the said worlds belong to his Ethereal Majesty, Aun'O Bork'an Vral, my master, by right of settlement. Should you wish to gain similar rights you must submit yourselves to his wisdom as members of the Tau empire.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 25 Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 39

quotes.speaker: Por'el Tau'n Ukos, Water caste negotiator
quotes.text: This is a message to the peoples of the Imperium, and to the honourable Emperor of that realm. We offer you peace should you join us, and war should you prefer it. We await your response, for we recognise in your race beings of honour and courage.

quotes.speaker: Por'O Dal'yth V'Rok greets the Thurm Brotherhood during first contact with the Demiurg
quotes.text: You misunderstand, this is Tau space, despite what the Gue’la may claim, and you are welcomed to it as friend. We too have suffered difficulty with the Or’es’la, since it seems they wish no unity. We would be honoured to have you fight alongside us against this common enemy. You will find it to the benefit of both our peoples. You will find it, I have no doubt, to be for the greater good.

quotes.speaker: Por'O Ssalo'yen, senior Water Caste Envoy
quotes.text: The Imperium seeks first to destroy what they cannot accept. We ust force them to accept that they cannot destroy us, and then the Greater Good shall prevail.

quotes.speaker: Por'O Volescur, Tau Water Caste
quotes.text: Ours is the destiny of all races to find peace and harmony amongst the stars against the reckless aggression of ancient and fearful empires. We are the new life of the galaxy, a tiny sapling from which a mighty tree will grow, and we will not be denied our right to thrive!

quotes.speaker: Por'O Volescur, Tau Water Caste
quotes.text: With each voyage we undertake, each species we contact, each world we settle, we see the absolute necessity of our mission.

quotes.speaker: Por'ui Dal'yth Ukos, water caste envoy
quotes.text: There are two kinds of conduct the Ethereals will not countenance. The first is a failure to reflect upon what is for the Greater Good of the Tau race. The second is the deliberate refusal to follow the path of the Greater Good when it is clear what it must be.

quotes.speaker: Por'vre Tau Cho, Water caste envoy
quotes.text: They are fierce indeed these Kroot, and savage. I look upon them and tremble at their ferocity. I can only hope that when the enemy sees them they tremble as I do.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 29; Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 17

quotes.speaker: Por'vre Tau'n Ch'koras
quotes.text: There is no victory without unity, no peace when there's conflict. In this dark time, we can only forget our differences and fight the darkness together.

quotes.speaker: Private Wouldrin Adevitr,59th Brontian Longknives,concerning Kroot
quotes.text: They say they'll eat anything. Me, I haven't asked 'em. Don't want to find out too late that they've got a taste for Guardsmen who ask questions.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, p. 98

quotes.speaker: Rogue Trader Guarnerius
quotes.text: It is well that they are known as the Water caste. One might as well try to nail the sea to the wall as pin them down with a straight answer. They flow around your words until they wear you down, like the trickle of water that over time will split the rock.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 25 Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 11

quotes.speaker: Shaper Lok Ch'kreen of the Oathbreakers Kindred
quotes.text: The galaxy is large, and full of many strong creatures. We admire their strength. We honour that strength as we consume their meat. You humans are not so strong, but you are tenacious, and your meat is more palatable than Orkflesh.
quotes.source: Only War: Enemies of the Imperium, p. 95

quotes.speaker: Shas'el Sa'cea Or'es, Fire caste commander
quotes.text: It is not our technology that will enable us to prevail in this galaxy. It is our shared sense of honour and commonality of cause that unites us and will give us the power to defeat our enemies.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 32 Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 8

quotes.speaker: Shas'el Vorcah of Kel’shan about Tyranids
quotes.text: Never have I faced a tide of foes that can alter both strategy and flesh to hasten our downfall. Swift as these beasts adapt, we must be swifter, for if we fail, the Tau Empire will drown in its own blood.
quotes.source: FFG Teaser of The Great Devourer - Conquest Expansion(last accessed 29 June 2015)

quotes.speaker: Shas'o Arra'kon
quotes.text: Are you aware my battle-brothers mock the idea of your Empire clawing its way to greatness? As if all we need do is muster a fraction of our strength and crush you. As if that were so simple a task. The Imperium's blessed war machine is a diseased giant, not easily stirred. Your Empire is a dynamo of conquest. Unchallenged, you will set your ambitions on the realm of Ultramar, or even the holy sanctum of Segmentum Solar. Your significance is not in the threat you pose today. It is in the threat you pose in ten thousand years.

quotes.speaker: Shas'o Arra'kon
quotes.text: Some say that perfection is the enemy of the good. They speak nonsense. Such apathetic thinking is what turns that which is good into bad. We should never cease our striving for greatness, never halt our purpose of bettering ourselves. Anything else is nothing but stagnation, and I have seen enough of the galaxy to know that to stagnate is to wither and die in shameful ignominy.
quotes.source: Psychic Awakening: The Greater Good, p. 66

quotes.speaker: Shas'O M'yen
quotes.text: We make war because we must; they make war because they know no other existence.

quotes.speaker: Shas'O R'th
quotes.text: GW - Medusa V Campaign(saved archive page, dated March 2007, last accessed 7 August 2019)

quotes.speaker: Shas'O Sa'cea Dre'koran Ta'ar
quotes.text: I do not fear death. Death fears ME!

quotes.speaker: Shas'o Vior'la Kais, Fire Warrior commander
quotes.text: A mightier shoal of predators I have never seen; all grace and measured fury.

quotes.speaker: Shas'o Vior'la Kais, Fire Warrior commander
quotes.text: A thousand fibres connect each of us with our fellow Tau and along those fibres our deeds run as causes which come back to us as effects. Everything we must do must be in furtherance of the Greater Good lest we return to the Mont’au, the Terror.
quotes.source: Codex: Tau (3rd Edition), p. 26 Codex: Tau Empire (4th Edition), p. 5

quotes.speaker: Shas'O Vorsheem
quotes.text: Our earth-caste brothers have provided us with the means to destroy our foe, utilize the sensor array and rain death on the greenskin invaders.-Flavour Text

quotes.speaker: Sio't meditation 12, lesson 4
quotes.text: A well-maintained warrior, is an effective warrior.

quotes.speaker: The Air Caste Petition ahead of the Tau’n Campaign
quotes.text: Ours is to be an empire of worlds, not merely of castes or nations, or races or peoples. To simply control the worlds which we claim as our own will not be enough – we must control the paths between them also, or be divided, and so fail.

quotes.speaker: Thorm 4-Gener Bakerus, Xeno-Biologis
quotes.text: Contact with the latest xenos threat to the Imperium was made here. A star cluster in the distant spiral arm. The race is young, vigourus, and technilogically advanced. They call themselves the T'au.

quotes.speaker: Trokah the Scaleskin, Kroot Mercenary
quotes.text: You have nothing to fear from me, Sergeant. I only eat the strong.
quotes.source: Wrath & Glory: Forsaken System Player's Guide, pg. 119

quotes.speaker: Unknown Academy Instructor giving a lesson on hand to hand combat
quotes.text: The first rule of unarmed combat is: don’t be unarmed.

quotes.speaker: Unknown meditation shortly interrupted by a comm call
quotes.text: A single blade of grass will bend and falter in the lightest wind. But where grass grows in pasture, in field or savannah; each blade feels but a fraction of the wind’s full force. It prospers due to the common purpose of its fe—

quotes.speaker: Warlord Skamork, the Great Despoiler
quotes.text: They got ded big shooty guns dat'll kill tons of boyz, but if yer can get near em den you've got a chance. Just gotta make sure you bring loads of boyz, coz you ain't gonna have a whole lot left when you get close enough ta crump em.
